13	   "git.author_name": "Arijit Roy",
14	   "git.date": "2026-07-13T05:58:56Z",
15	   "git.message": "fix(security): stop logging DataQ PATs in plaintext (#849)

Found live in prod App Insights:

    DecodeError: Malformed token received. dq_live_NNZ5… Error: Not enough segments

`fastapi_azure_auth` logs the RAW token when a JWT decode fails (auth.py:171).
A DataQ PAT is not a JWT, so it fails that decode by construction — and
`Security(azure_scheme)` is a FastAPI dependency, so it resolves BEFORE
get_current_user's body. The "PAT is tried first by prefix" ordering documented
in core/auth.py was therefore never actually first: every PAT-authenticated
request handed the token to a JWT validator, which logged the plaintext
credential at WARNING (plus an exception record), and only then fell through to
the PAT path — which succeeded, so nothing looked wrong.

A PAT is a live bearer credential. Anyone with read access to App Insights could
lift one and authenticate as its owner — including the workspace-admin PAT.

Two layers, both needed:

1. Remove the cause: _PatAwareAzureScheme short-circuits to None when the bearer
   carries the PAT prefix, so a PAT never reaches a JWT validator.

2. Logger-level backstop: core.logging now scrubs bare bearer credentials
   (dq_live_…, JWTs, `Bearer <token>`) from log strings. The existing scrubber
   only caught `key=value` query params and URL userinfo, so a BARE token in a
   message body sailed straight through.

Layer 2 is the architectural point: the call site that leaked was NOT OURS.
CLAUDE.md §10 already says redact at the logger, not the call site — this is
exactly why. We cannot audit what every dependency chooses to log, and grepping
our own code for "places we log tokens" would never have found this, because we
don't log it: a dependency does.

The PAT-short-circuit test intercepts the LIBRARY's own __call__, not our return
value — the buggy version also returned None (after logging the credential), so a
test that only checked the return value would have passed on the bug.

Both prod PATs must be rotated AFTER this deploys (re-minting before then would
just leak the new one).

Fixes #849

27-| **Open issues** | **30** in the **`v1.1 Backlog`** milestone (72 open repo-wide) — `gh issue list --milestone 'v1.1 Backlog' --state open`; the population is stated here because the figure has silently drifted before, so **re-count it, don't decrement it**. (**2026-07-17 (latest) — ADR [0036](adr/0036-connection-anchored-check-engines.md) merged ([#894](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/894)):** connection-anchored check engines — every datasource connection offers GX, and a connection additionally unlocks its native engine(s) per its capability set (Snowflake → **DMF**, Unity Catalog → **DQX**, a future BigQuery → **Dataplex**); engine selection is **per check** (`check.engine`, default `gx`, validated against the connection's capabilities; `kind` stays orthogonal to `engine`), superseding the earlier suite-level `engine` toggle. Design-only (index + CLAUDE.md §5 link); DMF is the first native build, DQX/Dataplex trigger-gated. **#858 warehouse-native lineage BUILT end-to-end, all four slices merged; [#858](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/858) auto-closed:** the spike (below) hardened into a shipped feature behind a new **`WarehouseLineageProvider`** seam distinct from the catalog `LineageProvider` — the warehouse returns identifiers in the engine's own case, byte-identical to `asset_identity`, so there is **no fold/enumerate** step and the #823 identity-drift class cannot arise here. **[#886](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/886)** Snowflake provider (`is_incremental=False` → snapshot-diff + **prune**, since `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` is a current-state view) with the spike's source ladder GET_LINEAGE → ACCESS_HISTORY → OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES: GET_LINEAGE gated by a `0A000` **preflight** (clean-error descent, not catch; per-seed traversal deferred → **[#892](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/892)**), ACCESS_HISTORY **empty-corroborated** against QUERY_HISTORY (silently empty on Standard — a catch would mislabel it), OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES the live-verified floor; review fix — `skipped_tiers` carries the real exception message, not a hardcoded "unsupported on this edition" that was wrong for Enterprise. **[#887](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/887)** Unity Catalog provider (`is_incremental=True` → incremental `event_time` watermark, **never prune**, since `system.access.table_lineage` is an append-only log) with a **6h safety re-scan window** so ingestion-lag rows landing after the watermark aren't lost (idempotent upsert makes the re-read harmless); the watermark never regresses below `since`. **[#890](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/890)** the daily beat task (`refresh_warehouse_lineage`, **dark by default** — `warehouse_lineage_enabled=False`) + a backward-compatible migration (`960d18679639`, 5 nullable additive columns) persisting `lineage_watermark`/`last_refresh_at`/`last_tier`/`degraded_reason`/`last_error` per connection; the recorded reason is **classified, never raw** (same discipline as #828's poll-health). **[#891](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/891)** surfaces tier/degraded/failing on the asset graph — an **INFO**-level advisory (distinct from the failing-source **WARNING**) reading the stored `degraded_reason` / `last_error`, gated behind the same asset-stake rule as `failing_lineage_sources`. Providers are tested against **real captured payloads** (byte-for-byte identity pins per #823, query-column projections committed / full captures gitignored). Two process notes banked to memory: a `mypy (tests)` gate miss (CI runs mypy over `backend/tests` too — #418 — a `str | None` used with `in` without an `is not None` narrow cost one round-trip on #891); and the live-warehouse reads stayed spike-parked (Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25), so **[#893](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/893)** — verify e2e against a deployed warehouse — is filed. **Dependabot batch cleared + the #858 spike ran (user-approved):** all 12 version bumps (#860–#871) merged — the stale branches predated the #878 audit fix, and #861 (mypy 2.1→2.3) needed a manual companion commit aligning the pre-commit hook rev that Dependabot can't touch (the `typecheck-deps-sync` gate would have failed it); mypy 2.3.0 verified clean repo-wide first. CodeQL alert 49 fixed ([#884](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/884) — the deadline-thread helper now records-and-RE-RAISES BaseException, keeping the no-masquerade property compliantly). **#858 spike findings** (issue comments have the full detail): real payloads captured from BOTH warehouses (redacted, parked untracked in `backend/tests/fixtures/lineage_native/` for the build PR) — UC `system.access.table_lineage`/`column_lineage` **work on Free Edition** with `event_time` as the watermark and reads-as-NULL-target rows to filter; Snowflake `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` captured with UPPER identity byte-identical to `asset_identity`; **`ACCESS_HISTORY` is silently EMPTY on Standard** (45k QUERY_HISTORY rows, 0 ACCESS_HISTORY — the preflight must corroborate, not catch); **`GET_LINEAGE` errors cleanly (0A000) on Standard** → the build's source ladder is GET_LINEAGE → ACCESS_HISTORY → OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES, preferring the clean-error preflight. Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25; captures are banked so the build no longer depends on the window.) (**2026-07-17 — `schema_drift` SHIPPED end-to-end ([#881](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/881) engine + [#883](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/883) UI):** the first stateful monitor kind — one registry entry (#880's seam held: zero chain edits), a session-aware baseline-diff executor (the #794 comparison pattern; runners never see stateful kinds), per-datasource introspection (SQL via one bound-param `information_schema` query, flat-file via Parquet footer / bounded CSV sample, **Iceberg from table metadata — closing #859's drift leg**), `metric_value` = drifted-column count under ADR-0016 bands, dry-run preview that never writes, and the edit-gated re-baseline action (drop-the-row, recapture-next-run — never introspection on the API thread). The review caught two shippers: concurrent first runs both inserting the baseline — the loser's IntegrityError failed the WHOLE run and discarded sibling results (now `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`), and `UPPER()` information_schema matching MERGING two case-variant tables into a union schema no real table has (now exact-spelling-wins + classified 'ambiguous' refusal). Honest-limits documented: CSV dtype-inference noise (ignore_columns/re-baseline is the workaround) and whole-blob Parquet reads (range-read filed as [#882](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/882)). Two CI-only failures taught local-gate lessons now in memory: `pnpm lint` ≠ `tsc`, and version-dependent library inference must never anchor a fixture.) (**2026-07-16 — the W4 schema-drift prep chain shipped in one session:** #427 one-engine-per-runner ([#875](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/875) — review added `LazyEngine`, `pool_pre_ping` for the mixed-suite stale-connection regression, and the `owned_runner` lifecycle) · #592 slice 1, the `monitor_baselines` diff-reference store ([#876](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/876) — migration-safety review caught a missing `gen_random_uuid()` id default that only a real INSERT could surface) · #726+#429 monitor-kind strategy registry + runner-advertised capability gate ([#880](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/880)) · the #859 Iceberg metadata fast-paths ([#879](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/879) — review caught that snapshot `total-records` never nets row-level deletes: a merge-on-read table would have false-PASSED a volume floor; both metadata paths now require PROOF of zero deletes). **CI-wide outage fixed same session (#877/[#878](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/878)):** npm retired the legacy audit endpoints 2026-07-15 (410) and no released pnpm speaks the bulk endpoint (verified vs 11.13.1) — the required audit gate now queries the bulk advisory endpoint directly over the same lockfile graph, proven to FAIL on known-vulnerable versions and fail-closed on parse/endpoint drift; #877 stays open for the revert-to-pnpm-audit condition. The #858 warehouse-native lineage spike is consent-blocked on live prod reads.) (**2026-07-14 — #789 prefix-keyed per-IP buckets shipped ([#874](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/874)):** every per-IP bucket (unauth, per-provider webhook, both `ipall` ceilings) now keys on an address prefix — IPv4 /24, IPv6 /64, configurable — closing the NAT/proxy-pool dilution observed in the #725 post-deploy smoke (200 requests over 11 sibling /32s, none near the cap). The agentic review caught a shipping bug: IPv4-mapped IPv6 (`::ffff:a.b.c.d`, the dual-stack socket-peer form) folded to `::/64` — the ENTIRE IPv4 internet in one bucket, so one abuser would have 429'd every IPv4 client; now unwrapped via `ipv4_mapped` and folded as IPv4. Also fixed off review: a false ADR claim about CGNAT being unaffected (pool-based CGNAT now shares one bucket — masks are tunable, /32 disables), and test pins so a partial regression (ceilings reverting to raw /32) can't ship green.) (**2026-07-14 — #785 per-provider webhook rate-limit buckets shipped ([#872](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/872)):** the webhook class is now dual-key — a per provider+IP bucket (adf/airflow/dbt folded into the key, unknown segments share one bare-IP bucket so a scanner can't mint buckets) **plus** a `rl:webhook:ipall:{ip}` aggregate ceiling (`RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_IP_PER_MINUTE`, default 240) the agentic review added — without it, per-provider folding would have quietly multiplied the per-IP webhook budget by 4. The review also pinned the fail-open log's `key_kind` to the documented `{tok, ip}` domain (the post-#852 observability-drift lesson) and replaced a hand-copied provider frozenset with the public `db.models.ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS` — the claimed layering obstacle was false. ADR 0035 + deploy/README synced, incl. the new Redis SCAN pattern `rl:webhook:*:ip:*`.) (**2026-07-13 — prod lineage restored + the #829/#830 pair merged.** The lineage outage was ours: the dbt connection's ADLS SAS **expired 6 Jul**, so every 10-min poll failed `ClientAuthenticationError` and DataQ never read `manifest.json` — the dbt builds stayed green and kept publishing artifacts nobody consumed. Marquez was a red herring; the live path is the #759 dbt-manifest one. Re-minted the SAS (1y), re-ran the build, and the chain fired end to end (poll → `refresh_dbt_lineage` → `lineage_edges` → graph; the trigger-binding also ran the bound suite 4/4 green). **Two systemic defects fall out, still open on [#828](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/828):** a failing poll has **no user-visible signal** and the UI's empty-lineage state is indistinguishable from "this asset has no upstreams"; and `_POLL_LOOKBACK` is 15 min, so once auth was fixed the poll succeeded and still recorded **nothing** — every build produced during the outage had aged out and is stranded in the artifact store, recoverable only by re-running the producer. [#823](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/823) narrowed to the still-unproven Marquez pull path. Shipped alongside: [#829](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/829) — a suite was **unshareable from a phone** (the Add button painted at x=407 on a 390px viewport) — and [#830](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/830) — the assets UI printed the raw OL namespace (an Iceberg DSN) instead of a human label. **Both agentic reviews earned their keep:** on #829 the review caught that the new e2e spec was **worthless** (`toBeInViewport()` defaults to `ratio: 0`, and 71% of the broken button was on screen — it passed on the bug); on #830 it caught a **missed fifth render site** (the dashboard) and that the label still **leaked the DB username** when a catalog URI had no path, which made the commit message's own claim false.) (**user follow-up batch #801–#805 all closed 2026-07-12** — the five UI/ops follow-ups filed off the ADR-0015 session: mobile overlay nav ([#817](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/817)), hierarchical asset browse ([#818](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/818)), asset-health split into connection-vs-data-quality axes ([#819](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/819)), the left-to-right lineage graph ([#820](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/820)), and the Marquez local verification + runbook correction ([#821](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/821)). Four latent bugs surfaced and were fixed en route: a run that evaluated nothing read as a green "Passing"; a `cancelled` run rolled up green on **both** health axes; an asset→asset navigation showed the *previous* asset's data (nothing could reach it before the graph's clickable nodes); and the demo seed had **no lineage at all**, so a fresh install could not show the feature. **#813 closed via [#814](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/814)** — weekly `/security-scan` OWASP spot-check found the MCP `create_check`/`update_check` tools had no Pydantic layer, so `name`/`expectation_type` could exceed the REST 256/128 bounds and hit a raw Postgres `StringDataRightTruncation` instead of a clean 422; pushed `check_service.validate_lengths` (now public) into both, extended to `suite_io_service.import_suite`'s direct `Check(...)` construction too (agentic review caught that same-class gap pre-merge). Same session: 13 open CodeQL code-scanning alerts triaged — 10 dismissed as false-positive/by-design (module-level cache globals, a documented lazy-import cycle, an intentional test canary), 3 genuine (`side-effect-in-assert` ×2, `unnecessary-lambda`) fixed in [#815](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/815). **#799 comparison:columns + tolerance + optimization shipped (this PR)** — FDC's column grain as `comparison:columns` (per-column matched/mismatched/additional-per-side VALUE buckets, dropna parity, same sample shape → redaction/report reuse), numeric `config.tolerance` {absolute, relative} on both grains (the #808-review float32 finding — one-sided NULLs never tolerance-equal), and the shared keys+positions alignment pass (×1.52 on the 1M-row benchmark, bucket parity asserted; integer keys now render "1" not "1.0" in samples); catalog fixture regenerated, per-column breakdown + tolerance fields in the UI. · **ADR [0015](adr/0015-two-connection-comparison-check-model.md) written + merged ([#790](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/790), 2026-07-12)** — the two-connection comparison check model: the suite stays single-connection and supplies the **target under test**; a `comparison` check adds one **source** ref. Closes the design gate reserved since ADR 0014; build set [#791](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/791)–[#795](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/795) filed on W3. **#795 comparison UI + report download shipped (this PR) — the ADR 0015 build is COMPLETE (#791–#795, five PRs in one session 2026-07-12)**: side-by-side check editor (source pane = connection picker + table-or-SQL per type + keys; target pane locked to the suite's connection — the §1 invariant made visible; common options below), RunDetail bucket counts + redacted sample tables + on-demand CSV/XLSX report download (derived server-side from the redacted buckets via new `comparison_report.py` + `openpyxl` (MIT, pinned), never stored), comparison-safe CheckEdit (name+thresholds only), catalog entry + regenerated GX-contract fixture, feature-matrix row; Playwright comparison spec green against the live local stack (which also re-validated #797 — the stale api container 429'd the suite until recreated). **#794 comparison run path shipped ([#809](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/809))** — `services/comparison_run.py` builds a per-run executor bound to the suite's resolved target (source side = the check's ref + `config.source` through `resolve_target`/batch materialization or a query spec; target side = the run's materialized table or `config.target_query`), reads both via the #792 reader, diffs with the #793 engine, and maps buckets → `CheckOutcome` (mismatch-% → `metric_value` under ADR 0016 banding; counts → `observed_value`; capped samples → `sample_failures`). `_run_outcomes` routes comparison checks to the injected executor (None → per-check `error`, callers stay safe); worker builds it only when the suite has comparison checks. Failure semantics #122: reader/engine refusals + unexpected exceptions → operational `error` results (classified messages, never raw text). The sample redactor gains a comparison branch — suffix-stripped policy matching so `pii_columns: [email]` masks `email_src`+`email_tgt`, unknown columns keep default-mask. **#793 comparison engine shipped ([#808](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/808))** — `datasources/comparison.py`, the FDC record-grain diff port (ADR 0014 §2: engine yes, app no): key-joined outer-merge buckets (matched / mismatched / additional-in-source / additional-in-target) with per-column mismatch counts, typed `DuplicateKeyError` (FDC silently drop_duplicates-ed — pairing must never be ambiguous) + `ComparisonInputError`, NA-safe nullable-`string` comparison (null==null matches; a literal "nan" ≠ NULL — fixes the FDC astype(str) quirk), pairwise numeric canonicalization (numpy NULL-int→float64 "10.0" vs Arrow "10" can't fabricate mismatches; dup-key detection on normalized keys, samples echo original values), mismatch-% badness scalar over the logical-row union, capped JSON-clean sample buckets. **#792 DatasetReader seam shipped ([#807](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/807))** — `services/dataset_reader.py`: per-datasource capped DataFrame reads behind profiler-style dict dispatch (SQL via the profiler engine plumbing, flat-file object read, `pyiceberg` scan; orchestration types 422), COUNT/scan-metadata preflight + post-read race guard + typed `DatasetTooLargeError` (fail-fast, never truncation), read-time read-only re-validation of query specs, `COMPARISON_MAX_ROWS` setting (default 100k, per-check `config.max_rows` override) documented in deploy/README. **#791 schema + authoring slice shipped ([#798](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/798))** — `checks.source_connection_id` FK + kind⇔presence CHECK (migration `b7c8d9e0f1a2`, up+down tested), author-time validation (source spec via `resolve_target`, read-only per-side SQL per ADR 0019, join keys, orchestration sources rejected), RESTRICT delete guard (409 `connection_in_use`), export/import `(name, env)` round-trip, version snapshots, and the run-path guard (a comparison check yields a per-check `error` result until the #794 runner — never fails its siblings' run). User follow-up batch filed to `v1.1 Backlog` (2026-07-12): [#799](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/799) comparison:columns + optimization pass · [#800](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/800) Astronomer verification · [#801](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/801) mobile overlay nav · [#802](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/802) hierarchical asset browse · [#803](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/803) asset health split · [#804](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/804) Marquez in harness · [#805](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/805) lineage graph view · [#806](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/806) stale-nosec chore. Same session: **#796 CI-E2E 429 regression fixed via [#797](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/797)** — the #783 limiter throttled the tokenless dev-bypass Playwright suite (whole suite = one per-IP `unauth` bucket); diagnosed from the report artifact ("Too many requests" rendered in the failing pages); `RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=false` in the CI E2E job + compose dev default. · **#725 request rate limiting shipped (PR #783)** — app-level *innermost* HTTP middleware on every public surface (REST + orchestration webhooks + the `/mcp` mount a route dependency can't reach); fixed-window Redis counter (window index in the key → no EXPIRE race), **no new dep** (`redis` INCR over slowapi/limits), **fail-open**; endpoint classes `webhook`(per-IP) · `default`(per-`sha256(token)`) · `unauth`(per-IP), raw token never keyed/logged; `/healthz`+`OPTIONS` exempt; 429 carries `Retry-After` + `detail.retry_after_seconds`; ADR [0035](adr/0035-request-rate-limiting.md); the named hard prereq for the ADR-0032 OTP sign-in (#738). · **#773 nav inversion shipped (this PR)** — G-d phase 4: the dashboard opens with an asset-health lead strip (monitored / need-attention / in-progress, severity-sorted preview, explicit first-200 truncation note), Assets moves to the top of the sidebar, suite/run detail link back to their asset, and admins get an owner-reassignment picker on AssetDetail (mount-gated so the /me-resolution race can't strand it; `/admin/users` never fetched for non-admins — structural + test-pinned). Frontend-only — the owner PATCH backend had already shipped in #772. Same session: **UC dbt lineage live-verified PASS** (real `dbt-databricks` build on the live workspace → manifest v12 → `refresh_dbt_lineage` → correct 2-edge chain; the run-stamped UC asset and the dbt node resolved to the SAME row, provenance preserved; blast radius correct both directions — closes the feature-matrix's "adapter-aware, not live-tested" caveat) and the **AssetDetail CI flake fixed** (#778 via [#780](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/780), 15s budgets). · **#768 check-editor type guidance closed** — the `expect_column_values_to_be_of_type` UX finding from the #767 live schema-drift session: added the expectation to the check-editor catalog with a datasource-tailored `type_` hint (Snowflake: exact SQL dialect type; UC/flat-file/Iceberg: Python value type name — verified per-runner execution engine, not guessed) + a [datasources-checks.md](datasources-checks.md) cheat-sheet; the dry-run preview already surfaced `observed_value` for calibration, so no new plumbing there. **G-d build COMPLETE — #762 LineageProvider/Marquez shipped** (the seam + pull + compose profile; all six G-d issues #596/#757–#762 now closed) · **#761 incidents shipped** (G-d phase 3 — lifecycle+dedup engine, evidence card, publisher payload, grant-derived incident API + AssetDetail panel; #773 nav-inversion filed as phase 4) · **W3 in progress 2026-07-11** — **#767 P1 result-misattribution fixed** ([PR #771](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/771) — GX error-first reorder vs positional zip; found + fixed off the **2026-07-10/11 live-prod-DB test session**: schema-drift 6/6 detected on SF+UC, #759 add/drop-view lineage propagation proven E2E, dbt `artifacts_uri` docs footgun [#769](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/769); filed [#768](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/768) type-calibration UX + [#770](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/770) orphan-asset sweep; leaked UC PAT rotated same-session). **#760 asset view shipped** (G-d phase 2 — `/assets` read API + Assets pages, grant-derived authz, `asset_id` on Suite/RunRead; MCP tool explicitly deferred). 1	title = "DataQ betterleaks config"

3	# The global `filter` MUST be a top-level key, BEFORE [extend] — otherwise TOML
4	# scopes it under [extend] and betterleaks silently ignores it. A finding is
5	# skipped when this expression evaluates true (betterleaks discard-on-true).
6	#
7	# Skip ONLY Entire (entire.io) session-transcript checkpoints. Two path shapes:
8	#   - checkpoint shard commits:  <2hex>/<hex>/<seq>/full.jsonl  (e.g. c1/c6b5fdf0cb/0/full.jsonl)
9	#   - .entire metadata dir:      .entire/.../full.jsonl
10	# These are agent session logs that capture raw tool output (public download-URL
11	# hashes, sha256 digests, git SHAs) which trip high-entropy / 40-hex rules such as
12	# `sourcegraph-access-token` (e.g. the SWA CLI's StaticSitesClient@<40hex> URL).
13	# They live on an ephemeral checkpoint ref and never merge to main.
14	#
15	# Anchored to those exact shapes so a stray src/full.jsonl or evilfull.jsonl is
16	# still fully scanned. Verified (betterleaks v1.4.1, git mode): real secrets in
17	# normal files AND in src/full.jsonl / evilfull.jsonl are still reported; only the
18	# two checkpoint shapes are filtered; the default ruleset stays active (extend).
19	# Also verified the public checkpoint ref holds NO real credentials — only these
20	# hash false positives — so this filter hides false positives, not secrets.
21	filter = 'attributes[?"path"].orValue("").matches("^([0-9a-f]{2}/[0-9a-f]+/[0-9]+|\\.entire/.*)/full\\.jsonl$")'

file.content:
title = "DataQ betterleaks config"

# The global `filter` MUST be a top-level key, BEFORE [extend] — otherwise TOML
# scopes it under [extend] and betterleaks silently ignores it. A finding is
# skipped when this expression evaluates true (betterleaks discard-on-true).
#
# Skip ONLY Entire (entire.io) session-transcript checkpoints. Two path shapes:
#   - checkpoint shard commits:  <2hex>/<hex>/<seq>/full.jsonl  (e.g. c1/c6b5fdf0cb/0/full.jsonl)
#   - .entire metadata dir:      .entire/.../full.jsonl
# These are agent session logs that capture raw tool output (public download-URL
# hashes, sha256 digests, git SHAs) which trip high-entropy / 40-hex rules such as
# `sourcegraph-access-token` (e.g. the SWA CLI's StaticSitesClient@<40hex> URL).
# They live on an ephemeral checkpoint ref and never merge to main.
#
# Anchored to those exact shapes so a stray src/full.jsonl or evilfull.jsonl is
# still fully scanned. Verified (betterleaks v1.4.1, git mode): real secrets in
# normal files AND in src/full.jsonl / evilfull.jsonl are still reported; only the
# two checkpoint shapes are filtered; the default ruleset stays active (extend).
# Also verified the public checkpoint ref holds NO real credentials — only these
# hash false positives — so this filter hides false positives, not secrets.
filter = 'attributes[?"path"].orValue("").matches("^([0-9a-f]{2}/[0-9a-f]+/[0-9]+|\\.entire/.*)/full\\.jsonl$")'

[extend]
useDefault = true

input.command: docker run --rm ghcr.io/betterleaks/betterleaks:v1.4.1 git --help 2>&1 | head -40
input.description: Check betterleaks CLI options for ignore support

content.content:
scan git repositories for secrets

Usage:
  betterleaks git [flags] [repo]

Flags:
      --git-workers int   number of parallel git log workers (0 = single process)
  -h, --help              help for git
      --log-opts string   git log options
      --platform string   the target platform used to generate links (github, gitlab)
      --pre-commit        scan using git diff
      --staged            scan staged commits (good for pre-commit)

Global Flags:
  -b, --baseline-path string          path to baseline with issues that can be ignored
  -c, --config string                 config file path
                                      order of precedence:
                                      1. --config/-c
                                      2. env var BETTERLEAKS_CONFIG or GITLEAKS_CONFIG
                                      3. env var BETTERLEAKS_CONFIG_TOML or GITLEAKS_CONFIG_TOML with the file content
                                      4. (target path)/.betterleaks.toml or .gitleaks.toml
                                      If none of the four options are used, then the default config will be used
      --diagnostics string            enable diagnostics (http OR comma-separated list: cpu,mem,trace). cpu=CPU prof, mem=memory prof, trace=exec tracing, http=serve via net/http/pprof
      --diagnostics-dir string        directory to store diagnostics output files when not using http mode (defaults to current directory)
      --enable-rule strings           only enable specific rules by id
      --exit-code int                 exit code when leaks have been encountered (default 1)
      --experiments string            comma-separated list of experimental features to enable
  -i, --gitleaks-ignore-path string   path to .betterleaksignore or .gitleaksignore file or folder containing one (default ".")
      --ignore-gitleaks-allow         ignore gitleaks:allow and betterleaks:allow comments
      --legacy-print                  use legacy key/value verbose finding format (requires --verbose)
  -l, --log-level string              log level (trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal) (default "info")
      --match-context string          context around match: L (lines), C (columns/characters). e.g. 10L, 100C, -2C,+4C
      --max-archive-depth int         allow scanning into nested archives up to this depth (default "0", no archive traversal is done)
      --max-decode-depth int          allow recursive decoding up to this depth (default 5)
      --max-target-megabytes int      files larger than this will be skipped
      --no-banner                     suppress banner
      --no-color                      turn off color for verbose output
      --redact uint[=100]             redact secrets from logs and stdout. To redact only parts of the secret just apply a percent value from 0..100. For example --redact=20 (default 100%)
      --regex-engine string           regex engine (stdlib, re2) (default "re2")
  -f, --report-format string          output format (json, csv, junit, sarif, template)

---
name: project-azure-winddown
description: "Azure free-trial credit exhausted — subscription DISABLED 2026-07-12 (~2 weeks earlier than the planned ~2026-07-26 wind-down); all dataq-rg resources down"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 0aef84ae-9324-4eaf-a425-b6451fedf029
---

**UPDATE 2026-07-12: the subscription is now DISABLED — the wind-down happened on its
own, early and hard.** User got the "$200 credit used up, account and services disabled"
email same day. Verified via `az rest .../subscriptions/{id}?api-version=2022-12-01`:
`.state == "Disabled"` (`quotaId: FreeTrial_2014-09-01`, `spendingLimit: On` — no PAYG
upgrade, so the spending limit hard-stopped everything instead of billing). Confirmed
dead, not a stale CLI cache: Postgres `dataq-pg-wus3-3erlgd` shows `state: Disabled`;
every Container App in `dataq-rg` (dataq-app-api/worker/frontend/redis + the 3 harness
apps) shows `provisioningState: Failed`; the live frontend URL times out completely (no
response, not even a 401). **Gotcha:** `az account show` / `az account list` still
reported `state: Enabled` — those two commands are not reliable for judging subscription
health; use the `az rest` subscription-resource check above, or just try to reach a
resource directly.

**How to apply:** treat DataQ's Azure deployment as gone until the user decides to either
(a) add a payment method in the portal to convert to PAYG and restore, or (b) accept this
as the local-first cutover (#590/#591) landing ~2 weeks early. Don't propose Azure-
dependent work (live smoke tests, prod deploys, KV secret reads, the harness wake
runbook) without first re-checking subscription state via the `az rest` call — it may
still be Disabled. As of 2026-07-12 the user has only asked for a status check, no
restore/wind-down action taken yet. See [[project-live-connections-and-demo]] and
[[project-dbt-lineage-harness]] for what lived on this subscription.

---

Original 2026-07-04 wind-down plan (superseded above, kept for the cost-actuals context —
the harness stop/mockdata-suspend steps below are accurate history of what was already
off before the hard disablement):

Decision (2026-07-04): **no pay-as-you-go upgrade** — DataQ wraps up its Azure presence by
the free-trial day-30 mark (**~2026-07-26**; W3 milestone due 2026-07-25 is the hard
deadline for #590 teardown + #591 local-first posture). Snowflake subscription lapses
separately ~2026-07-05..10 (v1.1 W1, #588).

**Why:** Cost Management actuals (billing currency **CAD**): burn was ~21.7/day
(harness = 80% — Airflow scheduler 8.99 + 2×2-vCPU workers 6.92/day); CAD 144.80 of the
free credit already consumed by Jul 4 → credit would have died ~Jul 9–10. Postgres
B1ms/32GB is $0 (12-month free SKU). App-only burn ~CAD 4.5/day.

**State since 2026-07-04:** the 4 harness container apps (`dataq-harness-airflow`,
`-airflow-worker`, `-airflow-trigger`, `-redis`) are **Stopped**; both ADF triggers
(`tr_customers_daily`, `tr_orders_landed`) **Stopped**; 5 `mockdata-*` cron jobs
**suspended** (api-version `2024-08-02-preview`). The 4 `dataq-app-*` apps still run.
Flows B/C dead until woken; DataQ's 10-min Airflow poll logs failures (noise only).
**Wake runbook is scripted: `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/scripts/harness_window.sh`**
(`status|start|run [--adf]|stop|window [--adf]`; ~CAD 17/day ≈ 0.70/hour while awake;
`--adf` needs live Snowflake). Also mirrored as a #590 comment.
**Snowflake timeline corrected 2026-07-04:** the trial ends ~2026-07-25 (21 days,
$384 credits left) — same window as Azure, NOT "within days". The #588 retirement was
executed then reversed the same day; the real retirement re-runs at actual lapse with
#590. See [[project-live-connections-and-demo]] for the reversal state (fresh 25-day
PAT; schedules/bindings restore still pending — needs dataq-admin).

**How to apply:** don't treat stopped harness apps / failing Airflow polls as incidents;
before any live E2E, wake per the runbook and stop again after. `az` CLI: was left logged
in as demo user Olivia after go-live; owner login is royarijit04@outlook.com (relevant to
[[project-live-connections-and-demo]] credential hygiene at teardown).

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# harness_window.sh — open/close an ad-hoc DataQ harness test window on Azure.
#
# The harness compute was stopped 2026-07-04 (cost wind-down, DataQ #590): while
# awake it burns ~CAD 17/day (~0.70/hour); stopped it costs ~0. This script wakes
# it, optionally kicks the data flows, and puts it back to sleep.
#
# Usage:
#   harness_window.sh status                  # show app/trigger/job states (read-only)
#   harness_window.sh start                    # wake: redis -> airflow -> worker -> trigger, ADF triggers on
#   harness_window.sh run [--adf] [--dags] [--dbt] [--iceberg]
#                                              # resume+kick the 5 mockdata jobs as MANUAL executions, re-suspend after
#                                              #   --adf:  manual create-run of the Flow-A ADF pipelines*
#                                              #   --iceberg: resume+start the iceberg-writer ACA job (Flow D — appends
#                                              #      a batch to the Iceberg table retail.purchase_orders via pyiceberg),
#                                              #      wait, re-suspend. NO Snowflake dep (like --dags); DataQ reads the
#                                              #      table natively on its own schedule (no trigger-binding).
#                                              #   --dbt:  resume+start the dbt-lineage ACA job (#609 — dbt build
#                                              #      staging views + mart dynamic tables, artifacts to ADLS), wait,
#                                              #      re-suspend. Needs live Snowflake (like --adf). Runs LAST so a
#                                              #      same-window --adf/--dags load is what the marts refresh from.
#                                              #   --dags: manual-trigger the 3 cron DAGs via the Airflow REST API
#                                              #     (POST /api/v1/dags/{id}/dagRuns, basic auth; unpauses first --
#                                              #      a run created on a paused DAG never executes). Needs the
#                                              #      harness awake. flow_a_payments_event is NOT force-triggered:
#                                              #      it requires an Event Grid conf payload and fires naturally
#                                              #      when the mockdata files land while the harness is awake.
#                                              #      flow_a_snowflake_load fails after the Snowflake lapse (#588).
#   harness_window.sh stop                     # sleep: triggers off, jobs suspended, apps stopped (reverse order)
#   harness_window.sh window [--adf] [--dags] [--dbt] [--iceberg]  # start -> run -> stop, one shot
#
# Job semantics: a suspended ACA job REFUSES manual starts, so `run` resumes each
# job, starts the execution, and suspends again at the end — everything executes
# on demand, never waiting for (or leaving armed) the cron schedules. `run` works
# standalone (mockdata jobs have no dependency on the Airflow apps).
#
# *--adf create-runs pl_flow_a_customers + pl_flow_a_orders. Flow A loads
#  SNOWFLAKE — after the Snowflake subscription lapses (v1.1 W1, #588) these runs
#  will fail; the mockdata jobs (file landing) keep working regardless.
#  Airflow DAGs are NOT triggered here — they fire on their own cron while the
#  scheduler is awake (or trigger manually in the Airflow UI).
set -euo pipefail

SUB="37e710fd-b956-4a2e-94f2-b132bb26b3d6"
RG="dataq-rg"
ADF="dataq-harness-adf"
ARM="https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB/resourceGroups/$RG/providers"
APP_API="2024-03-01"
JOB_API="2024-08-02-preview"
AIRFLOW_URL="https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io"
TF_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../terraform" && pwd)"   # harness TF holds the Airflow admin creds
DAGS=(flow_a_snowflake_load flow_a_uc_reference flow_b_medallion)     # cron DAGs; payments_event is event-only

# Wake order matters: broker first, scheduler before workers. Stop is the reverse.
# Marquez (the ADR 0034 LineageProvider catalog, #804) rides the same window: the
# dbt job emits OpenLineage into it (`dbt-ol`, only when OPENLINEAGE_URL is set) and
# DataQ pulls the graph back out into its own `lineage_edges` cache. It is started
# FIRST (so the dbt emit has somewhere to land) and stopped LAST — and stopping it
# is safe: the pulled edges are cached in DataQ's DB, so the lineage graph in the UI
# survives the catalog going away, and a dead catalog is treated as *unavailable*

#                                              #      when the mockdata files land while the harness is awake.
#                                              #      flow_a_snowflake_load fails after the Snowflake lapse (#588).
#   harness_window.sh stop                     # sleep: triggers off, jobs suspended, apps stopped (reverse order)
#   harness_window.sh window [--adf] [--dags] [--dbt] [--iceberg]  # start -> run -> stop, one shot
#
# Job semantics: a suspended ACA job REFUSES manual starts, so `run` resumes each
# job, starts the execution, and suspends again at the end — everything executes
# on demand, never waiting for (or leaving armed) the cron schedules. `run` works
# standalone (mockdata jobs have no dependency on the Airflow apps).
#
# *--adf create-runs pl_flow_a_customers + pl_flow_a_orders. Flow A loads
#  SNOWFLAKE — after the Snowflake subscription lapses (v1.1 W1, #588) these runs
#  will fail; the mockdata jobs (file landing) keep working regardless.
#  Airflow DAGs are NOT triggered here — they fire on their own cron while the
#  scheduler is awake (or trigger manually in the Airflow UI).
set -euo pipefail

SUB="37e710fd-b956-4a2e-94f2-b132bb26b3d6"
RG="dataq-rg"
ADF="dataq-harness-adf"
ARM="https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB/resourceGroups/$RG/providers"
APP_API="2024-03-01"
JOB_API="2024-08-02-preview"
AIRFLOW_URL="https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io"
TF_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../terraform" && pwd)"   # harness TF holds the Airflow admin creds
DAGS=(flow_a_snowflake_load flow_a_uc_reference flow_b_medallion)     # cron DAGs; payments_event is event-only

# Wake order matters: broker first, scheduler before workers. Stop is the reverse.
# Marquez (the ADR 0034 LineageProvider catalog, #804) rides the same window: the
# dbt job emits OpenLineage into it (`dbt-ol`, only when OPENLINEAGE_URL is set) and
# DataQ pulls the graph back out into its own `lineage_edges` cache. It is started
# FIRST (so the dbt emit has somewhere to land) and stopped LAST — and stopping it
# is safe: the pulled edges are cached in DataQ's DB, so the lineage graph in the UI
# survives the catalog going away, and a dead catalog is treated as *unavailable*

input.command:
gh pr create --title "chore(security): allowlist the triaged historical jwt fixture in full-history secret scans" --milestone "v1.1 Week 3 — Azure wind-down + local-first posture" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What

Adds `.betterleaksignore` with one fingerprint: the #849 redaction test's original fixture literals in historical commit `08d2bbd3` (the public jwt.io demo token + a fabricated-value PAT stand-in, both since replaced in-tree by constructed strings). The blob can't be purged — force-push to `main` is blocked — so the periodic **full-history** secret scan permanently reported 1 finding.

## Why

A standing known false positive in the full-history scan is worse than noise: it normalises a non-zero result, which is exactly how a real leak hides (the #828/#852 lesson applied to scanning — fix the noise first). CI's incremental scan is unaffected either way.

## Verification

betterleaks v1.4.1, git mode, full 6.75 GB history: 1 finding before → **"no leaks found"** after. The fingerprint pins `commit:path:rule:line`, so any new secret — even in the same file — still reports.

Surfaced by today's periodic security sweep (full-history secret scan + Bandit + pip-audit + pnpm audit, all otherwise clean).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.content: Launching skill: code-review

content.text:
`low effort → 1 diff pass → no verify → ≤4 findings`

## Turn 1 — read

One tool call: read the unified diff (`git diff @{upstream}...HEAD; git diff HEAD`
to cover both committed and uncommitted changes, or `git diff main...HEAD` /
the target passed as an argument). Skip test/fixture
hunks (`test/`, `spec/`, `__tests__/`, `*_test.*`, `*.test.*`,
`fixtures/`, `testdata/`) — test-file changes are not reviewed at this level.
No subagents, no full-file reads.

## Turn 2 — findings

Flag runtime-correctness bugs visible from the hunk alone: inverted/wrong
condition, off-by-one, null/undefined deref where adjacent lines show the value
can be absent, removed guard, falsy-zero check, missing `await`,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in a catch that should propagate.
Also flag — still from the hunk alone — new code that duplicates an existing
helper visible in the diff context, and dead code the diff leaves behind.

Do **not** flag style, naming, perf, missing tests, or anything outside the
hunk.

Output at most **4 findings**, most-severe first, one line each:
`path/to/file.ext:123 — what's wrong and the concrete failure`. If nothing
qualifies, output exactly `(none)`.


## Posting to GitHub (--comment)

The `--comment` flag was passed. After producing the findings list, if the
review target is a GitHub PR, post each finding as an inline PR comment via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` (one call per finding;
include a suggestion block only when it fully fixes the issue). If that tool
is not available in this session, fall back to `gh api` (repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr}/comments)
or print the findings instead. If the target is not a PR, print the findings
to the terminal and note that `--comment` was ignored.

28	  serverless warehouse **b6403b6e3734f0ce** (2X-Small, http_path `/sql/1.0/warehouses/b6403b6e3734f0ce`;
29	  warehouse config LOCKED by Free Edition — auto_stop edit 400s). Catalog `dataq_retail` +
30	  raw/reference/silver/gold recreated (**Free Edition Default Storage: catalogs must be created
31	  via SQL/UI, not TF/API** — created via SQL Statements API then `terraform import`ed; harness
32	  tf apply is clean incl. grants). Three dedicated 90-day PATs (minted 2026-07-02, expire
33	  ~2026-09-30) in KV: `conn-unity-catalog-retail`, `conn-unity-catalog-qa`,
34	  `databricks-token-harness` (TF reads the last into the Airflow ACA secret). Local access:
35	  databricks CLI OAuth profile **`dataq-free`** (`databricks auth token --profile dataq-free`)
36	  — PATs mint via POST /api/2.0/token/create. Free Edition constraints: fair-use quota (compute
37	  pauses for the DAY if exceeded — keep smoke bursts short), non-commercial only, no SLA, 5
38	  concurrent job tasks, account-level APIs unavailable. Migration tracker:
39	  `DataQ-harness/DATABRICKS_MIGRATION_TODO.md` (closed). End-to-end verified 2026-07-02
40	  22:47Z: DAG reseeds green + trigger-bound DataQ suite runs `succeeded` on the new warehouse.
41	- `conn-adls-landing` — ADLS `dataqharness3erlgd`/landing. Secret is an **account SAS** (services=b,
42	  resource-types=sco, perms=rl, **expires 2027-06-28**). NOTE: a *container* SAS 403s on the adapter's
43	  get_container_properties probe — must be an **account** SAS. v1 ADLS adapter only accepts SAS (no MI).
44	- `conn-adf-factory` — ADF, SP = ARM_CLIENT_ID/SECRET from harness secrets.sh.
45	- `conn-airflow` — harness Airflow (basic auth, user `admin`), base_url is the harness ACA ingress
46	  `https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io`. The **Airflow
47	  callback is LIVE** (2026-06-30, #490): the harness DAGs (image `airflow-dataq:callback-1`, both
48	  `dataq-harness-airflow` + `-worker` ACAs) carry the `dataq_airflow_callback` snippet with
49	  `on_success/on_failure_callback`; they POST HMAC-signed events to `…/events/airflow` (200,
50	  near-real-time, trigger-on-success works). `DATAQ_WEBHOOK_SECRET` self-sources from KV
51	  `airflow-webhook-secret` (same value the api verifies with). There are TWO airflow connections
52	  (dev "Apache Airflow" + qa) sharing this one URL → `orchestration_resource_ambiguous` (attributes
53	  to dev) — benign demo artifact. ADF webhook URL is generated in-app (Settings → Webhooks, #493/#496).
54	  **ADF near-real-time is LIVE (2026-07-02, #492 closed):** Action Group `dataq-adf-ag` + metric
55	  alert `dataq-adf-failed-runs` (PipelineFailedRuns > 0, 1-min freq, Common Alert Schema ON, dim
56	  Name) on `dataq-harness-adf` → webhook → `AlertPing` → immediate-poll ingest (#534); deliberate
57	  `pl_dataq_smoke_fail` failure (a Fail activity pipeline, kept in the factory for re-smoking)
58	  was visible in DataQ 4m14s after fire. Alert emails to the AG owner accompany each fire.
59	  **⚠️ BOTH STANDING PATs REVOKED 2026-07-13 (#849 — they were LEAKED).** `fastapi_azure_auth`
60	logs the raw bearer on a failed JWT decode, and a PAT is never a valid JWT — so **every**
61	PAT-authenticated request wrote the plaintext token into App Insights. Admin
62	(`dq_live_NNZ5…`) + member (`dq_live_uTSi…`) are revoked (verified 401) and the KV secrets
63	`dataq-pat-w1-admin` / `dataq-pat-w1-member` now hold DEAD values — do not try to use them.
64	Re-mint only AFTER the #849 fix is deployed (minting earlier just leaks the new one). An
65	admin PAT re-mint needs an interactive `az login` as **dataq-admin** (self-service endpoint:
66	`POST /api/v1/me/api-keys`, needs a bearer for THAT user). Old leaked values remain in
67	App Insights history but are inert now.
68	
69	**Live-smoke auth (CURRENT since 2026-07-05 — DataQ PATs, ADR 0026 phase 1 live):** two
70	  standing PATs minted post-#613-deploy and verified vs prod REST + `/mcp/` — **admin**
71	  `dq_live_NNZ5…` (dataq-admin, expires 2026-08-04) and **member** `dq_live_uTSi…` (Olivia,
72	  expires 2026-10-03); `Authorization: Bearer <pat>` works everywhere a bearer went before
73	  (REST + MCP identically; member sees 4 suites / admin 10 + `/admin`). Plaintexts shown
74	  once, then **stored in KV as `dataq-pat-w1-admin` / `dataq-pat-w1-member`** (2026-07-05,
75	  as royarijit04/deployer; read them back from KV for headless work — demo users have no
76	  KV RBAC, ForbiddenByRbac as dataq-admin). If revoked/expired, re-mint via
77	  `POST /api/v1/me/api-keys` (self-service only; needs a bearer for THAT user). The
78	  **az-CLI pre-auth (#565) remains the PAT-minting bootstrap**:
79	  `az account get-access-token --resource api://21491115-d620-4fe8-9c66-8d567dfae601` for
80	  whoever is az-logged-in (az left signed in as **royarijit04** (deployer) 2026-07-05; dataq-admin sees all
81	  suites, an unshared identity like royarijit04@outlook.com reads an empty workspace —
82	  authz working, not a bug). The #531 captured-session refresh-token trick is DEAD for
83	  renewal — SPA refresh tokens hard-cap at 24h (AADSTS700084); re-capture interactively.
84	
85	Live suite **"Retail Orders — Snowflake"** (owner=Olivia) on the snowflake conn, target
86	RETAIL.ORDER_LINES, 4 checks (order_number not-null, qty>0, unit_price>=0, **plus
87	"line_total within sane band (seeded breach)"** — max_value:0, fails by design ~100%
88	unexpected to keep alerting/severity exercised; a `fail` on this check is EXPECTED, not
89	a regression). Flat-file suite **"Logistics Tracking — flat file"** (recreated 2026-07-03
90	by dataq-admin — the original was deleted reproducing #540): ADLS landing conn, batch
91	target prefix `adls_flatfile/logistics_tracking/` pattern `tracking_events_([a-z_]+)\.csv`
92	strategy latest, 2 checks (tracking_number not-null, status in-set). W8-batch smoke
93	2026-07-03: all three legs (Snowflake/UC/flat-file) ran `succeeded` on images `8dee4f4a`.
94	
95	Demo Entra users (members in royarijit04outlook.onmicrosoft.com): Olivia/Vera/Adam/Ethan/
96	Nora/Wesley each own a suite (7 suites total; Olivia owns 2), cross-shared at owner/admin/
97	edit/view. **dataq-admin** is the only **workspace-admin** via `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` on
98	dataq-app-api (set via `az containerapp update`, reconciled in gitignored
99	deploy/terraform/terraform.tfvars; wesley.khan was removed — now a plain owner). Suites are
100	wired with a mix of cron schedules + ADF/Airflow trigger-bindings; scheduled runs execute
101	(GX→Snowflake) and succeed. NOTE: the suite permission model is now **ADR 0027** (#482, merged
102	#485–#489): workspace-admin is implicit `admin` on every suite AND has **workspace-wide visibility**
103	(Dashboard/Suites/Results, not just `/admin`); grantable suite-admin was dropped (shares are
104	view/edit only). This **supersedes the old #411/#412 framing** above.
105	Passwords live only in the session scratchpad, never in repo/DB.
106	See [[dev-aca-secret-restart-after-pg-recreate]].
107	
108	Live app URL (**ADR 0028 §5 cutover, 2026-07-01 — SWA is GONE**): the frontend is now a
109	**Container App** `dataq-app-frontend` at
110	**https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io** (the old SWA
111	`black-meadow-034fc261e.7.azurestaticapps.net` / `dataq-app-web` is destroyed). The frontend is
112	the **sole public surface** (one generic nginx image, runtime `DATAQ_AUTH_*` OIDC config — MSAL
113	retired for a generic oidc-client-ts). The **api is INTERNAL ingress** (`external_enabled=false`),
114	reached only via the frontend's nginx proxy (`/api` + `/mcp` + `/healthz`). Three ACA gotchas
115	that bit the cutover (all fixed, see ADR 0028 §5 / deploy/README §1.4):
116	1. nginx must proxy **HTTP/1.1** (`proxy_http_version 1.1`) or ACA ingress returns **426**.
117	2. api ingress needs **HTTP + `allow_insecure_connections=true`** (ACA internal service-to-service
118	   pattern; upstream is `http://<app>.internal.<envdomain>`).
119	3. the api had **orphaned ACA EasyAuth** (`azureStaticWebApps` provider, auto-added by the old SWA
120	   backend-link); destroying the SWA left it 401ing everything → **disabled** via
121	   `az containerapp auth update -n dataq-app-api -g dataq-rg --enabled false` (durable; app does its
122	   own `fastapi-azure-auth` token validation). Terraform image rollouts are `ignore_changes`'d
123	   (deploy workflow / `az` owns them); frontend live tag is **:v2**.
124	E2E-verified **2026-07-01** via Playwright MCP signed in as Olivia (generic OIDC vs real Entra SSO,
125	new URL): PKCE authorize → `/dashboard` with live metrics (301 runs, 3 conns, suite perf, recent
126	runs). Earlier (2026-06-29, SWA) verified manual run → live-progress → GX→Snowflake 3/4 + redacted
127	sample drill-down. NOTE: deployed images build on **manual `workflow_dispatch`** only — newly-merged
128	main isn't live until a Deploy run (or an `az containerapp update --image`).
129	
130	To touch the prod `dataq` DB from local: add a temp PG firewall rule for your IP on
131	`dataq-pg-wus3-3erlgd`, run via the conda `dataq` env with PYTHONPATH=repo root + DATABASE_URL
132	from the local tfstate, then remove the rule. KV writes work as the deployer (Key Vault Secrets Officer).
133	
134	**Update 2026-07-06 (full QA/security sweep session):** prod has grown well past the "5
135	connections / 6 suites" snapshot above — as of this date: **11 suites**, **8 members**
136	(added `Arijit Roy royarijit04@outlook.com`, 0 owned/0 shared — likely the deployer's own
137	account showing up as a workspace member), **23 access grants**, 5+ connections including
138	`dbt — Retail Lineage (harness)`. New suites beyond the original roster: **Orders Header**
139	(Olivia), **Payments** (Vera), **Customers** (Ethan), **Products** (Adam), **Refunds** (Nora),
140	**Orders Freshness — Snowflake (harness demo)** (dataq-admin, a freshness monitor-kind check,
141	showing "Critical" 50%), **dbt Lineage — Order Revenue (dynamic table)** (dataq-admin, 4
142	checks) — the last one **verified running successfully end-to-end live**, triggered via the
143	dbt orchestration provider (`triggered_by: dbt:dbt:<run-id>`, 4/4 checks passed) during this
144	session's harness window.
145	
146	**Recurring live issue, not yet root-caused:** Orders Header / Payments / Customers / Products
147	(all backed by the `Snowflake — Orders` or `Snowflake — Payments` connections) fail on
148	**every** schedule/trigger in prod, consistently in ~3s with `checks_total: 0` and no failure
149	reason anywhere in the API response — this is masked by the known visibility gaps **#571**
150	(`checks_total` wrong on pre-dispatch failures) and **#605** (no failure reason surfaced), so
151	the *actual* underlying cause (most likely a credential/connectivity issue specific to those
152	two connections, since the older `Snowflake — Retail (DATAQ_READER)`-backed suites succeed
153	fine) can't be diagnosed until #605 ships. Worth revisiting once #605 lands.
154	
155	**Alerting IS configured + delivering in prod (verified 2026-07-06):** the worker has
156	`REDACTED` + full email (Gmail SMTP, EMAIL_TO/FROM
157	`theurgicduke771@gmail.com`, `REDACTED`) + `PUBLIC_BASE_URL`
158	set — both channel secrets present in KV. Confirmed `slack_alert_sent` + `email_alert_sent` in App
159	Insights. **Teams is implemented (`alerting/card.py`+`teams.py`, wired in the composite) but a quiet
160	no-op — no `TEAMS_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME` set.** KEY GOTCHA (this whole session's confusion): you rarely
161	SEE an alert because **dedup suppresses repeat/unchanged failures** (`alert_deduped`) — the persistent
162	Snowflake-Orders/Payments failures alerted once (maybe pre-config → no-op) and dedup every run since.
163	**To live-test notifications, create a NEW suite** (new suite_id → first run → not deduped) with a
164	guaranteed-fail check (e.g. `expect_table_row_count_to_be_between` min 1e9 on `Snowflake — Retail
165	(DATAQ_READER)` / RETAIL.ORDERS_HEADER, which connects fine, observed ~24960) → alert fires on both
166	channels. #416 (enriched Slack/email: deep link + metadata + expected-vs-observed) + #661 (fully
167	tabular email, owner name, Teams #416 parity) shipped + live-verified this way. To drive the prod API
168	headlessly: `az account get-access-token --resource api://21491115-d620-4fe8-9c66-8d567dfae601` →
169	`Bearer` vs `https://dataq-app-frontend.…/api/v1` (the frontend proxy; api is internal). Deploy after
170	merge is manual: `gh workflow run Deploy --ref main`, then the worker image = the deployed SHA.
171	
172	**Milestone re-triage 2026-07-06 (user-directed):** the 4 new findings from this session's
173	sweep (#651 check-authoring validation, #652 dbt missing from Triggers/Pipeline-runs UI, #653
174	snooze UI, #654 connection-history UI) were filed to `v1.1 Backlog` by the
175	`/gh-issue-from-finding` default, then the user explicitly pulled all 4 into the **current**
176	milestone (`v1.1 Week 2 — Portability`) since the UI ones directly block a user with no
177	workaround. The user then also pulled forward 4 **pre-existing** issues reconfirmed live
178	this session — **#617** (was Week 3), **#618** (was Backlog), **#571** (was Week 6/P3 — this
179	reverses a deliberate v1.1-planning deferral decision recorded elsewhere as "neither gates
180	v1.0.0"), and **#605** (was Backlog) — all now also in Week 2. Takeaway: don't default new
181	runtime-finding issues to Backlog without checking blocking severity first — a P1/blocking
182	UI or correctness gap belongs in the active week, not an unscheduled pile.
183

---
name: project-live-connections-and-demo
description: "DataQ prod (Azure) live datasource+orchestration connections, demo users/access, and credential expiries to watch"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project

---

DataQ prod (deployed Azure app) has 5 live connections, owned by the
`dataq-admin` user. KV = `dataq-app-kv-aw6laj`. Secret names are human-readable
(not the app's default `conn-<uuid>`):

- **Snowflake — retirement (#588) executed then REVERSED same day (2026-07-04):** user
  clarified the trial has ~21 days + $384 credits left (ends ~2026-07-25, same as Azure) —
  keep the leg alive; real retirement re-runs at actual lapse alongside #590. Reversal

  `DATAQ_READER_PAT` (25-day expiry, minted 2026-07-04)** — the old one was revoked;
  connections renamed back; test-connection green. **Fully restored 2026-07-04** (user
  az-logged-in as dataq-admin): 4 schedules re-enabled + 4 bindings recreated via the API.
  The #587 PERF schema was dropped (regenerable; baseline in docs). az CLI is now signed
  in as **dataq-admin** (workspace admin) — use it for admin-scoped API work (e.g. the
  #461 admin PAT). Found live: workspace-admin list-reads on /schedules + /trigger-bindings
  return [] (#488 gap); writes work.
- `conn-unity-catalog-retail` + `conn-unity-catalog-qa` — Databricks/UC on **Free Edition**
  (**MIGRATED 2026-07-02**: the AWS trial `dbc-b16e0901-d85c` exhausted its credits and was
  deleted/cancelled). New workspace **https://dbc-4492dde4-090c.cloud.databricks.com**, the one
  serverless warehouse **b6403b6e3734f0ce** (2X-Small, http_path `/sql/1.0/warehouses/b6403b6e3734f0ce`;
  warehouse config LOCKED by Free Edition — auto_stop edit 400s). Catalog `dataq_retail` +
  raw/reference/silver/gold recreated (**Free Edition Default Storage: catalogs must be created
  via SQL/UI, not TF/API** — created via SQL Statements API then `terraform import`ed; harness
  tf apply is clean incl. grants). Three dedicated 90-day PATs (minted 2026-07-02, expire
  ~2026-09-30) in KV: `conn-unity-catalog-retail`, `conn-unity-catalog-qa`,
  `databricks-token-harness` (TF reads the last into the Airflow ACA secret). Local access:
  databricks CLI OAuth profile **`dataq-free`** (`databricks auth token --profile dataq-free`)
  — PATs mint via POST /api/2.0/token/create. Free Edition constraints: fair-use quota (compute
  pauses for the DAY if exceeded — keep smoke bursts short), non-commercial only, no SLA, 5
  concurrent job tasks, account-level APIs unavailable. Migration tracker:
  `DataQ-harness/DATABRICKS_MIGRATION_TODO.md` (closed). End-to-end verified 2026-07-02
  22:47Z: DAG reseeds green + trigger-bound DataQ suite runs `succeeded` on the new warehouse.
- `conn-adls-landing` — ADLS `dataqharness3erlgd`/landing. Secret is an **account SAS** (services=b,
  resource-types=sco, perms=rl, **expires 2027-06-28**). NOTE: a *container* SAS 403s on the adapter's
  get_container_properties probe — must be an **account** SAS. v1 ADLS adapter only accepts SAS (no MI).
- `conn-adf-factory` — ADF, SP = ARM_CLIENT_ID/SECRET from harness secrets.sh.
- `conn-airflow` — harness Airflow (basic auth, user `admin`), base_url is the harness ACA ingress
  `https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io`. The **Airflow
  callback is LIVE** (2026-06-30, #490): the harness DAGs (image `airflow-dataq:callback-1`, both
  `dataq-harness-airflow` + `-worker` ACAs) carry the `dataq_airflow_callback` snippet with
  `on_success/on_failure_callback`; they POST HMAC-signed events to `…/events/airflow` (200,
  near-real-time, trigger-on-success works). `DATAQ_WEBHOOK_SECRET` self-sources from KV
  `airflow-webhook-secret` (same value the api verifies with). There are TWO airflow connections
  (dev "Apache Airflow" + qa) sharing this one URL → `orchestration_resource_ambiguous` (attributes
  to dev) — benign demo artifact. ADF webhook URL is generated in-app (Settings → Webhooks, #493/#496).
  **ADF near-real-time is LIVE (2026-07-02, #492 closed):** Action Group `dataq-adf-ag` + metric
  alert `dataq-adf-failed-runs` (PipelineFailedRuns > 0, 1-min freq, Common Alert Schema ON, dim
  Name) on `dataq-harness-adf` → webhook → `AlertPing` → immediate-poll ingest (#534); deliberate
  `pl_dataq_smoke_fail` failure (a Fail activity pipeline, kept in the factory for re-smoking)
  was visible in DataQ 4m14s after fire. Alert emails to the AG owner accompany each fire.
  **⚠️ BOTH STANDING PATs REVOKED 2026-07-13 (#849 — they were LEAKED).** `fastapi_azure_auth`
logs the raw bearer on a failed JWT decode, and a PAT is never a valid JWT — so **every**
PAT-authenticated request wrote the plaintext token into App Insights. Admin
(`dq_live_NNZ5…`) + member (`dq_live_uTSi…`) are revoked (verified 401) and the KV secrets
`dataq-pat-w1-admin` / `dataq-pat-w1-member` now hold DEAD values — do not try to use them.
Re-mint only AFTER the #849 fix is deployed (minting earlier just leaks the new one). An
admin PAT re-mint needs an interactive `az login` as **dataq-admin** (self-service endpoint:
`POST /api/v1/me/api-keys`, needs a bearer for THAT user). Old leaked values remain in
App Insights history but are inert now.

**Live-smoke auth (CURRENT since 2026-07-05 — DataQ PATs, ADR 0026 phase 1 live):** two
  standing PATs minted post-#613-deploy and verified vs prod REST + `/mcp/` — **admin**
  `dq_live_NNZ5…` (dataq-admin, expires 2026-08-04) and **member** `dq_live_uTSi…` (Olivia,
  expires 2026-10-03); `Authorization: Bearer <pat>` works everywhere a bearer went before
  (REST + MCP identically; member sees 4 suites / admin 10 + `/admin`). Plaintexts shown
  once, then **stored in KV as `dataq-pat-w1-admin` / `dataq-pat-w1-member`** (2026-07-05,
  as royarijit04/deployer; read them back from KV for headless work — demo users have no
  KV RBAC, ForbiddenByRbac as dataq-admin). If revoked/expired, re-mint via
  `POST /api/v1/me/api-keys` (self-service only; needs a bearer for THAT user). The
  **az-CLI pre-auth (#565) remains the PAT-minting bootstrap**:
  `az account get-access-token --resource api://21491115-d620-4fe8-9c66-8d567dfae601` for
  whoever is az-logged-in (az left signed in as **royarijit04** (deployer) 2026-07-05; dataq-admin sees all
  suites, an unshared identity like royarijit04@outlook.com reads an empty workspace —
  authz working, not a bug). The #531 captured-session refresh-token trick is DEAD for
  renewal — SPA refresh tokens hard-cap at 24h (AADSTS700084); re-capture interactively.

Live suite **"Retail Orders — Snowflake"** (owner=Olivia) on the snowflake conn, target
RETAIL.ORDER_LINES, 4 checks (order_number not-null, qty>0, unit_price>=0, **plus
"line_total within sane band (seeded breach)"** — max_value:0, fails by design ~100%
unexpected to keep alerting/severity exercised; a `fail` on this check is EXPECTED, not
a regression). Flat-file suite **"Logistics Tracking — flat file"** (recreated 2026-07-03
by dataq-admin — the original was deleted reproducing #540): ADLS landing conn, batch
target prefix `adls_flatfile/logistics_tracking/` pattern `tracking_events_([a-z_]+)\.csv`
strategy latest, 2 checks (tracking_number not-null, status in-set). W8-batch smoke
2026-07-03: all three legs (Snowflake/UC/flat-file) ran `succeeded` on images `8dee4f4a`.

Demo Entra users (members in royarijit04outlook.onmicrosoft.com): Olivia/Vera/Adam/Ethan/
Nora/Wesley each own a suite (7 suites total; Olivia owns 2), cross-shared at owner/admin/
edit/view. **dataq-admin** is the only **workspace-admin** via `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` on
dataq-app-api (set via `az containerapp update`, reconciled in gitignored
deploy/terraform/terraform.tfvars; wesley.khan was removed — now a plain owner). Suites are
wired with a mix of cron schedules + ADF/Airflow trigger-bindings; scheduled runs execute
(GX→Snowflake) and succeed. NOTE: the suite permission model is now **ADR 0027** (#482, merged
#485–#489): workspace-admin is implicit `admin` on every suite AND has **workspace-wide visibility**
(Dashboard/Suites/Results, not just `/admin`); grantable suite-admin was dropped (shares are
view/edit only). This **supersedes the old #411/#412 framing** above.
Passwords live only in the session scratchpad, never in repo/DB.
See [[dev-aca-secret-restart-after-pg-recreate]].

Live app URL (**ADR 0028 §5 cutover, 2026-07-01 — SWA is GONE**): the frontend is now a
**Container App** `dataq-app-frontend` at
**https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io** (the old SWA
`black-meadow-034fc261e.7.azurestaticapps.net` / `dataq-app-web` is destroyed). The frontend is
the **sole public surface** (one generic nginx image, runtime `DATAQ_AUTH_*` OIDC config — MSAL
retired for a generic oidc-client-ts). The **api is INTERNAL ingress** (`external_enabled=false`),
reached only via the frontend's nginx proxy (`/api` + `/mcp` + `/healthz`). Three ACA gotchas
that bit the cutover (all fixed, see ADR 0028 §5 / deploy/README §1.4):
1. nginx must proxy **HTTP/1.1** (`proxy_http_version 1.1`) or ACA ingress returns **426**.
2. api ingress needs **HTTP + `allow_insecure_connections=true`** (ACA internal service-to-service
   pattern; upstream is `http://<app>.internal.<envdomain>`).
3. the api had **orphaned ACA EasyAuth** (`azureStaticWebApps` provider, auto-added by the old SWA
   backend-link); destroying the SWA left it 401ing everything → **disabled** via
   `az containerapp auth update -n dataq-app-api -g dataq-rg --enabled false` (durable; app does its
   own `fastapi-azure-auth` token validation). Terraform image rollouts are `ignore_changes`'d
   (deploy workflow / `az` owns them); frontend live tag is **:v2**.
E2E-verified **2026-07-01** via Playwright MCP signed in as Olivia (generic OIDC vs real Entra SSO,
new URL): PKCE authorize → `/dashboard` with live metrics (301 runs, 3 conns, suite perf, recent
runs). Earlier (2026-06-29, SWA) verified manual run → live-progress → GX→Snowflake 3/4 + redacted
sample drill-down. NOTE: deployed images build on **manual `workflow_dispatch`** only — newly-merged
main isn't live until a Deploy run (or an `az containerapp update --image`).

To touch the prod `dataq` DB from local: add a temp PG firewall rule for your IP on
`dataq-pg-wus3-3erlgd`, run via the conda `dataq` env with PYTHONPATH=repo root + DATABASE_URL
from the local tfstate, then remove the rule. KV writes work as the deployer (Key Vault Secrets Officer).

**Update 2026-07-06 (full QA/security sweep session):** prod has grown well past the "5
connections / 6 suites" snapshot above — as of this date: **11 suites**, **8 members**
(added `Arijit Roy royarijit04@outlook.com`, 0 owned/0 shared — likely the deployer's own
account showing up as a workspace member), **23 access grants**, 5+ connections including
`dbt — Retail Lineage (harness)`. New suites beyond the original roster: **Orders Header**
(Olivia), **Payments** (Vera), **Customers** (Ethan), **Products** (Adam), **Refunds** (Nora),
**Orders Freshness — Snowflake (harness demo)** (dataq-admin, a freshness monitor-kind check,
showing "Critical" 50%), **dbt Lineage — Order Revenue (dynamic table)** (dataq-admin, 4
checks) — the last one **verified running successfully end-to-end live**, triggered via the
dbt orchestration provider (`triggered_by: dbt:dbt:<run-id>`, 4/4 checks passed) during this
session's harness window.

**Recurring live issue, not yet root-caused:** Orders Header / Payments / Customers / Products
(all backed by the `Snowflake — Orders` or `Snowflake — Payments` connections) fail on
**every** schedule/trigger in prod, consistently in ~3s with `checks_total: 0` and no failure
reason anywhere in the API response — this is masked by the known visibility gaps **#571**
(`checks_total` wrong on pre-dispatch failures) and **#605** (no failure reason surfaced), so
the *actual* underlying cause (most likely a credential/connectivity issue specific to those
two connections, since the older `Snowflake — Retail (DATAQ_READER)`-backed suites succeed
fine) can't be diagnosed until #605 ships. Worth revisiting once #605 lands.

**Alerting IS configured + delivering in prod (verified 2026-07-06):** the worker has
`REDACTED` + full email (Gmail SMTP, EMAIL_TO/FROM
`theurgicduke771@gmail.com`, `REDACTED`) + `PUBLIC_BASE_URL`
set — both channel secrets present in KV. Confirmed `slack_alert_sent` + `email_alert_sent` in App
Insights. **Teams is implemented (`alerting/card.py`+`teams.py`, wired in the composite) but a quiet
no-op — no `TEAMS_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME` set.** KEY GOTCHA (this whole session's confusion): you rarely
SEE an alert because **dedup suppresses repeat/unchanged failures** (`alert_deduped`) — the persistent
Snowflake-Orders/Payments failures alerted once (maybe pre-config → no-op) and dedup every run since.
**To live-test notifications, create a NEW suite** (new suite_id → first run → not deduped) with a
guaranteed-fail check (e.g. `expect_table_row_count_to_be_between` min 1e9 on `Snowflake — Retail
(DATAQ_READER)` / RETAIL.ORDERS_HEADER, which connects fine, observed ~24960) → alert fires on both
channels. #416 (enriched Slack/email: deep link + metadata + expected-vs-observed) + #661 (fully
tabular email, owner name, Teams #416 parity) shipped + live-verified this way. To drive the prod API
headlessly: `az account get-access-token --resource api://21491115-d620-4fe8-9c66-8d567dfae601` →
`Bearer` vs `https://dataq-app-frontend.…/api/v1` (the frontend proxy; api is internal). Deploy after
merge is manual: `gh workflow run Deploy --ref main`, then the worker image = the deployed SHA.

**Milestone re-triage 2026-07-06 (user-directed):** the 4 new findings from this session's
sweep (#651 check-authoring validation, #652 dbt missing from Triggers/Pipeline-runs UI, #653
snooze UI, #654 connection-history UI) were filed to `v1.1 Backlog` by the
`/gh-issue-from-finding` default, then the user explicitly pulled all 4 into the **current**
milestone (`v1.1 Week 2 — Portability`) since the UI ones directly block a user with no
workaround. The user then also pulled forward 4 **pre-existing** issues reconfirmed live
this session — **#617** (was Week 3), **#618** (was Backlog), **#571** (was Week 6/P3 — this
reverses a deliberate v1.1-planning deferral decision recorded elsewhere as "neither gates
v1.0.0"), and **#605** (was Backlog) — all now also in Week 2. Takeaway: don't default new
runtime-finding issues to Backlog without checking blocking severity first — a P1/blocking
UI or correctness gap belongs in the active week, not an unscheduled pile.

(**2026-07-14 — #789 prefix-keyed per-IP buckets shipped ([#874](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/874)):** every per-IP bucket (unauth, per-provider webhook, both `ipall` ceilings) now keys on an address prefix — IPv4 /24, IPv6 /64, configurable — closing the NAT/proxy-pool dilution observed in the #725 post-deploy smoke (200 requests over 11 sibling /32s, none near the cap). The agentic review caught a shipping bug: IPv4-mapped IPv6 (`::ffff:a.b.c.d`, the dual-stack socket-peer form) folded to `::/64` — the ENTIRE IPv4 internet in one bucket, so one abuser would have 429'd every IPv4 client; now unwrapped via `ipv4_mapped` and folded as IPv4. Also fixed off review: a false ADR claim about CGNAT being unaffected (pool-based CGNAT now shares one bucket — masks are tunable, /32 disables), and test pins so a partial regression (ceilings reverting to raw /32) can't ship green.) (**2026-07-14 — #785 per-provider webhook rate-limit buckets shipped ([#872](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/872)):** the webhook class is now dual-key — a per provider+IP bucket (adf/airflow/dbt folded into the key, unknown segments share one bare-IP bucket so a scanner can't mint buckets) **plus** a `rl:webhook:ipall:{ip}` aggregate ceiling (`RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_IP_PER_MINUTE`, default 240) the agentic review added — without it, per-provider folding would have quietly multiplied the per-IP webhook budget by 4. The review also pinned the fail-open log's `key_kind` to the documented `{tok, ip}` domain (the post-#852 observability-drift lesson) and replaced a hand-copied provider frozenset with the public `db.models.ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS` — the claimed layering obstacle was false. (**2026-07-13 — prod lineage restored + the #829/#830 pair merged.** The lineage outage was ours: the dbt connection's ADLS SAS **expired 6 Jul**, so every 10-min poll failed `ClientAuthenticationError` and DataQ never read `manifest.json` — the dbt builds stayed green and kept publishing artifacts nobody consumed. Marquez was a red herring; the live path is the #759 dbt-manifest one. Re-minted the SAS (1y), re-ran the build, and the chain fired end to end (poll → `refresh_dbt_lineage` → `lineage_edges` → graph; the trigger-binding also ran the bound suite 4/4 green). **Two systemic defects fall out, still open on [#828](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/828):** a failing poll has **no user-visible signal** and the UI's empty-lineage state is indistinguishable from "this asset has no upstreams"; and `_POLL_LOOKBACK` is 15 min, so once auth was fixed the poll succeeded and still recorded **nothing** — every build produced during the outage had aged out and is stranded in the artifact store, recoverable only by re-running the producer. [#823](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/823) narrowed to the still-unproven Marquez pull path. Shipped alongside: [#829](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/829) — a suite was **unshareable from a phone** (the Add button painted at x=407 on a 390px viewport) — and [#830](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/830) — the assets UI printed the raw OL namespace (an Iceberg DSN) instead of a human label. **Both agentic reviews earned their keep:** on #829 the review caught that the new e2e spec was **worthless** (`toBeInViewport()` defaults to `ratio: 0`, and 71% of the broken button was on screen — it passed on the bug); on #830 it caught a **missed fifth render site** (the dashboard) and that the label still **leaked the DB username** when a catalog URI had no path, which made the commit message's own claim false.)
