27	| **Open issues** | **30** in the **`v1.1 Backlog`** milestone (72 open repo-wide) — `gh issue list --milestone 'v1.1 Backlog' --state open`; the population is stated here because the figure has silently drifted before, so **re-count it, don't decrement it**. (**2026-07-17 (latest) — ADR [0036](adr/0036-connection-anchored-check-engines.md) merged ([#894](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/894)):** connection-anchored check engines — every datasource connection offers GX, and a connection additionally unlocks its native engine(s) per its capability set (Snowflake → **DMF**, Unity Catalog → **DQX**, a future BigQuery → **Dataplex**); engine selection is **per check** (`check.engine`, default `gx`, validated against the connection's capabilities; `kind` stays orthogonal to `engine`), superseding the earlier suite-level `engine` toggle. Design-only (index + CLAUDE.md §5 link); DMF is the first native build, DQX/Dataplex trigger-gated. **#858 warehouse-native lineage BUILT end-to-end, all four slices merged; [#858](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/858) auto-closed:** the spike (below) hardened into a shipped feature behind a new **`WarehouseLineageProvider`** seam distinct from the catalog `LineageProvider` — the warehouse returns identifiers in the engine's own case, byte-identical to `asset_identity`, so there is **no fold/enumerate** step and the #823 identity-drift class cannot arise here. **[#886](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/886)** Snowflake provider (`is_incremental=False` → snapshot-diff + **prune**, since `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` is a current-state view) with the spike's source ladder GET_LINEAGE → ACCESS_HISTORY → OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES: GET_LINEAGE gated by a `0A000` **preflight** (clean-error descent, not catch; per-seed traversal deferred → **[#892](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/892)**), ACCESS_HISTORY **empty-corroborated** against QUERY_HISTORY (silently empty on Standard — a catch would mislabel it), OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES the live-verified floor; review fix — `skipped_tiers` carries the real exception message, not a hardcoded "unsupported on this edition" that was wrong for Enterprise. **[#887](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/887)** Unity Catalog provider (`is_incremental=True` → incremental `event_time` watermark, **never prune**, since `system.access.table_lineage` is an append-only log) with a **6h safety re-scan window** so ingestion-lag rows landing after the watermark aren't lost (idempotent upsert makes the re-read harmless); the watermark never regresses below `since`. **[#890](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/890)** the daily beat task (`refresh_warehouse_lineage`, **dark by default** — `warehouse_lineage_enabled=False`) + a backward-compatible migration (`960d18679639`, 5 nullable additive columns) persisting `lineage_watermark`/`last_refresh_at`/`last_tier`/`degraded_reason`/`last_error` per connection; the recorded reason is **classified, never raw** (same discipline as #828's poll-health). **[#891](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/891)** surfaces tier/degraded/failing on the asset graph — an **INFO**-level advisory (distinct from the failing-source **WARNING**) reading the stored `degraded_reason` / `last_error`, gated behind the same asset-stake rule as `failing_lineage_sources`. Providers are tested against **real captured payloads** (byte-for-byte identity pins per #823, query-column projections committed / full captures gitignored). Two process notes banked to memory: a `mypy (tests)` gate miss (CI runs mypy over `backend/tests` too — #418 — a `str | None` used with `in` without an `is not None` narrow cost one round-trip on #891); and the live-warehouse reads stayed spike-parked (Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25), so **[#893](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/893)** — verify e2e against a deployed warehouse — is filed. **Dependabot batch cleared + the #858 spike ran (user-approved):** all 12 version bumps (#860–#871) merged — the stale branches predated the #878 audit fix, and #861 (mypy 2.1→2.3) needed a manual companion commit aligning the pre-commit hook rev that Dependabot can't touch (the `typecheck-deps-sync` gate would have failed it); mypy 2.3.0 verified clean repo-wide first. CodeQL alert 49 fixed ([#884](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/884) — the deadline-thread helper now records-and-RE-RAISES BaseException, keeping the no-masquerade property compliantly). **#858 spike findings** (issue comments have the full detail): real payloads captured from BOTH warehouses (redacted, parked untracked in `backend/tests/fixtures/lineage_native/` for the build PR) — UC `system.access.table_lineage`/`column_lineage` **work on Free Edition** with `event_time` as the watermark and reads-as-NULL-target rows to filter; Snowflake `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` captured with UPPER identity byte-identical to `asset_identity`; **`ACCESS_HISTORY` is silently EMPTY on Standard** (45k QUERY_HISTORY rows, 0 ACCESS_HISTORY — the preflight must corroborate, not catch); **`GET_LINEAGE` errors cleanly (0A000) on Standard** → the build's source ladder is GET_LINEAGE → ACCESS_HISTORY → OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES, preferring the clean-error preflight. Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25; captures are banked so the build no longer depends on the window.) (**2026-07-17 — `schema_drift` SHIPPED end-to-end ([#881](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/881) engine + [#883](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/883) UI):** the first stateful monitor kind — one registry entry (#880's seam held: zero chain edits), a session-aware baseline-diff executor (the #794 comparison pattern; runners never see stateful kinds), per-datasource introspection (SQL via one bound-param `information_schema` query, flat-file via Parquet footer / bounded CSV sample, **Iceberg from table metadata — closing #859's drift leg**), `metric_value` = drifted-column count under ADR-0016 bands, dry-run preview that never writes, and the edit-gated re-baseline action (drop-the-row, recapture-next-run — never introspection on the API thread). The review caught two shippers: concurrent first runs both inserting the baseline — the loser's IntegrityError failed the WHOLE run and discarded sibling results (now `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`), and `UPPER()` information_schema matching MERGING two case-variant tables into a union schema no real table has (now exact-spelling-wins + classified 'ambiguous' refusal). Honest-limits documented: CSV dtype-inference noise (ignore_columns/re-baseline is the workaround) and whole-blob Parquet reads (range-read filed as [#882](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/882)). Two CI-only failures taught local-gate lessons now in memory: `pnpm lint` ≠ `tsc`, and version-dependent library inference must never anchor a fixture.) (**2026-07-16 — the W4 schema-drift prep chain shipped in one session:** #427 one-engine-per-runner ([#875](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/875) — review added `LazyEngine`, `pool_pre_ping` for the mixed-suite stale-connection regression, and the `owned_runner` lifecycle) · #592 slice 1, the `monitor_baselines` diff-reference store ([#876](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/876) — migration-safety review caught a missing `gen_random_uuid()` id default that only a real INSERT could surface) · #726+#429 monitor-kind strategy registry + runner-advertised capability gate ([#880](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/880)) · the #859 Iceberg metadata fast-paths ([#879](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/879) — review caught that snapshot `total-records` never nets row-level deletes: a merge-on-read table would have false-PASSED a volume floor; both metadata paths now require PROOF of zero deletes). **CI-wide outage fixed same session (#877/[#878](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/878)):** npm retired the legacy audit endpoints 2026-07-15 (410) and no released pnpm speaks the bulk endpoint (verified vs 11.13.1) — the required audit gate now queries the bulk advisory endpoint directly over the same lockfile graph, proven to FAIL on known-vulnerable versions and fail-closed on parse/endpoint drift; #877 stays open for the revert-to-pnpm-audit condition. The #858 warehouse-native lineage spike is consent-blocked on live prod reads.) (**2026-07-14 — #789 prefix-keyed per-IP buckets shipped ([#874](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/874)):** every per-IP bucket (unauth, per-provider webhook, both `ipall` ceilings) now keys on an address prefix — IPv4 /24, IPv6 /64, configurable — closing the NAT/proxy-pool dilution observed in the #725 post-deploy smoke (200 requests over 11 sibling /32s, none near the cap). The agentic review caught a shipping bug: IPv4-mapped IPv6 (`::ffff:a.b.c.d`, the dual-stack socket-peer form) folded to `::/64` — the ENTIRE IPv4 internet in one bucket, so one abuser would have 429'd every IPv4 client; now unwrapped via `ipv4_mapped` and folded as IPv4. Also fixed off review: a false ADR claim about CGNAT being unaffected (pool-based CGNAT now shares one bucket — masks are tunable, /32 disables), and test pins so a partial regression (ceilings reverting to raw /32) can't ship green.) (**2026-07-14 — #785 per-provider webhook rate-limit buckets shipped ([#872](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/872)):** the webhook class is now dual-key — a per provider+IP bucket (adf/airflow/dbt folded into the key, unknown segments share one bare-IP bucket so a scanner can't mint buckets) **plus** a `rl:webhook:ipall:{ip}` aggregate ceiling (`RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_IP_PER_MINUTE`, default 240) the agentic review added — without it, per-provider folding would have quietly multiplied the per-IP webhook budget by 4. The review also pinned the fail-open log's `key_kind` to the documented `{tok, ip}` domain (the post-#852 observability-drift lesson) and replaced a hand-copied provider frozenset with the public `db.models.ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS` — the claimed layering obstacle was false. ADR 0035 + deploy/README synced, incl. the new Redis SCAN pattern `rl:webhook:*:ip:*`.) (**2026-07-13 — prod lineage restored + the #829/#830 pair merged.** The lineage outage was ours: the dbt connection's ADLS SAS **expired 6 Jul**, so every 10-min poll failed `ClientAuthenticationError` and DataQ never read `manifest.json` — the dbt builds stayed green and kept publishing artifacts nobody consumed. Marquez was a red herring; the live path is the #759 dbt-manifest one. Re-minted the SAS (1y), re-ran the build, and the chain fired end to end (poll → `refresh_dbt_lineage` → `lineage_edges` → graph; the trigger-binding also ran the bound suite 4/4 green). **Two systemic defects fall out, still open on [#828](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/828):** a failing poll has **no user-visible signal** and the UI's empty-lineage state is indistinguishable from "this asset has no upstreams"; and `_POLL_LOOKBACK` is 15 min, so once auth was fixed the poll succeeded and still recorded **nothing** — every build produced during the outage had aged out and is stranded in the artifact store, recoverable only by re-running the producer. [#823](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/823) narrowed to the still-unproven Marquez pull path. Shipped alongside: [#829](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/829) — a suite was **unshareable from a phone** (the Add button painted at x=407 on a 390px viewport) — and [#830](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/830) — the assets UI printed the raw OL namespace (an Iceberg DSN) instead of a human label. **Both agentic reviews earned their keep:** on #829 the review caught that the new e2e spec was **worthless** (`toBeInViewport()` defaults to `ratio: 0`, and 71% of the broken button was on screen — it passed on the bug); on #830 it caught a **missed fifth render site** (the dashboard) and that the label still **leaked the DB username** when a catalog URI had no path, which made the commit message's own claim false.) (**user follow-up batch #801–#805 all closed 2026-07-12** — the five UI/ops follow-ups filed off the ADR-0015 session: mobile overlay nav ([#817](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/817)), hierarchical asset browse ([#818](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/818)), asset-health split into connection-vs-data-quality axes ([#819](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/819)), the left-to-right lineage graph ([#820](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/820)), and the Marquez local verification + runbook correction ([#821](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/821)). Four latent bugs surfaced and were fixed en route: a run that evaluated nothing read as a green "Passing"; a `cancelled` run rolled up green on **both** health axes; an asset→asset navigation showed the *previous* asset's data (nothing could reach it before the graph's clickable nodes); and the demo seed had **no lineage at all**, so a fresh install could not show the feature. **#813 closed via [#814](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/814)** — weekly `/security-scan` OWASP spot-check found the MCP `create_check`/`update_check` tools had no Pydantic layer, so `name`/`expectation_type` could exceed the REST 256/128 bounds and hit a raw Postgres `StringDataRightTruncation` instead of a clean 422; pushed `check_service.validate_lengths` (now public) into both, extended to `suite_io_service.import_suite`'s direct `Check(...)` construction too (agentic review caught that same-class gap pre-merge). Same session: 13 open CodeQL code-scanning alerts triaged — 10 dismissed as false-positive/by-design (module-level cache globals, a documented lazy-import cycle, an intentional test canary), 3 genuine (`side-effect-in-assert` ×2, `unnecessary-lambda`) fixed in [#815](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/815). **#799 comparison:columns + tolerance + optimization shipped (this PR)** — FDC's column grain as `comparison:columns` (per-column matched/mismatched/additional-per-side VALUE buckets, dropna parity, same sample shape → redaction/report reuse), numeric `config.tolerance` {absolute, relative} on both grains (the #808-review float32 finding — one-sided NULLs never tolerance-equal), and the shared keys+positions alignment pass (×1.52 on the 1M-row benchmark, bucket parity asserted; integer keys now render "1" not "1.0" in samples); catalog fixture regenerated, per-column breakdown + tolerance fields in the UI. · **ADR [0015](adr/0015-two-connection-comparison-check-model.md) written + merged ([#790](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/790), 2026-07-12)** — the two-connection comparison check model: the suite stays single-connection and supplies the **target under test**; a `comparison` check adds one **source** ref. Closes the design gate reserved since ADR 0014; build set [#791](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/791)–[#795](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/795) filed on W3. **#795 comparison UI + report download shipped (this PR) — the ADR 0015 build is COMPLETE (#791–#795, five PRs in one session 2026-07-12)**: side-by-side check editor (source pane = connection picker + table-or-SQL per type + keys; target pane locked to the suite's connection — the §1 invariant made visible; common options below), RunDetail bucket counts + redacted sample tables + on-demand CSV/XLSX report download (derived server-side from the redacted buckets via new `comparison_report.py` + `openpyxl` (MIT, pinned), never stored), comparison-safe CheckEdit (name+thresholds only), catalog entry + regenerated GX-contract fixture, feature-matrix row; Playwright comparison spec green against the live local stack (which also re-validated #797 — the stale api container 429'd the suite until recreated). **#794 comparison run path shipped ([#809](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/809))** — `services/comparison_run.py` builds a per-run executor bound to the suite's resolved target (source side = the check's ref + `config.source` through `resolve_target`/batch materialization or a query spec; target side = the run's materialized table or `config.target_query`), reads both via the #792 reader, diffs with the #793 engine, and maps buckets → `CheckOutcome` (mismatch-% → `metric_value` under ADR 0016 banding; counts → `observed_value`; capped samples → `sample_failures`). `_run_outcomes` routes comparison checks to the injected executor (None → per-check `error`, callers stay safe); worker builds it only when the suite has comparison checks. Failure semantics #122: reader/engine refusals + unexpected exceptions → operational `error` results (classified messages, never raw text). The sample redactor gains a comparison branch — suffix-stripped policy matching so `pii_columns: [email]` masks `email_src`+`email_tgt`, unknown columns keep default-mask. **#793 comparison engine shipped ([#808](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/808))** — `datasources/comparison.py`, the FDC record-grain diff port (ADR 0014 §2: engine yes, app no): key-joined outer-merge buckets (matched / mismatched / additional-in-source / additional-in-target) with per-column mismatch counts, typed `DuplicateKeyError` (FDC silently drop_duplicates-ed — pairing must never be ambiguous) + `ComparisonInputError`, NA-safe nullable-`string` comparison (null==null matches; a literal "nan" ≠ NULL — fixes the FDC astype(str) quirk), pairwise numeric canonicalization (numpy NULL-int→float64 "10.0" vs Arrow "10" can't fabricate mismatches; dup-key detection on normalized keys, samples echo original values), mismatch-% badness scalar over the logical-row union, capped JSON-clean sample buckets. **#792 DatasetReader seam shipped ([#807](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/807))** — `services/dataset_reader.py`: per-datasource capped DataFrame reads behind profiler-style dict dispatch (SQL via the profiler engine plumbing, flat-file object read, `pyiceberg` scan; orchestration types 422), COUNT/scan-metadata preflight + post-read race guard + typed `DatasetTooLargeError` (fail-fast, never truncation), read-time read-only re-validation of query specs, `COMPARISON_MAX_ROWS` setting (default 100k, per-check `config.max_rows` override) documented in deploy/README. **#791 schema + authoring slice shipped ([#798](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/798))** — `checks.source_connection_id` FK + kind⇔presence CHECK (migration `b7c8d9e0f1a2`, up+down tested), author-time validation (source spec via `resolve_target`, read-only per-side SQL per ADR 0019, join keys, orchestration sources rejected), RESTRICT delete guard (409 `connection_in_use`), export/import `(name, env)` round-trip, version snapshots, and the run-path guard (a comparison check yields a per-check `error` result until the #794 runner — never fails its siblings' run). | **Open issues** | **30** in the **`v1.1 Backlog`** milestone (72 open repo-wide) — `gh issue list --milestone 'v1.1 Backlog' --state open`; the population is stated here because the figure has silently drifted before, so **re-count it, don't decrement it**. (**2026-07-17 (latest) — ADR [0036](adr/0036-connection-anchored-check-engines.md) merged ([#894](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/894)):** connection-anchored check engines — every datasource connection offers GX, and a connection additionally unlocks its native engine(s) per its capability set (Snowflake → **DMF**, Unity Catalog → **DQX**, a future BigQuery → **Dataplex**); engine selection is **per check** (`check.engine`, default `gx`, validated against the connection's capabilities; `kind` stays orthogonal to `engine`), superseding the earlier suite-level `engine` toggle. Design-only (index + CLAUDE.md §5 link); DMF is the first native build, DQX/Dataplex trigger-gated. **#858 warehouse-native lineage BUILT end-to-end, all four slices merged; [#858](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/858) auto-closed:** the spike (below) hardened into a shipped feature behind a new **`WarehouseLineageProvider`** seam distinct from the catalog `LineageProvider` — the warehouse returns identifiers in the engine's own case, byte-identical to `asset_identity`, so there is **no fold/enumerate** step and the #823 identity-drift class cannot arise here. **[#886](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/886)** Snowflake provider (`is_incremental=False` → snapshot-diff + **prune**, since `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` is a current-state view) with the spike's source ladder GET_LINEAGE → ACCESS_HISTORY → OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES: GET_LINEAGE gated by a `0A000` **preflight** (clean-error descent, not catch; per-seed traversal deferred → **[#892](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/892)**), ACCESS_HISTORY **empty-corroborated** against QUERY_HISTORY (silently empty on Standard — a catch would mislabel it), OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES the live-verified floor; review fix — `skipped_tiers` carries the real exception message, not a hardcoded "unsupported on this edition" that was wrong for Enterprise. **[#887](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/887)** Unity Catalog provider (`is_incremental=True` → incremental `event_time` watermark, **never prune**, since `system.access.table_lineage` is an append-only log) with a **6h safety re-scan window** so ingestion-lag rows landing after the watermark aren't lost (idempotent upsert makes the re-read harmless); the watermark never regresses below `since`. **[#890](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/890)** the daily beat task (`refresh_warehouse_lineage`, **dark by default** — `warehouse_lineage_enabled=False`) + a backward-compatible migration (`960d18679639`, 5 nullable additive columns) persisting `lineage_watermark`/`last_refresh_at`/`last_tier`/`degraded_reason`/`last_error` per connection; the recorded reason is **classified, never raw** (same discipline as #828's poll-health). **[#891](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/891)** surfaces tier/degraded/failing on the asset graph — an **INFO**-level advisory (distinct from the failing-source **WARNING**) reading the stored `degraded_reason` / `last_error`, gated behind the same asset-stake rule as `failing_lineage_sources`. Providers are tested against **real captured payloads** (byte-for-byte identity pins per #823, query-column projections committed / full captures gitignored). Two process notes banked to memory: a `mypy (tests)` gate miss (CI runs mypy over `backend/tests` too — #418 — a `str | None` used with `in` without an `is not None` narrow cost one round-trip on #891); and the live-warehouse reads stayed spike-parked (Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25), so **[#893](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/893)** — verify e2e against a deployed warehouse — is filed. **Dependabot batch cleared + the #858 spike ran (user-approved):** all 12 version bumps (#860–#871) merged — the stale branches predated the #878 audit fix, and #861 (mypy 2.1→2.3) needed a manual companion commit aligning the pre-commit hook rev that Dependabot can't touch (the `typecheck-deps-sync` gate would have failed it); mypy 2.3.0 verified clean repo-wide first. CodeQL alert 49 fixed ([#884](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/884) — the deadline-thread helper now records-and-RE-RAISES BaseException, keeping the no-masquerade property compliantly). **#858 spike findings** (issue comments have the full detail): real payloads captured from BOTH warehouses (redacted, parked untracked in `backend/tests/fixtures/lineage_native/` for the build PR) — UC `system.access.table_lineage`/`column_lineage` **work on Free Edition** with `event_time` as the watermark and reads-as-NULL-target rows to filter; Snowflake `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` captured with UPPER identity byte-identical to `asset_identity`; **`ACCESS_HISTORY` is silently EMPTY on Standard** (45k QUERY_HISTORY rows, 0 ACCESS_HISTORY — the preflight must corroborate, not catch); **`GET_LINEAGE` errors cleanly (0A000) on Standard** → the build's source ladder is GET_LINEAGE → ACCESS_HISTORY → OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES, preferring the clean-error preflight. Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25; captures are banked so the build no longer depends on the window.) (**2026-07-17 — `schema_drift` SHIPPED end-to-end ([#881](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/881) engine + [#883](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/883) UI):** the first stateful monitor kind — one registry entry (#880's seam held: zero chain edits), a session-aware baseline-diff executor (the #794 comparison pattern; runners never see stateful kinds), per-datasource introspection (SQL via one bound-param `information_schema` query, flat-file via Parquet footer / bounded CSV sample, **Iceberg from table metadata — closing #859's drift leg**), `metric_value` = drifted-column count under ADR-0016 bands, dry-run preview that never writes, and the edit-gated re-baseline action (drop-the-row, recapture-next-run — never introspection on the API thread). The review caught two shippers: concurrent first runs both inserting the baseline — the loser's IntegrityError failed the WHOLE run and discarded sibling results (now `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`), and `UPPER()` information_schema matching MERGING two case-variant tables into a union schema no real table has (now exact-spelling-wins + classified 'ambiguous' refusal). Honest-limits documented: CSV dtype-inference noise (ignore_columns/re-baseline is the workaround) and whole-blob Parquet reads (range-read filed as [#882](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/882)). Two CI-only failures taught local-gate lessons now in memory: `pnpm lint` ≠ `tsc`, and version-dependent library inference must never anchor a fixture.) (**2026-07-16 — the W4 schema-drift prep chain shipped in one session:** #427 one-engine-per-runner ([#875](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/875) — review added `LazyEngine`, `pool_pre_ping` for the mixed-suite stale-connection regression, and the `owned_runner` lifecycle) · #592 slice 1, the `monitor_baselines` diff-reference store ([#876](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/876) — migration-safety review caught a missing `gen_random_uuid()` id default that only a real INSERT could surface) · #726+#429 monitor-kind strategy registry + runner-advertised capability gate ([#880](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/880)) · the #859 Iceberg metadata fast-paths ([#879](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/879) — review caught that snapshot `total-records` never nets row-level deletes: a merge-on-read table would have false-PASSED a volume floor; both metadata paths now require PROOF of zero deletes). **CI-wide outage fixed same session (#877/[#878](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/878)):** npm retired the legacy audit endpoints 2026-07-15 (410) and no released pnpm speaks the bulk endpoint (verified vs 11.13.1) — the required audit gate now queries the bulk advisory endpoint directly over the same lockfile graph, proven to FAIL on known-vulnerable versions and fail-closed on parse/endpoint drift; #877 stays open for the revert-to-pnpm-audit condition. The #858 warehouse-native lineage spike is consent-blocked on live prod reads.) (**2026-07-14 — #789 prefix-keyed per-IP buckets shipped ([#874](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/874)):** every per-IP bucket (unauth, per-provider webhook, both `ipall` ceilings) now keys on an address prefix — IPv4 /24, IPv6 /64, configurable — closing the NAT/proxy-pool dilution observed in the #725 post-deploy smoke (200 requests over 11 sibling /32s, none near the cap). The agentic review caught a shipping bug: IPv4-mapped IPv6 (`::ffff:a.b.c.d`, the dual-stack socket-peer form) folded to `::/64` — the ENTIRE IPv4 internet in one bucket, so one abuser would have 429'd every IPv4 client; now unwrapped via `ipv4_mapped` and folded as IPv4. Also fixed off review: a false ADR claim about CGNAT being unaffected (pool-based CGNAT now shares one bucket — masks are tunable, /32 disables), and test pins so a partial regression (ceilings reverting to raw /32) can't ship green.) (**2026-07-14 — #785 per-provider webhook rate-limit buckets shipped ([#872](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/872)):** the webhook class is now dual-key — a per provider+IP bucket (adf/airflow/dbt folded into the key, unknown segments share one bare-IP bucket so a scanner can't mint buckets) **plus** a `rl:webhook:ipall:{ip}` aggregate ceiling (`RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_IP_PER_MINUTE`, default 240) the agentic review added — without it, per-provider folding would have quietly multiplied the per-IP webhook budget by 4. The review also pinned the fail-open log's `key_kind` to the documented `{tok, ip}` domain (the post-#852 observability-drift lesson) and replaced a hand-copied provider frozenset with the public `db.models.ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS` — the claimed layering obstacle was false. ADR 0035 + deploy/README synced, incl. the new Redis SCAN pattern `rl:webhook:*:ip:*`.) (**2026-07-13 — prod lineage restored + the #829/#830 pair merged.** The lineage outage was ours: the dbt connection's ADLS SAS **expired 6 Jul**, so every 10-min poll failed `ClientAuthenticationError` and DataQ never read `manifest.json` — the dbt builds stayed green and kept publishing artifacts nobody consumed. Marquez was a red herring; the live path is the #759 dbt-manifest one. Re-minted the SAS (1y), re-ran the build, and the chain fired end to end (poll → `refresh_dbt_lineage` → `lineage_edges` → graph; the trigger-binding also ran the bound suite 4/4 green). **Two systemic defects fall out, still open on [#828](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/828):** a failing poll has **no user-visible signal** and the UI's empty-lineage state is indistinguishable from "this asset has no upstreams"; and `_POLL_LOOKBACK` is 15 min, so once auth was fixed the poll succeeded and still recorded **nothing** — every build produced during the outage had aged out and is stranded in the artifact store, recoverable only by re-running the producer. [#823](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/823) narrowed to the still-unproven Marquez pull path. Shipped alongside: [#829](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/829) — a suite was **unshareable from a phone** (the Add button painted at x=407 on a 390px viewport) — and [#830](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/830) — the assets UI printed the raw OL namespace (an Iceberg DSN) instead of a human label. **Both agentic reviews earned their keep:** on #829 the review caught that the new e2e spec was **worthless** (`toBeInViewport()` defaults to `ratio: 0`, and 71% of the broken button was on screen — it passed on the bug); on #830 it caught a **missed fifth render site** (the dashboard) and that the label still **leaked the DB username** when a catalog URI had no path, which made the commit message's own claim false.) (**user follow-up batch #801–#805 all closed 2026-07-12** — the five UI/ops follow-ups filed off the ADR-0015 session: mobile overlay nav ([#817](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/817)), hierarchical asset browse ([#818](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/818)), asset-health split into connection-vs-data-quality axes ([#819](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/819)), the left-to-right lineage graph ([#820](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/820)), and the Marquez local verification + runbook correction ([#821](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/821)). Four latent bugs surfaced and were fixed en route: a run that evaluated nothing read as a green "Passing"; a `cancelled` run rolled up green on **both** health axes; an asset→asset navigation showed the *previous* asset's data (nothing could reach it before the graph's clickable nodes); and the demo seed had **no lineage at all**, so a fresh install could not show the feature. **#813 closed via [#814](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/814)** — weekly `/security-scan` OWASP spot-check found the MCP `create_check`/`update_check` tools had no Pydantic layer, so `name`/`expectation_type` could exceed the REST 256/128 bounds and hit a raw Postgres `StringDataRightTruncation` instead of a clean 422; pushed `check_service.validate_lengths` (now public) into both, extended to `suite_io_service.import_suite`'s direct `Check(...)` construction too (agentic review caught that same-class gap pre-merge). Same session: 13 open CodeQL code-scanning alerts triaged — 10 dismissed as false-positive/by-design (module-level cache globals, a documented lazy-import cycle, an intentional test canary), 3 genuine (`side-effect-in-assert` ×2, `unnecessary-lambda`) fixed in [#815](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/815). **#799 comparison:columns + tolerance + optimization shipped (this PR)** — FDC's column grain as `comparison:columns` (per-column matched/mismatched/additional-per-side VALUE buckets, dropna parity, same sample shape → redaction/report reuse), numeric `config.tolerance` {absolute, relative} on both grains (the #808-review float32 finding — one-sided NULLs never tolerance-equal), and the shared keys+positions alignment pass (×1.52 on the 1M-row benchmark, bucket parity asserted; integer keys now render "1" not "1.0" in samples); catalog fixture regenerated, per-column breakdown + tolerance fields in the UI. · **ADR [0015](adr/0015-two-connection-comparison-check-model.md) written + merged ([#790](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/790), 2026-07-12)** — the two-connection comparison check model: the suite stays single-connection and supplies the **target under test**; a `comparison` check adds one **source** ref. Failure semantics #122: reader/engine refusals + unexpected exceptions → operational `error` results (classified messages, never raw text). The sample redactor gains a comparison branch — suffix-stripped policy matching so `pii_columns: [email]` masks `email_src`+`email_tgt`, unknown columns keep default-mask. **#793 comparison engine shipped ([#808](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/808))** — `datasources/comparison.py`, the FDC record-grain diff port (ADR 0014 §2: engine yes, app no): key-joined outer-merge buckets (matched / mismatched / additional-in-source / additional-in-target) with per-column mismatch counts, typed `DuplicateKeyError` (FDC silently drop_duplicates-ed — pairing must never be ambiguous) + `ComparisonInputError`, NA-safe nullable-`string` comparison (null==null matches; a literal "nan" ≠ NULL — fixes the FDC astype(str) quirk), pairwise numeric canonicalization (numpy NULL-int→float64 "10.0" vs Arrow "10" can't fabricate mismatches; dup-key detection on normalized keys, samples echo original values), mismatch-% badness scalar over the logical-row union, capped JSON-clean sample buckets. **#792 DatasetReader seam shipped ([#807](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/807))** — `services/dataset_reader.py`: per-datasource capped DataFrame reads behind profiler-style dict dispatch (SQL via the profiler engine plumbing, flat-file object read, `pyiceberg` scan; orchestration types 422), COUNT/scan-metadata preflight + post-read race guard + typed `DatasetTooLargeError` (fail-fast, never truncation), read-time read-only re-validation of query specs, `COMPARISON_MAX_ROWS` setting (default 100k, per-check `config.max_rows` override) documented in deploy/README. **#791 schema + authoring slice shipped ([#798](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/798))** — `checks.source_connection_id` FK + kind⇔presence CHECK (migration `b7c8d9e0f1a2`, up+down tested), author-time validation (source spec via `resolve_target`, read-only per-side SQL per ADR 0019, join keys, orchestration sources rejected), RESTRICT delete guard (409 `connection_in_use`), export/import `(name, env)` round-trip, version snapshots, and the run-path guard (a comparison check yields a per-check `error` result until the #794 runner — never fails its siblings' run). content.text: Hey, what the status

Here's where everything stands:

**1. Mutation spikes — done, findings filed as [#898](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/898)**
- Backend (mutmut, targeted at the newest #858 code): `warehouse_refresh.py` scored **236/239 killed (98.7%)** — but only after I caught a harness artifact: mutmut's copied tree has no `.env`, so the DB-backed tests silently skip and the whole module reads "no tests". The fix (export `TEST_DATABASE_URL`) is documented in the issue. The Snowflake/UC lineage providers scored ~65% on covered mutants — most survivors are unkillable SQL-text literals against stub connections (accepted), but there are real gaps: a `continue`→`break` truncation risk in `_from_object_dependencies`, the access-history None-guard, and unasserted success-path result shapes (`tier`/`skipped_tiers`).
- Frontend (Stryker, assets modules): **81.8%** overall; `lineageLayout.ts` worst at 66%. Real gaps: health.ts connection-axis guards and the assetTree sort comparator are unasserted.

**2. Security scan — clean, one hygiene fix shipped**
Bandit, pip-audit, and pnpm audit all clean. Full-history betterleaks found exactly one hit: the #849 redaction test's *original* fixture (the public jwt.io demo token) in an old blob — benign, unpurgeable without force-push, so I allowlisted its fingerprint via `.betterleaksignore` (PR [#899](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/899), reviewed, CI green, **merged**; full-history scan now reads zero). Targeted re-verification of the #849/#852 incident classes all held: logger-level scrub (including exported OTel attributes and rendered tracebacks), `azure.core` filter + no-propagate, span URLs stripped to path-only, and classified-never-raw error persistence in the new lineage columns.

**3. Docs — audited, current**
Ledger, ADR index (0036 present), feature-matrix, and README all reflect the latest merges. One real nit found and fixed: the deploy runbook's smoke probe expected `$FE/openapi.json → 404`, but since the ACA cutover the SPA catch-all serves 200 HTML there — the actual gate is `/api/v1/openapi.json → 404` (which holds in prod). Fix is committed on a docs branch, going out with the ledger update.

**4. Prod deploy — done and green, final validation step in flight**
- Note: your Azure subscription is **Enabled again as PayAsYouGo** (the "disabled 7/12" state is over — memory updated; no spending limit now, so awake resources bill real money).
- Pre-deploy checklist all green (CI green on `4f8b2ae0`, both new migrations additive and up/down-tested locally, no new required config). Deployed via the workflow: migrate job `Succeeded`, api/worker/frontend all on the SHA tag, beat started clean, probes correct (200/401/401/404), App Insights post-roll clean — the only exception was a single transient broker reconnect during the revision handoff, and its exported stack frames show `<redacted>`, confirming the scrubber is live in prod.
- Harness validation: ran **one** `window` cycle — all 5 mockdata jobs `Succeeded`, and the `tr_orders_landed` event trigger fired `pl_flow_a_orders`, which **Succeeded on live Snowflake** at 00:19Z. The harness auto-stopped afterward and I verified everything is back to `Stopped`/suspended (burn back to app-only ~CAD 4.5/day).
- **In flight right now:** a watcher is waiting for DataQ's 10-minute orchestration poll to ingest that ADF pipeline run and fire the bound suite (App Insights ingests with ~10 min lag, so confirmation is due shortly). Once it lands, I'll finalize the ledger entry (spike + scan + deploy record) on the docs branch and open the last PR.

One environment note: subagent-based work died mid-session on your monthly spend limit, so I did the security review and docs audit inline instead — same coverage, just done directly.

def _get_attributes(record: logging.LogRecord) -> Any:
                return _redact_pii(None, "", dict(LoggingHandler._get_attributes(record)))

        provider = LoggerProvider(resource=otel.build_resource(service_name))
        for exporter in exporters:
            provider.add_log_record_processor(BatchLogRecordProcessor(exporter))
        set_logger_provider(provider)

        with warnings.catch_warnings():
            # The sdk `LoggingHandler` carries a DeprecationWarning nudging toward
            # `opentelemetry-instrumentation-logging` — which only injects trace
            # context into records; it is NOT an export bridge. The sdk handler IS
            # the bridge (it's what the azure-monitor distro uses under the hood).
            # Suppress just that message so an unrelated deprecation still surfaces.
            warnings.filterwarnings(
                "ignore", message=r".*LoggingHandler.*", category=DeprecationWarning
            )
            handler = _RedactingOTelLogHandler(level=level, logger_provider=provider)
        # Same redacting ProcessorFormatter as stdout, so records exported to the
        # backend pass through `_redact_pii` (incl. the secret-string scrubber) — a
        # foreign record carrying a secret in its message is scrubbed before export
        # (#494). App-level structlog records are already redacted upstream.
        handler.setFormatter(formatter)
        # Break the exporter's feedback loop AT THE BRIDGE (#852).
        #
        # `azure.core`'s HTTP policy logs a request AND a response line for every call the
        # SDK makes — including the exporter's own uploads. Those records reach root,
        # re-enter this handler, are exported, and generate more uploads. Measured in prod
        # at ~10/sec.
        #
        # Setting that logger's level was not enough: it works in the API but NOT in the
        # Celery worker, where something in the worker/prefork logging setup restores INFO.
        # Rather than keep chasing who resets it, drop the records here, at the one place
        # they must pass through to reach the backend. A filter on the handler cannot be
        # defeated by a level reset elsewhere.
        #
        # Only the EXPORT is filtered — the records still reach stdout, so a real transport
        # problem is still visible in the container log.
        handler.addFilter(lambda record: not record.name.startswith("azure.core"))
        root.addHandler(handler)

        # Break the feedback loop: the SDK/exporter log their own "failed to export"
        # warnings on these namespaces; propagated to root they re-enter THIS bridge
        # and get re-queued for export — an amplifier that fires exactly when the
        # backend is unreachable. Keep them off the export pipeline (a well-known
        # OTel root-bridge footgun).
        for noisy in ("opentelemetry", "azure.monitor.opentelemetry"):
            logging.getLogger(noisy).propagate = False

        # Positive confirmation so "my logs never reached the backend" is diagnosable
        # (misconfig looks identical to telemetry-off otherwise). Endpoint is infra
        # config, not a secret; the App Insights connection string is NOT logged.
        logging.getLogger(__name__).info(
            "otel_log_export_configured service=%s exporters=%d azure=%s otlp=%s",
            service_name,
            len(exporters),
            bool(settings.applicationinsights_connection_string),
            settings.otel_exporter_otlp_endpoint or "off",
        )
    except Exception:
        # Degrade to stdout-only rather than take the process down (the #405 blast
        # radius: orchestration polling / scheduled dispatch / gap recovery / purge).
        logging.getLogger(__name__).exception("otel_log_export_setup_failed")


def configure_logging(service_name: str = "dataq") -> None:
    settings = get_settings()
    level = getattr(logging, settings.log_level)

    shared_processors: list[Processor] = [
        structlog.contextvars.merge_contextvars,
        _add_request_id,
        structlog.processors.add_log_level,
        structlog.processors.TimeStamper(fmt="iso", utc=True),
        _redact_pii,
    ]

    # Bridge stdlib logging (uvicorn.access, uvicorn.error, etc.) through the
    # same processor chain so every line out of the app is JSON with a
    # request_id when available. Without this the uvicorn access log emits
    # human-readable text that App Insights can't correlate (#50).
    formatter = structlog.stdlib.ProcessorFormatter(
        foreign_pre_chain=shared_processors,
        processors=[
            structlog.stdlib.ProcessorFormatter.remove_processors_meta,
            _dict_tracebacks_no_locals,
            # Re-run the redactor AFTER the traceback is rendered to a dict —
            # the pre-chain pass ran before the exception existed as strings, so
            # exception messages/frames never met the scrubber (#536). Idempotent
            # on everything else.
            _redact_pii,
            structlog.processors.JSONRenderer(),
        ],
    )

    handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
    handler.setFormatter(formatter)
    root = logging.getLogger()
    root.handlers = [handler]
    root.setLevel(level)

    # `azure.core`'s HTTP logging policy logs a full request AND response line for EVERY
    # HTTP call the Azure SDK makes — Key Vault reads, and (once the OTel bridge below is
    # attached) the exporter's own uploads to App Insights. That last one is a
    # self-sustaining amplifier: the upload is logged, the log record reaches root,
    # re-enters the export bridge, is uploaded, and is logged again. Measured in prod at
    # ~10 records/second — 19,000 in half an hour — which drowned the application's real
    # logs (a genuine orchestration-poll event became unfindable in the noise) and burned
    # ingestion quota (#852).
    #
    # Silenced at INFO rather than detached (`propagate = False`), so a genuine transport
    # WARNING from the SDK still reaches stdout and the backend; only the per-request
    # chatter that feeds the loop is dropped.
    logging.getLogger("azure.core.pipeline.policies.http_logging_policy").setLevel(logging.WARNING)

file.content:
# and identifiers are GDPR-grade personal data under Article 4(1).
        "oid",
        "aad_oid",
        "aad_object_id",
        "upn",
        "preferred_username",
        # Credential-bearing headers in their HYPHENATED header spelling (#849 review).
        # `authorization` was already covered above, but a headers dict is keyed
        # `x-api-key`, not `x_api_key`, and the key match is exact. Key-based redaction is
        # the sturdier of the two mechanisms — it cannot be fooled by a token SHAPE nobody
        # anticipated, which is exactly how the PAT leak happened.
        "api-key",
        "x-api-key",
        "cookie",
        "set-cookie",
        "user_id",
        "name",
        "display_name",
    }
)
_REDACTED = "<redacted>"

# Secret-bearing query params / `key=value` pairs embedded in *string* values
# (the key-based redaction below only catches dict KEYS). The prime case is the
# ADF webhook URL `…/events/adf?token=<secret>` (ADR 0006) surfacing inside a log
# message string — e.g. an access line or an error that interpolated the URL —
# where it would otherwise slip past the key redactor (#494).
_SECRET_QS_RE = re.compile(
    r"(?i)\b(token|sig|signature|secret|api[_-]?key|access[_-]?key|password)=[^&\s\"']+"
)
# URL-userinfo credentials (`REDACTED`, e.g. a SQLAlchemy engine
# URL `databricks://token:<PAT>@host/…`) — a different shape than the query-param
# scrub above, missed by it until #536.
_URL_USERINFO_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b([a-z][a-z0-9+.-]*://[^/\s:@\"']+):[^@/\s\"']+@")

# BARE credentials — a token sitting in a message with no `key=` prefix and no URL
# around it, so neither scrub above sees it (#849).
#
# This is not hypothetical: `fastapi_azure_auth` logs
# ``log.warning('Malformed token received. %s. Error: %s', access_token, …)``, and a
# DataQ PAT is not a JWT, so **every PAT-authenticated request** drove that line and
# shipped the raw token to App Insights. The token is a live bearer credential: anyone
# with read access to telemetry could authenticate as its owner.
#
# The lesson is the one CLAUDE.md §10 already states — redact at the LOGGER, not the
# call site. We do not control what a third-party library logs, and "grep the codebase
# for places we log tokens" would never have found this one, because we don't log it:
# a dependency does.
#
# `dq_live_` is duplicated from `api_key_service.TOKEN_PREFIX` deliberately — core.logging
# must not import the service layer (import cycle) — and a drift-guard test pins the two
# together so a renamed prefix can't silently stop being redacted.
_BEARER_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(
    r"(?i)(?:"
    r"dq_live_[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{6,}"  # a DataQ PAT (ADR 0026)
    r"|eyJ[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{4,}\.[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{4,}\.[A-Za-z0-9_\-]*"  # a JWT (AAD access token)
    # A `Bearer <token>` header echo. Deliberately requires the value to LOOK like a
    # token — ≥16 chars AND containing a digit or one of `-._~+/=` — because
    # `bearer\s+\w{8,}` also matches prose ("bearer authentication required") and an
    # over-eager redactor is not a safe failure: it hides the diagnostics an operator
    # needs, and the usual response is to weaken or disable the scrubber entirely, at
    # which point nothing is redacted at all (#849 review).
    r"|\bbearer\s+(?=[A-Za-z0-9._~+/\-]*[0-9\-._~+/])[A-Za-z0-9._~+/\-]{16,}=*"
    r")"
)


def _scrub_secret_strings(text: str) -> str:
    text = _SECRET_QS_RE.sub(lambda m: f"{m.group(1)}={_REDACTED}", text)
    text = _URL_USERINFO_RE.sub(lambda m: f"{m.group(1)}:{_REDACTED}@", text)
    return _BEARER_TOKEN_RE.sub(_REDACTED, text)


def _redact_pii(_logger: Any, _name: str, event_dict: EventDict) -> EventDict:
    def walk(value: Any) -> Any:
        if isinstance(value, dict):
            return {k: (_REDACTED if k.lower() in _PII_KEYS else walk(v)) for k, v in value.items()}
        if isinstance(value, list):
            return [walk(v) for v in value]
        if isinstance(value, str):
            return _scrub_secret_strings(value)
        return value

    result: EventDict = walk(event_dict)
    return result


def _add_request_id(_logger: Any, _name: str, event_dict: EventDict) -> EventDict:
    rid = request_id_var.get()
    if rid is not None:
        event_dict["request_id"] = rid
    return event_dict


# Traceback → dict WITHOUT frame locals (#536): `dict_tracebacks`' default

content.content:
``COUNT(*)``) — the only datasource-specific bit: a SQL runner builds+runs a
    query (`evaluate_monitors`), the Iceberg runner computes it natively
    (``scan().count()`` / a column ``MAX``). DB-free and unit-testable.

    A monitor that can't be evaluated — bad column/range (config error) or its
    scalar source raised (e.g. unknown column) — yields an ``errored`` outcome for
    *that* check only; its siblings still run (mirrors `CheckRunner`'s per-check
    `error`, #122). The scalar source must **not** swallow a datasource-establishment
    failure (open connection / load catalog): callers do that before the loop so it
    propagates and fails the whole run."""
    outcomes: list[CheckOutcome] = []
    for spec in monitors:
        try:
            outcomes.append(
                monitor_outcome(spec.kind, scalar=scalar_for(spec), config=spec.config, now=now)
            )
        except Exception as exc:  # one bad monitor errors, never its siblings
            outcomes.append(
                CheckOutcome(
                    expectation_type=monitor_expectation_type(spec.kind),
                    success=False,
                    errored=True,
                    error_message=str(exc),
                )
            )
    return outcomes


def evaluate_monitors(
    fetch_scalar: Callable[[str], Any],
    *,
    table: str,
    schema: str | None,
    catalog: str | None,
    monitors: list[MonitorSpec],
) -> list[CheckOutcome]:

toolUseResult.stdout:
``COUNT(*)``) — the only datasource-specific bit: a SQL runner builds+runs a
    query (`evaluate_monitors`), the Iceberg runner computes it natively
    (``scan().count()`` / a column ``MAX``). DB-free and unit-testable.

    A monitor that can't be evaluated — bad column/range (config error) or its
    scalar source raised (e.g. unknown column) — yields an ``errored`` outcome for
    *that* check only; its siblings still run (mirrors `CheckRunner`'s per-check
    `error`, #122). The scalar source must **not** swallow a datasource-establishment
    failure (open connection / load catalog): callers do that before the loop so it
    propagates and fails the whole run."""
    outcomes: list[CheckOutcome] = []
    for spec in monitors:
        try:
            outcomes.append(
                monitor_outcome(spec.kind, scalar=scalar_for(spec), config=spec.config, now=now)
            )
        except Exception as exc:  # one bad monitor errors, never its siblings
            outcomes.append(
                CheckOutcome(
                    expectation_type=monitor_expectation_type(spec.kind),
                    success=False,
                    errored=True,
                    error_message=str(exc),
                )
            )
    return outcomes

input.command:
gh issue create --title "security: per-monitor error_message persists raw str(exc) — route through classify_failure_reason like every sibling path" --label security --label priority/P2 --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Found by the 2026-07-17 targeted log-sanitization sweep (the #849/#828/#852 class review)

`backend/app/datasources/monitors.py:382` — when a single monitor's scalar evaluation raises, the per-check outcome stores **raw** exception text:

```python
except Exception as exc:  # one bad monitor errors, never its siblings
    outcomes.append(CheckOutcome(..., errored=True, error_message=str(exc)))
```

That `error_message` flows into `results.observed_value` JSONB (`run_service.py:88`: `observed = {"error": outcome.error_message}`) → the run-detail API → the UI. **This sink never passes the logger-level scrubber** — the logger discipline (CLAUDE.md §10) protects logs, not DB columns.

Every sibling path already classifies before persisting/echoing:
- `run.failure_reason` → `classify_failure_reason` (#702)
- `connections.last_poll_error` / `lineage_last_error` → classified (#839 / #890)
- `schema_drift.introspect_columns` → wraps unexpected exceptions as `SchemaIntrospectionError(f"could not introspect columns: {classify_failure_reason(exc)}")`
- dry-run → generic message + classified `detail.reason`
- comparison run path → `classify_failure_reason` (#809)

## Why it matters (and why it's P2, not P0)

The #828 incident showed Azure storage exceptions embed the **full SAS-signed URL** in their message. Establishment failures (open connection / load catalog — where credential-bearing messages live) deliberately propagate *out* of the monitor loop and get classified at run level, so the common case is safe. But the boundary is not airtight for **Iceberg** (#879 metadata fast-paths): FileIO reads happen lazily, so an S3/ADLS IO failure *inside* `scalar_for` (e.g. during `scan().count()` on a stale-credential FileIO) would put a botocore/azure message — which can include the request path/query — straight into a user-visible JSONB. Low likelihood, high blast-radius shape, and an inconsistency either way.

## Fix

`error_message=classify_failure_reason(exc)` at monitors.py:382 (plus a test pinning that a monitor-loop exception whose message carries a `sig=...` query string never reaches `observed_value` raw — test the *pipeline*, not the helper, per the #849 lesson).
EOF
)"

content.text:
Here's the full verdict on the log-sanitization class and its siblings — I walked every sink, not just the logs, and found **one real gap** (now filed as [#900](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/900)).

**The logger pipeline (the #849 fix) — verified sound, at every layer:**
- The scrubber runs at the **root handler's formatter** with a `foreign_pre_chain`, so a credential logged by *any* library — the exact way #849 happened — gets scrubbed, not just our own structlog calls. Three shapes covered: `key=value` query params (including `sig=`, so SAS tokens), URL userinfo (`://token:PAT@host`, the #536 live-smoke leak), and **bare** tokens (`dq_live_…`, JWTs, `Bearer <…>` — the #849 shape). The PAT prefix has a drift-guard test pinning it to what `api_key_service` actually issues, so a renamed prefix can't silently escape redaction.
- Tracebacks are rendered to dicts **without frame locals** and then **re-scrubbed after rendering** — the #536 hole (exception text that didn't exist as strings when the pre-chain ran) stays closed.
- The OTel export bridge scrubs the exported **attributes** too, not just the message body — and I confirmed it live: the one post-roll exception in App Insights tonight shows `<redacted>` frame names.

**The #852 amplifier — verified dead two ways:** `azure.core` records are dropped by a **filter on the export handler** (can't be defeated by the level reset that broke the worker last time) *plus* `http_logging_policy` at WARNING, and `opentelemetry`/`azure.monitor.opentelemetry` have `propagate=False`. Filtering is export-only, so real transport failures still hit stdout.

**Spans:** URL-bearing attributes are overwritten with path-only and `url.query` blanked — a SAS in a query string can't ride a span.

**The DB/API sinks (the #828 class — scrubbers don't protect these, discipline does):**
- `run.failure_reason`, `connections.last_poll_error`, the new `lineage_last_error`/`degraded_reason` (#890), schema_drift introspection errors, dry-run echoes, comparison-run errors, MCP tool errors, alerting payloads — **all verified classified-never-raw**. schema_drift and dry-run explicitly wrap unexpected exceptions in `classify_failure_reason` before the message is built.
- **The gap ([#900](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/900), P2):** the per-monitor error path (`monitors.py:382`) stores raw `str(exc)` into `results.observed_value` → run-detail UI. Auth/connection failures normally propagate out and get classified first, so the common case is safe — but Iceberg's lazy FileIO (#879) means an S3/ADLS failure *inside* the monitor loop could land a botocore message (which can carry the request path/query) into a user-visible JSONB unscrubbed. One-line fix + a pipeline-level test; filed rather than hot-fixed since it's not exploitable on the currently-deployed flows and deserves its own reviewed PR per the working agreements.

Also relevant: the full-history secret scan is now permanently **zero** (#899 merged) — so the next time something like #849 pushes a token-shaped string anywhere near git, it can't hide behind a known false positive.

Meanwhile the deploy validation watcher is still open — the post-window orchestration poll's telemetry hasn't surfaced in App Insights yet (ingestion lag). I'll close out the ledger PR once it confirms the pipeline-run → suite-trigger chain.

content.text:
Confirmed — `WAREHOUSE_LINEAGE_ENABLED` is unset on both prod apps, so the beat is a no-op there. Here's the honest per-warehouse picture:

**What's built and proven (merged in this deploy, #886–#891):**

- **Unity Catalog — yes, full table-level lineage.** The provider reads `system.access.table_lineage`, which the spike proved works on your Free Edition workspace with real captured payloads: incremental `event_time` watermark, 6-hour safety re-scan for ingestion lag, never-prune (append-only log), identity byte-identical to `asset_identity`. The one scope limit: **table grain only** — `system.access.column_lineage` exists but column-level lineage wasn't built.

- **Snowflake — tiered, and on your current account the ceiling is the floor tier.** The ladder is GET_LINEAGE → ACCESS_HISTORY → OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES, and on the trial's **Standard edition**: GET_LINEAGE preflights cleanly to `0A000` (Enterprise feature — and even on Enterprise its per-seed traversal is deferred, [#892](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/892)); ACCESS_HISTORY is *silently empty* on Standard — the spike proved this with 45k QUERY_HISTORY rows against 0 ACCESS_HISTORY rows, which is exactly why the provider corroborates before trusting an empty result. So what you actually get is **tier 3, OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES: view/object-dependency lineage** (live-verified with real captures), with `degraded_reason` honestly telling the operator that richer tiers need Enterprise, surfaced on the asset graph by #891. That's "full" for what Standard edition can physically expose — DML-derived table→table lineage simply isn't available there.

**What's running in prod right now: neither.** The beat shipped dark by default (needs `ACCOUNT_USAGE` / `system.access` grants the connection principal may lack, so it's opt-in per the ADR). Prod's live lineage today is still the dbt-manifest path (#759) plus run-stamped assets. The end-to-end-against-a-deployed-warehouse verification is tracked as [#893](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/893) — the spike parked live reads and banked the captures instead.

**The clock matters here:** the Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25. If you want #893 done against live Snowflake (not just captures), it has to happen in the next week. The spike already proved both principals can read their lineage sources, so the steps are just: set `WAREHOUSE_LINEAGE_ENABLED=true` on the worker, let the beat run (or kick it), and check `lineage_last_tier`/watermark on both connections plus new `source='snowflake'`/`'unity_catalog'` edges on the graph. That's a prod config change with real billing behind it now, so I haven't flipped it — say the word and I'll enable it and run the live verification.

content.content:
@celery_app.task(name="refresh_warehouse_lineage")  # type: ignore[untyped-decorator]  # celery task decorator is unannotated
def refresh_warehouse_lineage() -> int:
    """Celery-beat entry point — refresh warehouse-native lineage for every eligible
    connection (#858, ADR 0034).

    **Dark by default**: no-ops with zero queries unless ``WAREHOUSE_LINEAGE_ENABLED``
    is set — the warehouse views it reads (Snowflake ACCOUNT_USAGE, UC system.access)
    need a grant the connection's principal may not have, so this stays opt-in like the
    catalog pull. Iterates the Snowflake / Unity Catalog connections and refreshes each
    **independently** (`refresh_connection_lineage` is fail-soft per connection: one
    unreachable warehouse records a classified error and never aborts the sweep). Daily
    cadence — a cache refresh of external truth, not a liveness path. Returns the number
    of connections successfully refreshed.
    """
    if not get_settings().warehouse_lineage_enabled:
        return 0
    session = get_session()
    try:
        connections = list(
            session.scalars(
                select(Connection).where(Connection.type.in_(("snowflake", "unity_catalog")))
            )
        )
        refreshed = 0
        for connection in connections:
            outcome = warehouse_refresh.refresh_connection_lineage(
                session, connection=connection, secret_store=get_secret_store()
            )
            if outcome is not None:
                refreshed += 1
        return refreshed
    finally:
        session.close()

content.content:
"""Warehouse-native lineage — pull edges straight from the warehouse (#858, ADR 0034).

The catalog `LineageProvider` (`lineage.provider`, Marquez) exists to reconcile a
byte-mismatched identity we *cannot construct* (#823 — a producer spells a name in
some other case, so we must enumerate the catalog and fold). **This seam solves a
different, simpler problem and is therefore a distinct interface.** Querying the
warehouse directly returns identifiers in the engine's OWN case — Snowflake
`ACCOUNT_USAGE` returns UPPER, Unity Catalog `system.access` returns lower — which is
**byte-identical to `services.asset_identity`**. No enumerate-and-fold step, no
node-graph normalization: a warehouse provider runs SQL and yields
``(upstream_identity, downstream_identity)`` edge pairs directly.

The pull is **incremental where the source is a log** (UC `table_lineage` has an
`event_time` watermark) and **snapshot-diff where the source is current-state**
(Snowflake `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` is a view, not a log — no event time, so its refresh
upserts + prunes by `last_seen` like the dbt path). Both write `lineage_edges` with a
real ``connection_id`` (the pull rides a datasource connection) — the non-NULL,
full-unique-constraint regime, so a Snowflake refresh never touches a UC or dbt row.

**Honest degradation (#828).** A warehouse offers lineage at tiers that vary by edition
and grant. The provider tries the richest available and reports **which tier answered**
so the UI can say a graph is view-level-only rather than paint a confident empty state.
The tier ladder and its live-verified behaviour are per-provider (see `snowflake.py`).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Sequence
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import StrEnum
from typing import Protocol, runtime_checkable

from backend.app.services.asset_identity import AssetIdentity


class LineageTier(StrEnum):
    """Which source answered a warehouse lineage pull — surfaced so a degraded graph
    never reads as a confident one (#828).

    The values are stable UI/telemetry tags. ``NONE`` means the pull could run but no
    tier was available (e.g. every richer tier is edition-gated and the floor found
    nothing) — distinct from :class:`WarehouseLineageUnavailableError`, which means the pull
    could not run at all.
    """

    # Snowflake
    SNOWFLAKE_GET_LINEAGE = "snowflake_get_lineage"  # SNOWFLAKE.CORE.GET_LINEAGE (Enterprise+)
    SNOWFLAKE_ACCESS_HISTORY = "snowflake_access_history"  # ACCOUNT_USAGE.ACCESS_HISTORY (Ent+)
    SNOWFLAKE_OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES = "snowflake_object_dependencies"  # view-level, all editions
    # Unity Catalog
    UNITY_CATALOG_SYSTEM_ACCESS = "unity_catalog_system_access"  # system.access.table_lineage
    NONE = "none"

    @property
    def is_column_level(self) -> bool:
        """True for tiers that carry column-level detail (used to label the graph)."""
        return self in {self.SNOWFLAKE_GET_LINEAGE, self.SNOWFLAKE_ACCESS_HISTORY}


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class LineageEdgePair:
    """One directed edge as two OpenLineage identities — the warehouse provider's
    output unit. Both endpoints are already in the engine's own case, so they join
    `assets` byte-for-byte with no fold (the whole point vs the catalog seam)."""

    upstream: AssetIdentity
    downstream: AssetIdentity


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class WarehouseLineageResult:
    """A successful warehouse pull: the edges found, the tier that produced them, and a
    human note when the answer is degraded (edition-gated, missing grant) — never a
    silent empty (#828).

    ``freshness_lag`` names the source's known staleness (Snowflake `ACCESS_HISTORY`
    lags 2-3h) so the UI can qualify "current as of ~3h ago"; ``None`` when the source
    is current-state (`OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES`, `GET_LINEAGE`).
    """

content.content:
/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/lineage/warehouse_snowflake.py:65:class SnowflakeLineageProvider:
/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/lineage/warehouse_snowflake.py:74:    def fetch_edges(
/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/lineage/warehouse_unity_catalog.py:72:    def fetch_edges(
192:def refresh_connection_lineage(
193-    session: Session, *, connection: Connection, secret_store: SecretStore
194-) -> WarehouseRefreshOutcome | None:
195-    """Refresh one warehouse connection's lineage AND persist its refresh state (#858).
196-
197-    The beat task's per-connection unit: resolve the provider for the connection type,
198-    open a datasource connection, run :func:`refresh_warehouse_edges` from the stored
199-    watermark, and record the outcome onto the connection —
200-    ``lineage_watermark`` (advanced for a log source), ``lineage_last_tier`` /
201-    ``lineage_degraded_reason`` (so the UI can qualify the graph, #828),
202-    ``lineage_last_refresh_at``, and a CLASSIFIED ``lineage_last_error`` (never raw
203-    exception text — the `last_poll_error` precedent).
204-
205-    Fail-soft and self-contained: a connection whose warehouse is unreachable records a
206-    classified error and returns ``None`` without touching the edge cache — one bad
207-    connection never aborts the sweep. Returns ``None`` for a non-warehouse type (no
208-    provider) with no state written.
209-    """
210-    provider = get_warehouse_lineage_provider(connection.type)
211-    if provider is None:
212-        return None
213-
214-    # Lazy import: profile_service pulls the heavy datasource stack; keep it off this
215-    # module's import cost (and clear of any import cycle) until a refresh actually runs.
216-    from backend.app.services.profile_service import _open_connection
217-
218-    # A snapshot source ignores the stored watermark; a log source reads from it.
219-    since = connection.lineage_watermark if provider.is_incremental else None
220-    try:
221-        with _open_connection(connection, secret_store) as conn:
222-            outcome = refresh_warehouse_edges(
223-                session, connection=connection, provider=provider, conn=conn, since=since
224-            )
225-    except Exception as exc:
226-        # Opening the datasource failed (bad/unreadable credential, unreachable host).
227-        _record_refresh_error(session, connection, exc)
228-        return None
229-
230-    if outcome is None:
231-        # refresh_warehouse_edges already logged the unavailable/failed cause and left
232-        # the cache untouched; surface it as connection state so the UI/health can see it.

    def fetch_edges(
        self,
        conn: object,
        *,
        connection_config: dict[str, object],
        since: datetime | None = None,
    ) -> WarehouseLineageResult:
        namespace = self._namespace(connection_config)
        skipped: list[str] = []

        # Tier 1: GET_LINEAGE. Its absence is a clean 0A000 — descend, don't fail.
        # The reason is carried from the exception, NOT hard-coded: on Enterprise the
        # function IS supported but its per-seed traversal is deferred (#858 follow-up),
        # so an Enterprise operator must not see a false "unsupported on this edition".
        try:
            edges = self._from_get_lineage(conn, namespace)
            return WarehouseLineageResult(
                edges=edges, tier=LineageTier.SNOWFLAKE_GET_LINEAGE, skipped_tiers=tuple(skipped)
            )
        except _FeatureUnsupportedError as exc:
            skipped.append(f"get_lineage: {exc}")

        # Tier 2: ACCESS_HISTORY. Present-but-empty on Standard — corroborate.
        try:
            access = self._from_access_history(conn, namespace)
            if access is not None:
                return WarehouseLineageResult(
                    edges=access,
                    tier=LineageTier.SNOWFLAKE_ACCESS_HISTORY,
                    freshness_lag="~2-3h (ACCOUNT_USAGE latency)",
                    skipped_tiers=tuple(skipped),
                )
            skipped.append("access_history: empty (edition-gated or no write history)")
        except _FeatureUnsupportedError:
            skipped.append("access_history: unsupported on this edition")

        # Tier 3: OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES — the all-editions floor.

input.content:
"""#893 live verification — run the REAL warehouse lineage providers against the LIVE
Snowflake + Unity Catalog warehouses, through the same open-connection path prod uses.

Credentials arrive ONLY via env vars (SF_PAT / UC_PAT, injected inline from Key Vault);
nothing secret is printed. Output: tier, edge counts, sample identities, degraded
reasons — plus a raw capture of UC system.access.column_lineage (for the column-lineage
build) written to a scratchpad JSON with no credentials in it.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, "/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ")

from backend.app.db.models import Connection  # noqa: E402
from backend.app.lineage.warehouse_snowflake import SnowflakeLineageProvider  # noqa: E402
from backend.app.lineage.warehouse_unity_catalog import UnityCatalogLineageProvider  # noqa: E402
from backend.app.services.profile_service import _open_connection  # noqa: E402

OUT = "/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-arijit-Coding-Python-DataQ/ac097c7b-6067-40e0-bf7f-36fe97d011df/scratchpad"


class _EnvSecretStore:
    def __init__(self, mapping: dict[str, str]):
        self._m = mapping

    def get(self, ref: str) -> str:
        return self._m[ref]


def show(result, label):
    print(f"--- {label}")
    print("tier:", result.tier)
    print("edges:", len(result.edges))
    print("skipped_tiers:", list(result.skipped_tiers))
    print("degraded_reason:", getattr(result, "degraded_reason", None))
    print("freshness_lag:", result.freshness_lag)
    print("watermark:", getattr(result, "watermark", None))
    for pair in list(result.edges)[:8]:
        print(f"  {pair.upstream.namespace} | {pair.upstream.name}  ->  {pair.downstream.name}")


def main() -> None:
    sf_conn = Connection(
        name="live-test-snowflake",
        type="snowflake",
        env="dev",
        config={
            "account": "PVQSOEQ-ZGB34383",
            "user": "DATAQ_READER",
            "database": "DATAQ_DB",
            "schema": "RETAIL",
            "warehouse": "DATAQ_WH",
            "role": "DATAQ_READER",
            "auth_type": "password",
        },
        secret_ref="sf",
    )
    uc_conn = Connection(
        name="live-test-uc",
        type="unity_catalog",
        env="dev",
        config={
            "workspace_url": "https://dbc-4492dde4-090c.cloud.databricks.com",
            "warehouse_id": "b6403b6e3734f0ce",
        },
        secret_ref="uc",
    )
    store = _EnvSecretStore({"sf": os.environ["SF_PAT"], "uc": os.environ["UC_PAT"]})

    if os.environ.get("RUN_SF", "1") == "1":
        provider = SnowflakeLineageProvider()
        with _open_connection(sf_conn, store) as conn:
            result = provider.fetch_edges(conn, connection_config=sf_conn.config)
            show(result, "SNOWFLAKE (live)")

    if os.environ.get("RUN_UC", "1") == "1":
        provider = UnityCatalogLineageProvider()
        with _open_connection(uc_conn, store) as conn:
            result = provider.fetch_edges(conn, connection_config=uc_conn.config)
            show(result, "UNITY CATALOG (live)")

            # Column-lineage capture for the build (#column-slice): raw rows, no creds.
            rows = conn.exec_driver_sql(
                "SELECT source_table_full_name, source_column_name, "
                "target_table_full_name, target_column_name, "
                "CAST(event_time AS STRING) AS event_time "
                "FROM system.access.column_lineage "
                "WHERE source_table_full_name IS NOT NULL "
                "AND target_table_full_name IS NOT NULL "
                "AND source_column_name IS NOT NULL "
                "AND target_column_name IS NOT NULL "
                "ORDER BY event_time DESC LIMIT 200"
            ).fetchall()
            payload = [list(r) for r in rows]
            with open(f"{OUT}/uc_column_lineage_capture.json", "w") as fh:
                json.dump(payload, fh, indent=1)
            print("--- UC column_lineage: rows captured:", len(payload))
            for r in payload[:6]:
                print("  ", r[0], ".", r[1], " -> ", r[2], ".", r[3], sep="")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

        provider = SnowflakeLineageProvider()
        with _open_connection(sf_conn, store) as conn:
            result = provider.fetch_edges(conn, connection_config=sf_conn.config)
            show(result, "SNOWFLAKE (live)")

    if os.environ.get("RUN_UC", "1") == "1":
        provider = UnityCatalogLineageProvider()
        with _open_connection(uc_conn, store) as conn:
            result = provider.fetch_edges(conn, connection_config=uc_conn.config)
            show(result, "UNITY CATALOG (live)")

            # Column-lineage capture for the build (#column-slice): raw rows, no creds.
            rows = conn.exec_driver_sql(
                "SELECT source_table_full_name, source_column_name, "
                "target_table_full_name, target_column_name, "
                "CAST(event_time AS STRING) AS event_time "
                "FROM system.access.column_lineage "
                "WHERE source_table_full_name IS NOT NULL "
                "AND target_table_full_name IS NOT NULL "
                "AND source_column_name IS NOT NULL "
                "AND target_column_name IS NOT NULL "
                "ORDER BY event_time DESC LIMIT 200"
            ).fetchall()
            payload = [list(r) for r in rows]
            with open(f"{OUT}/uc_column_lineage_capture.json", "w") as fh:
                json.dump(payload, fh, indent=1)
            print("--- UC column_lineage: rows captured:", len(payload))
            for r in payload[:6]:
                print("  ", r[0], ".", r[1], " -> ", r[2], ".", r[3], sep="")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
