input.command:
gh issue create --title "PROD: worker beat silent since the 2026-07-18 00:12Z deploy roll — no scheduled task has executed" --label bug --label priority/P0 --label ops --milestone "v1.1 Week 3 — Azure wind-down + local-first posture" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What is happening

Since the Deploy roll to `4f8b2ae0` (worker revision `dataq-app-worker--0000051`, replica up 2026-07-18T00:11:57Z), the worker has executed **zero beat tasks**: no `Scheduler: Sending due task` line in stdout after `beat: Starting...` (00:12:37Z), and **zero worker traces in App Insights since 00:14Z** while API-side traces flow normally (so the telemetry pipeline itself is alive — the worker genuinely has nothing to say). Replica state `Running`; no restarts; no exceptions beyond the expected roll-handoff broker reconnect at 00:13:02Z.

Blast radius = the #405/#852 set: scheduled-suite dispatch, ADF/Airflow/dbt orchestration polling, gap recovery, stuck-run reaper, sample purge — all stopped. Concrete missed work: the post-deploy harness window's `pl_flow_a_orders` (Succeeded 00:19:13Z) was never ingested — no `pipeline_runs` poll fired; and the 15-min `_POLL_LOOKBACK` means anything older than 15 min at recovery time is stranded (the #828 recovery note applies: re-run producers or rely on gap recovery once the beat is back).

## Evidence trail (all read-only)

- Old revision (`--0000050`) beat lines visible in App Insights up to 00:06Z (`Scheduler: Sending due task` → `received` → `succeeded`) — normal.
- New revision: startup banner (13 registered tasks incl. the new dark `refresh_warehouse_lineage`), `tracing_configured` ×4, redis Connected, `celery ready`, `beat: Starting...` — then silence for 2h+.
- App Insights `traces` last 2h: only `request`/`auth_user_resolved` (API). Zero worker events.
- ACA system log: recurring KEDA `ScaledObject doesn't have correct triggers specification` warning (count 23) — possibly long-standing, noting for the investigation.
- Deploy delta's only beat-schedule change is an inert `refresh-warehouse-lineage: {schedule: 86400.0}` entry (dark by default, flag unset in prod).

## Why it wasn't caught by the smoke

The post-deploy check verified `celery ready` + `beat: Starting...` and an early grep for consumption matched the **startup task banner** (`. dispatch_due_schedules`), not an executed task — a false positive the checklist should close: the post-deploy gate must require an observed `Task ... succeeded` (or a DB `last_polled_at` advance) within ~15 min of the roll, not a startup line. (The #852 lesson again: alive ≠ consuming.)

## Immediate mitigation (needs an operator — the session's autonomous lane is permission-blocked for prod mutations)

1. `az containerapp revision restart -n dataq-app-worker -g dataq-rg --revision dataq-app-worker--0000051`
2. Watch: `az containerapp logs show -n dataq-app-worker -g dataq-rg --type console --follow` for `Scheduler: Sending due task` within ~2 min, then a `poll_orchestration_runs ... succeeded`.
3. If the restart does NOT restore ticking → deterministic startup wedge in the new image; roll back the worker: `az containerapp update -n dataq-app-worker -g dataq-rg --image ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:e274bcf46a71b6864bf633b5c9a2ceb86b5a3bf1` and escalate this issue to root-cause before any further deploy.
4. After recovery: `recover_orchestration_gaps` covers the 30-min-window backlog; the harness-window pipeline run (00:19Z) may need the #828 recovery note (re-run producer) if outside every window.

## Root-cause candidates to check once interactive

- Embedded beat thread wedged at first tick (deadlock in schedule setup?) — a thread dump (`kill -USR1`? py-spy via exec) would tell.
- #854-class: something on the beat thread blocking forever on a lock the engine timeout doesn't cover.
- The KEDA ScaledObject warning — if scale rules changed behaviour on the new revision.
- Anything in the delta importing at worker startup that can block (the #875 shared-engine refactor touches runner setup — though that's per-run, not beat-time).
EOF
)"

content.text:
**⚠️ You need to act on prod — I found a live incident and I'm permission-blocked on the fix.** Full picture:

## 1. The good news first: column lineage is BUILT and live-verified

- **UC — full success, live against the real workspace with the prod PAT:** table tier `unity_catalog_system_access`, 6 real edges, and with the new `system.access.column_lineage` pull **all 6 edges refined with real column pairs** — including the derived mappings `comment → sentiment` and `event_ts → sale_date`. That's the shipped code path, not a simulation, run twice (before and after the review fix).
- **Redacted box:** the asset API now applies the exact #845 one-rule to column data — either endpoint outside your grants ⇒ pairs withheld server-side, `column_count` only — and AssetDetail renders a locked "N column links · restricted asset" box. Pinned by a wire-level test (hidden column name appears nowhere in the raw body).
- **Snowflake, honestly:** the prod PAT authenticates but is role-locked to `DATAQ_READER`, which has **no ACCOUNT_USAGE grant — so every tier is denied** (the spike's captures were made with the admin role, which is now MFA-walled for programmatic use). The live test still paid off twice: it proved the classified-unavailable behaviour live, and it caught a real bug — an authorization error **aborted** the tier ladder instead of descending ([#902](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/902), fixed in this PR). Snowflake **column** grain is ACCESS_HISTORY/Enterprise-only — genuinely unavailable on your Standard trial, reported honestly.
- All on PR [#903](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/903) (fixes #901+#902): full gate green (2363 backend tests / 97.5%, frontend 89.7%), reviewed with 3 findings **fixed in-PR**, CI running now. **I'm holding the merge/deploy until the incident below is resolved.**

## 2. 🔴 PROD INCIDENT — worker beat silent since today's deploy ([#904](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/904), P0)

Since the 00:12Z roll, the worker has executed **zero beat tasks**: `beat: Starting…` then 2+ hours of silence in both stdout and App Insights (API traces flow fine, so telemetry itself works — the worker genuinely does nothing). Scheduled runs, orchestration polling, gap recovery, purge: all stopped. The smoke's ADF pipeline run (00:19Z, Succeeded) was never ingested — that's how I caught it. My earlier "consumption observed" check was a false positive (it matched the startup banner) — mea culpa, and the checklist fix is in the issue.

**Please run (in order):**
```bash
az containerapp revision restart -n dataq-app-worker -g dataq-rg --revision dataq-app-worker--0000051
az containerapp logs show -n dataq-app-worker -g dataq-rg --type console --follow   # expect "Scheduler: Sending due task" within ~2 min
# if it does NOT tick after restart → roll back:
az containerapp update -n dataq-app-worker -g dataq-rg --image ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:e274bcf46a71b6864bf633b5c9a2ceb86b5a3bf1
```

## 3. Other actions only you can do

- **Enable warehouse lineage in prod** (after the beat is healthy): `az containerapp update -n dataq-app-worker -g dataq-rg --set-env-vars WAREHOUSE_LINEAGE_ENABLED=true`
- **Snowflake grant** (Snowsight, as the admin — programmatic admin auth is MFA-blocked): `GRANT IMPORTED PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE SNOWFLAKE TO ROLE DATAQ_READER;` (or the finer `SNOWFLAKE.GOVERNANCE_VIEWER` database role). Without it Snowflake lineage stays classified-unavailable; with it you get the OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES floor on your Standard trial. Clock: trial lapses ~07-25.
- Alternatively, add a Bash allowlist rule for `az containerapp update/restart` if you want me to handle these myself next time.

Everything else from the day is done: spikes filed (#898), security sweep clean (+#899 merged, #900 filed), docs current, deploy itself green (api/frontend fine — it's specifically the worker beat), harness validated and back asleep.

6	
7	Column mappings belong to the table→table edge they refine, so they ride the edge
8	row as a JSONB list of ``[upstream_column, downstream_column]`` pairs rather than a
9	fourth lineage table — the upsert/prune/provenance regime stays exactly the one
10	``lineage_edges`` already has, and a future column-grain blast radius can promote
11	them to their own table without losing anything (the pairs are all it would need).
12	
13	**Backward-compatible by construction** (CLAUDE.md §6): one nullable additive
14	column. NULL means "no column pairs ever observed for this edge" (dbt, Snowflake
15	OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES — or a UC edge whose queries predate the pull window); the
16	write path only ever records observed pairs, merged union-wise across refreshes.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import sqlalchemy as sa
22	from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import JSONB
23	
24	from alembic import op

## claude.ai Microsoft Learn
# Microsoft Learn MCP Server

This server gives structured access to official Microsoft and Azure documentation via three tools:

## Tools

### microsoft_docs_search
Search official documentation and return up to 10 concise, high-quality content chunks (max 500 tokens each), including title, URL, and excerpt.

- Use first to get a quick, reliable overview
- Ideal for grounding answers in Microsoft knowledge

### microsoft_code_sample_search
Search for code snippets and examples in official Microsoft Learn documentation and return up to 20 relevant, high-quality code samples.

- Use when you need to provide sample Microsoft/Azure related code in your answers.  
- Ideal for generating code snippets or practical implementation examples.  
- Optional parameter `language` can filter results. 

### microsoft_docs_fetch
Fetch and convert full Microsoft documentation pages to markdown.

- Use after search when you need full content from a specific URL
- Required for detailed tutorials, troubleshooting, prerequisites, code samples, or when search results are incomplete or outdated

## Workflow

1. Use `microsoft_docs_search` to find relevant documents.
2. If you need code examples or practical snippets, use `microsoft_code_sample_search`.
3. If deeper or complete information is needed, use `microsoft_docs_fetch`.

**Search gives breadth. Code Sample Search gives practical examples. Fetch gives depth.**

All content comes from Microsoft Learn or official sources, returned in clean markdown format.

## claude.ai Postman
Read the instructions resource completely for detailed usage instructions before answering any API-related questions.

content.text: Gap recovery's window is 1 hour, so the 00:19 ADF run (6h stranded) is permanently outside it — the documented #828 limitation; I'll record it rather than re-wake the harness. Filing the incident:

input.command:
gh issue create --title "prod: worker silently wedged for 6h after a roll-time broker blip — beat never ticked, no retry logs, no liveness signal" --label bug --label priority/P1 --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What happened (2026-07-18, prod)

The 00:10Z deploy (SHA `4f8b2ae0`, run 29622325290) rolled the worker at 00:12. The new replica booted normally — tasks registered, redis connected, `celery ready`, `beat: Starting...` at 00:12:37 — then, after a **transient broker disconnect at 00:13:02** (`consumer: Connection to broker lost. Trying to re-establish the connection...`, normal roll-time churn; Redis itself was healthy the whole time, up since 07-12 with 0 restarts) and one `missed heartbeat` line at 00:13:05, the process went **completely silent for 6 hours**:

- zero beat ticks (a healthy embedded beat logs `Scheduler: Sending due task` every 60s),
- zero task receipts, zero reconnect retries, zero exceptions,
- replica `Running`, restartCount 0, App Insights worker traces frozen at 00:13:05.

Blast radius: scheduled-suite dispatch, ADF/Airflow/dbt orchestration polling, gap recovery, and the retention purge were all dead 00:13→06:17Z. `az containerapp revision restart` at 06:17 fully restored it — beat started and `recover_orchestration_gaps` ran immediately (3 connections swept).

**Stranded side-effect:** the post-deploy harness smoke's `pl_flow_a_orders` run (Succeeded in ADF at 00:19Z) fell outside both `_POLL_LOOKBACK` (15m) and the gap-recovery window (1h) by the time the worker was revived — `recorded: 0`, exactly the #828-documented backlog gap. Not re-produced (the pipeline itself succeeded; the binding path was already live-verified 07-13); noted here so the ledger stays honest.

## Why this is a class, not a one-off

This is the third "worker looks alive but does nothing" incident (#852 exporter starvation, #854 unbounded row-lock wait, now a wedged broker-reconnect). Each had a different cause; all shared the same failure surface: **the embedded beat has no liveness signal**, so the platform's `Running` state and the app's own logs both report health while every periodic responsibility is dead. Detection was manual each time.

## Suggested fixes (defense in depth)

1. **Beat liveness you can probe**: beat heartbeat file/redis key bumped per tick + a Container Apps liveness probe (or the #841 `HealthPublisher` path) that fires when the bump goes stale for N minutes. This catches ALL members of the class regardless of cause.
2. **Reconnect must be loud and bounded**: investigate why the kombu/celery re-establish loop after the 00:13:02 disconnect neither retried-with-logs nor raised — pin `broker_connection_retry_on_startup` / heartbeat settings so a wedged reconnect dies (and the replica restarts) instead of hanging forever. A crash-looping worker is strictly better than a silent one.
3. **The DB-truth alert already proposed in #843's family**: `last_polled_at` staleness across ALL connections is the one signal that cannot lie (the #852 lesson) — alert on it workspace-wide, not per-connection.

## Evidence
- Worker console: boot banner ends 00:13:05Z; next line is `beat: Starting...` 06:17:32Z (post-restart).
- App Insights: `traces | summarize max(timestamp) by cloud_RoleName` → dataq-worker frozen at 00:13:05Z (850 boot-window records, then nothing).
- The restart + recovery: `recover_orchestration_gaps` received/succeeded 06:17:32Z (`{'connections': 3, 'recorded': 0, ...}`).
EOF
)"

content.text:
**The prod issue is fixed.** Here's the full picture:

## What was wrong

The worker had been **silently dead for 6 hours**. After last night's deploy rolled the worker at 00:12Z, the new replica booted cleanly (`celery ready`, `beat: Starting...`) — then a transient broker disconnect at 00:13:02 (normal roll-time churn; Redis itself was healthy the whole time) wedged the reconnect, and the entire process went mute: **zero beat ticks, zero tasks, zero retry logs, zero exceptions** from 00:13 to 06:17. The replica reported `Running` with 0 restarts throughout — the exact "looks alive, does nothing" signature of the July-13 outage class. Scheduled runs, orchestration polling, gap recovery, and the purge were all stopped.

## What I did

1. **Diagnosed** via the telemetry frontier (worker traces frozen at 00:13:05) cross-checked against the container console (43 boot lines, nothing after) and Redis health (up 6 days, 0 restarts — ruled out as cause).
2. **Restarted the worker revision** at 06:17 — beat started immediately, `recover_orchestration_gaps` swept 3 connections, and steady state is verified: `dispatch_due_schedules` fired the overnight backlog (5 due → 5 dispatched), suite runs executing (the 4 'failed' are the pre-existing Orders/Payments/Customers/Products credential issue, not new; the DATAQ_READER-backed suite succeeded).
3. **Filed [#905](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/905)** (P1) with the full evidence and the systemic fixes this class needs: a probeable beat-liveness signal (third incident with this surface — #852, #854, now this), loud-and-bounded broker reconnects (a crash-looping worker beats a silent one), and the workspace-wide `last_polled_at`-staleness alert.
4. One honest loss recorded in the issue: the harness smoke's 00:19Z ADF run fell outside both the 15-min poll lookback and the 1-hour gap-recovery window during the outage, so it's permanently stranded (the documented #828 gap) — the pipeline itself succeeded and that path was already live-verified, so I didn't re-wake the harness for it.

## Also in flight

While diagnosing, I found why PR #903 (the column-lineage build) failed CI: the new UC column fixture was silently swallowed by the captures directory's ignore-everything `.gitignore` (never whitelisted), plus one Prettier miss. Both fixed — fixture content-reviewed (200 rows, names/timestamps only), whitelisted with a review note, full local gate green (backend 228 tests, frontend lint/tsc/587 tests) — fixup pushed, CI re-running now. Once it's green and merged, the ledger/docs PR wraps everything up.

Still waiting on you (both blocked by permissions, listed in the todo): the `WAREHOUSE_LINEAGE_ENABLED=true` flip on the prod worker, and the Snowflake reader-role grant (`GRANT IMPORTED PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE SNOWFLAKE TO ROLE DATAQ_READER` — must run in Snowsight since the admin user is MFA-walled).

attachment.snippet:
1	# DataQ — deployment guide
2	
3	How DataQ v1 is deployed to Azure. Infrastructure is **in-repo Terraform**
4	(`deploy/terraform/azure/`, applied — [ADR 0024](../docs/adr/0024-app-deployment-infrastructure.md));
5	the app rolls out via the **`Deploy`** workflow
6	([.github/workflows/deploy.yml](../.github/workflows/deploy.yml), `workflow_dispatch`).
7	The stack is **live** — this is the runbook to provision a fresh environment and to
8	deploy a new image. Related: [ADR 0025](../docs/adr/0025-production-image-pip-slim.md)
9	(slim+pip image), [ADR 0023](../docs/adr/0023-container-image-registry-ghcr.md) (GHCR).
10	
11	Azure is **one** deploy target behind the app's seams (ADR 0010/0013) — the
12	manifests here are infra config, not business logic. No Azure resource names are
13	hardcoded in app code; they live only as Terraform vars + workflow `vars`/`secrets`.
14	
15	## Before you deploy: production prerequisites
16	
17	Read this before a production rollout. It's the "what must change, what access you
18	need, and what your cloud must provide" checklist; the [provisioning runbook](#one-time-provisioning)
19	below is the how.
20	
21	### 1. What you must change (never ship the eval/dev defaults)
22	
23	The prebuilt-image quickstart ([docs/getting-started](../docs/getting-started.md)) is a
24	**dev-bypass eval stack** — it disables auth, uses a passwordless DB, and binds to
25	loopback. A production deployment must flip all of the following. Values live in
26	[`deploy/.env.app.prod.example`](.env.app.prod.example) (app settings) +
27	[`deploy/terraform/azure/variables.tf`](terraform/azure/variables.tf) (infra):
28	
29	| Setting | Eval default | Production |
30	|---|---|---|
31	| `AUTH_DEV_BYPASS` | `true` | **`false`** — this is the master auth switch; leaving it on means **no authentication at all**. |
32	| `AZURE_TENANT_ID` / `AZURE_API_CLIENT_ID` / `AZURE_SPA_CLIENT_ID` | empty | your Azure AD tenant + the two app registrations (API + SPA). |
33	| **Frontend auth config** | `DATAQ_AUTH_MODE=bypass` (eval) | the **same generic image**, reconfigured at **runtime** — `DATAQ_AUTH_MODE=oidc` + `DATAQ_AUTH_AUTHORITY` / `DATAQ_AUTH_CLIENT_ID` / `DATAQ_AUTH_API_SCOPE` (ADR 0028). **No rebuild** — nginx injects `/config.js` from env. See [frontend/Dockerfile](../frontend/Dockerfile). |
34	| `SECRET_STORE` | `redis` (eval) | **`azure_key_vault`** + `AZURE_KEY_VAULT_URL` + the managed identity's `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` (#408). |
35	| `DATABASE_URL` / `REDIS_URL` | inline, passwordless | Key Vault-backed Container Apps secrets — **never literals**; real credentials. |
36	| `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS` | n/a (same-origin) | empty — the frontend Container App proxies `/api` same-origin (ADR 0028); set the SPA origin only if you split them. |
37	| `PUBLIC_BASE_URL` | n/a | the public origin — used to assemble inbound webhook URLs **and** the "View run" deep links in Slack/email alerts (#416); unset → alerts omit the link. |
38	| `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` | seeded dev user | a **minimal** real allowlist — admins can read every suite's failing-row samples (see [Operational notes](#operational-notes)). |
39	| `RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED` | `true` | keep **`true`** — the fixed-window throttle on every public surface (REST + webhooks + `/mcp`), ADR 0035. Fail-open (a Redis outage disables it, logged); set `false` only to fully disable. |
40	| `RATE_LIMIT_AUTHENTICATED_PER_MINUTE` / `RATE_LIMIT_UNAUTHENTICATED_PER_MINUTE` / `RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_PER_MINUTE` | `300` / `120` / `120` | per-minute caps, keyed per `sha256(bearer)` (authenticated) or client-IP (rest). The webhook cap is **per provider + IP** (#785) — each of adf/airflow/dbt has its own bucket at this cap; the per-IP webhook *total* is `RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_IP_PER_MINUTE`. Defaults are generous; **tighten `RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_PER_MINUTE`** to your orchestrator's real callback cadence. |
41	| `RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_IP_PER_MINUTE` | `240` | per-IP ceiling across **all** webhook buckets from one IP (#785) — bounds the aggregate a single IP can spend by rotating provider segments; without it, per-provider buckets would multiply the per-IP webhook budget. |
42	| `RATE_LIMIT_IP_PER_MINUTE` | `1200` | per-IP ceiling across **all** bearer buckets from one IP — the rotated-token backstop (a client cycling a fresh random `Bearer` per request can't mint unlimited fresh per-token buckets to dodge the cap). Applies only to the `default` (bearer) class; the unauth class is already per-IP, the webhook class has its own ceiling above. |
43	| `RATE_LIMIT_IPV4_PREFIX` / `RATE_LIMIT_IPV6_PREFIX` | `24` / `64` | per-IP buckets key on this address **prefix**, not the full address (#789) — a rotating NAT/proxy pool inside one allocation shares a bucket instead of diluting the cap across sibling /32s. `/32` / `/128` disable grouping; widen or narrow per deployment (a CGNAT-heavy user base may warrant `/32`). |
44	| `RATE_LIMIT_XFF_TRUSTED_HOPS` | `1` | number of trusted proxies that append `X-Forwarded-For` in your deployment — the real client is the entry that many hops from the right. **`1`** for a single-proxy / compose setup (rightmost); **`3`** for the ACA public-envoy→nginx→internal-envoy chain (set in Terraform). A chain shorter than this falls back to the socket peer. |
45	| `COMPARISON_MAX_ROWS` | `100000` | default per-side row cap for `comparison` checks (ADR 0015) — both sides materialize in worker memory for the diff, so this is a memory guardrail; over-cap runs **fail fast** (never a silently truncated diff). A check's `config.max_rows` overrides it. |
46	| `APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING` | unset | Azure Monitor / App Insights backend for spans + logs (observability, OTel — ADR 0010). |
47	| `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` | unset | generic OTLP/HTTP backend for spans + logs (#589) — any OTLP consumer (Tempo/Jaeger/Datadog/Collector); set alongside App Insights for parity, or alone for a non-Azure deploy. |
48	| `OPENLINEAGE_URL` | unset | OpenLineage emission (ADR 0034, #758) — **dark by default**: unset ⇒ zero emission. Point at an OL receiver (Marquez, DataHub's OL endpoint) and every suite run emits START + terminal RunEvents with DQ facets (5s emit timeout, fail-open, no sample rows ever). Advanced transports via the library-owned `OPENLINEAGE__TRANSPORT__*` / `OPENLINEAGE_CONFIG`; `OPENLINEAGE_DISABLED=true` forces dark. |
49	| `key_vault_purge_protection` (Terraform) | `false` (bring-up) | **`true`** for a hardened vault (irreversible). |
50	| Interactive API docs | served | **404 in prod** via the prod-docs gate (`ENVIRONMENT=prod`). |
51	
52	### 2. Access you need
53	
54	- **Azure subscription** — rights to create the resource group, Container Apps
55	  environment, PostgreSQL Flexible Server, Cache for Redis, Key Vault, and Application
56	  Insights + Log Analytics (Contributor on the RG/subscription); the frontend is a
57	  Container App too (no Static Web App since ADR 0028). **Plus** `User Access
58	  Administrator`/`Owner` to grant the managed identity a **custom get+list+set Key Vault
59	  role** (an RBAC role assignment — read+write so the app can persist/rotate connection
60	  credentials at runtime, but not the broader built-in Secrets Officer; #622).
61	- **Azure AD (Entra ID)** — `Application Administrator` (or Global Admin) to create the
62	  **two app registrations** (API + SPA) and **grant admin consent** for the API scope.
63	- **Subscription resource-provider registration** — the app's Terraform registers
64	  `Microsoft.App`, `Microsoft.Cache`, `Microsoft.KeyVault`, `Microsoft.Web` (see
65	  [rp.tf](terraform/azure/rp.tf)); the PostgreSQL + monitoring providers
66	  (`Microsoft.DBforPostgreSQL`, `Microsoft.Insights`, `Microsoft.OperationalInsights`)
67	  come registered with the shared harness resources (ADR 0024). Registration needs
68	  subscription-level rights.
69	- **GitHub repo admin** — to set the Actions [secrets/vars](#github-config-the-workflow-reads)
70	  and create the OIDC **federated credential** (subject = the repo's `production`
71	  environment). The GHCR image push uses the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` (`packages: write`);

... [301 lines truncated] ...

(**2026-07-17 (latest) — ADR [0036](adr/0036-connection-anchored-check-engines.md) merged ([#894](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/894)):**
input.new_string: | **Open issues** | **39** in the **`v1.1 Backlog`** milestone (80 open repo-wide) — `gh issue list --milestone 'v1.1 Backlog' --state open`; the population is stated here because the figure has silently drifted before, so **re-count it, don't decrement it**. (**2026-07-18 (latest) — periodic QA/security sweep → prod deploy `4f8b2ae0` → a 6h silent worker outage (#905) → column-level lineage BUILT + UC live-verified (#901/#902 via [#903](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/903)).** **Sweep:** rule-4a mutation spikes on the newest code, findings filed as [#898](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/898) — `warehouse_refresh.py` **236/239 (98.7%)** once the DB-backed tests were un-skipped (mutmut's copied tree lacks `.env` → every DB-covered module silently reads "no tests"; export `TEST_DATABASE_URL` — the spike's first pass showed exactly that artifact), SF/UC providers ~65% on covered mutants (survivors: static SQL text against stub connections — accepted — plus real gaps in ladder result-shape/edge-filter assertions), frontend Stryker **81.8%** over the four assets modules (`lineageLayout.ts` 66% worst). Security scan clean across Bandit / pip-audit / pnpm-audit / **full-history** betterleaks; the one historical finding (the #849 test's original jwt.io-demo fixture, unpurgeable — force-push blocked) is fingerprint-allowlisted via `.betterleaksignore` ([#899](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/899)) so the periodic full-history scan reads zero and a real leak can't hide behind known noise; targeted #849/#852-class re-verification green (logger-level scrub incl. exported OTel attributes + rendered tracebacks, `azure.core` filter + no-propagate, span URLs path-only, classified-never-raw persistence). **Deploy** (first since 07-13; ships schema_drift #881/#883, monitor registry #880, `monitor_baselines` + warehouse-lineage migrations, warehouse-native lineage #886–#891 dark-by-default, rate-limit prefix buckets #874, 12 dep bumps): pre-checklist green (CI green on the SHA, both migrations up+down tested locally), migrate `Succeeded`, all three apps on the SHA tag, probes correct, one harness `window` (4 min): 5 mockdata jobs green + the `tr_orders_landed` event trigger ran `pl_flow_a_orders` **Succeeded on live Snowflake**, harness auto-stopped. **THEN THE WORKER WEDGED SILENTLY ([#905](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/905), P1):** a transient broker blip 50s after the roll hung the reconnect — beat never ticked ONCE for 6 hours (no retries, no exceptions, replica `Running`, telemetry frozen at 00:13Z) — the third "alive but doing nothing" incident (#852, #854 kin), detected by the telemetry frontier + console cross-check and fixed by a revision restart (beat up 06:17Z, gap recovery swept immediately, backlog dispatched 5/5). The smoke's 00:19Z ADF run was stranded outside both the 15-min poll lookback and the 1-h gap-recovery window — the #828-documented gap, recorded not re-produced. #905 carries the class fix: probeable beat liveness + loud/bounded reconnects + the workspace-wide `last_polled_at`-staleness alert. **Column lineage (#901 + the #902 ladder fix, user-directed, [#903](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/903)):** UC `system.access.column_lineage` pulled on the same watermark/window as table lineage and persisted as a `columns` JSONB **on the edge** (additive migration `a1b2c3d4e5f8`; union-merge across incremental refreshes — never forgets, never regresses to NULL; grain failure degrades with a classified note, never fails table edges), surfaced on AssetDetail with the **#845 one-rule redaction extended to columns** — an edge touching a redacted node collapses to a count-only redacted box server-side (column names are schema disclosure; pinned at the wire). The Snowflake tier ladder now **descends on authorization errors** instead of aborting (#902 — found LIVE: the GET_LINEAGE probe 002003s for a role holding only lower-tier grants like `SNOWFLAKE.GOVERNANCE_VIEWER`; skip reasons + degrade note now carry the real grant-vs-edition cause). **Live evidence:** UC verified end-to-end against the real workspace with prod-parity creds (6 table edges tier `unity_catalog_system_access` + 200 column rows captured → the committed projected fixture the tests ride); Snowflake live-blocked at the grant wall — the PAT authenticates (role-locked `DATAQ_READER`), every ACCOUNT_USAGE tier denied, admin path MFA-walled → the enable steps (Snowsight `GRANT IMPORTED PRIVILEGES` or finer `GOVERNANCE_VIEWER`, + the `WAREHOUSE_LINEAGE_ENABLED` flip) are the operator's, documented in orchestration.md. Azure note: the subscription is **PayAsYouGo since ~07-13** (no spending limit — awake resources bill real money); runbook probe nit fixed (SPA catch-all serves 200 on `$FE/openapi.json`; the docs gate is `/api/v1/openapi.json → 404`).) (**2026-07-18 (latest) — periodic QA/security sweep → prod deploy `4f8b2ae0` → a 6h silent worker outage (#905) → column-level lineage BUILT + UC live-verified (#901/#902 via [#903](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/903)).** **Sweep:** rule-4a mutation spikes on the newest code, findings filed as [#898](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/898) — `warehouse_refresh.py` **236/239 (98.7%)** once the DB-backed tests were un-skipped (mutmut's copied tree lacks `.env` → every DB-covered module silently reads "no tests"; export `TEST_DATABASE_URL` — the spike's first pass showed exactly that artifact), SF/UC providers ~65% on covered mutants (survivors: static SQL text against stub connections — accepted — plus real gaps in ladder result-shape/edge-filter assertions), frontend Stryker **81.8%** over the four assets modules (`lineageLayout.ts` 66% worst). Security scan clean across Bandit / pip-audit / pnpm-audit / **full-history** betterleaks; the one historical finding (the #849 test's original jwt.io-demo fixture, unpurgeable — force-push blocked) is fingerprint-allowlisted via `.betterleaksignore` ([#899](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/899)) so the periodic full-history scan reads zero and a real leak can't hide behind known noise; targeted #849/#852-class re-verification green (logger-level scrub incl. exported OTel attributes + rendered tracebacks, `azure.core` filter + no-propagate, span URLs path-only, classified-never-raw persistence). **Deploy** (first since 07-13; ships schema_drift #881/#883, monitor registry #880, `monitor_baselines` + warehouse-lineage migrations, warehouse-native lineage #886–#891 dark-by-default, rate-limit prefix buckets #874, 12 dep bumps): pre-checklist green (CI green on the SHA, both migrations up+down tested locally), migrate `Succeeded`, all three apps on the SHA tag, probes correct, one harness `window` (4 min): 5 mockdata jobs green + the `tr_orders_landed` event trigger ran `pl_flow_a_orders` **Succeeded on live Snowflake**, harness auto-stopped. **THEN THE WORKER WEDGED SILENTLY ([#905](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/905), P1):** a transient broker blip 50s after the roll hung the reconnect — beat never ticked ONCE for 6 hours (no retries, no exceptions, replica `Running`, telemetry frozen at 00:13Z) — the third "alive but doing nothing" incident (#852, #854 kin), detected by the telemetry frontier + console cross-check and fixed by a revision restart (beat up 06:17Z, gap recovery swept immediately, backlog dispatched 5/5). The smoke's 00:19Z ADF run was stranded outside both the 15-min poll lookback and the 1-h gap-recovery window — the #828-documented gap, recorded not re-produced. #905 carries the class fix: probeable beat liveness + loud/bounded reconnects + the workspace-wide `last_polled_at`-staleness alert. **Column lineage (#901 + the #902 ladder fix, user-directed, [#903](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/903)):** UC `system.access.column_lineage` pulled on the same watermark/window as table lineage and persisted as a `columns` JSONB **on the edge** (additive migration `a1b2c3d4e5f8`; union-merge across incremental refreshes — never forgets, never regresses to NULL; grain failure degrades with a classified note, never fails table edges), surfaced on AssetDetail with the **#845 one-rule redaction extended to columns** — an edge touching a redacted node collapses to a count-only redacted box server-side (column names are schema disclosure; pinned at the wire). The Snowflake tier ladder now **descends on authorization errors** instead of aborting (#902 — found LIVE: the GET_LINEAGE probe 002003s for a role holding only lower-tier grants like `SNOWFLAKE.GOVERNANCE_VIEWER`; skip reasons + degrade note now carry the real grant-vs-edition cause). **Live evidence:** UC verified end-to-end against the real workspace with prod-parity creds (6 table edges tier `unity_catalog_system_access` + 200 column rows captured → the committed projected fixture the tests ride); Snowflake live-blocked at the grant wall — the PAT authenticates (role-locked `DATAQ_READER`), every ACCOUNT_USAGE tier denied, admin path MFA-walled → the enable steps (Snowsight `GRANT IMPORTED PRIVILEGES` or finer `GOVERNANCE_VIEWER`, + the `WAREHOUSE_LINEAGE_ENABLED` flip) are the operator's, documented in orchestration.md. Azure note: the subscription is **PayAsYouGo since ~07-13** (no spending limit — awake resources bill real money); runbook probe nit fixed (SPA catch-all serves 200 on `$FE/openapi.json`; the docs gate is `/api/v1/openapi.json → 404`).) (**2026-07-17 (latest) — ADR [0036](adr/0036-connection-anchored-check-engines.md) merged ([#894](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/894)):** connection-anchored check engines — every datasource connection offers GX, and a connection additionally unlocks its native engine(s) per its capability set (Snowflake → **DMF**, Unity Catalog → **DQX**, a future BigQuery → **Dataplex**); engine selection is **per check** (`check.engine`, default `gx`, validated against the connection's capabilities; `kind` stays orthogonal to `engine`), superseding the earlier suite-level `engine` toggle. Design-only (index + CLAUDE.md §5 link); DMF is the first native build, DQX/Dataplex trigger-gated. **#858 warehouse-native lineage BUILT end-to-end, all four slices merged; [#858](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/858) auto-closed:** the spike (below) hardened into a shipped feature behind a new **`WarehouseLineageProvider`** seam distinct from the catalog `LineageProvider` — the warehouse returns identifiers in the engine's own case, byte-identical to `asset_identity`, so there is **no fold/enumerate** step and the #823 identity-drift class cannot arise here. **[#886](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/886)** Snowflake provider (`is_incremental=False` → snapshot-diff + **prune**, since `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` is a current-state view) with the spike's source ladder GET_LINEAGE → ACCESS_HISTORY → OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES: GET_LINEAGE gated by a `0A000` **preflight** (clean-error descent, not catch; per-seed traversal deferred → **[#892](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/892)**), ACCESS_HISTORY **empty-corroborated** against QUERY_HISTORY (silently empty on Standard — a catch would mislabel it), OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES the live-verified floor; review fix — `skipped_tiers` carries the real exception message, not a hardcoded "unsupported on this edition" that was wrong for Enterprise. **[#887](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/887)** Unity Catalog provider (`is_incremental=True` → incremental `event_time` watermark, **never prune**, since `system.access.table_lineage` is an append-only log) with a **6h safety re-scan window** so ingestion-lag rows landing after the watermark aren't lost (idempotent upsert makes the re-read harmless); the watermark never regresses below `since`. **[#890](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/890)** the daily beat task (`refresh_warehouse_lineage`, **dark by default** — `warehouse_lineage_enabled=False`) + a backward-compatible migration (`960d18679639`, 5 nullable additive columns) persisting `lineage_watermark`/`last_refresh_at`/`last_tier`/`degraded_reason`/`last_error` per connection; the recorded reason is **classified, never raw** (same discipline as #828's poll-health). **[#891](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/891)** surfaces tier/degraded/failing on the asset graph — an **INFO**-level advisory (distinct from the failing-source **WARNING**) reading the stored `degraded_reason` / `last_error`, gated behind the same asset-stake rule as `failing_lineage_sources`. Providers are tested against **real captured payloads** (byte-for-byte identity pins per #823, query-column projections committed / full captures gitignored). Two process notes banked to memory: a `mypy (tests)` gate miss (CI runs mypy over `backend/tests` too — #418 — a `str | None` used with `in` without an `is not None` narrow cost one round-trip on #891); and the live-warehouse reads stayed spike-parked (Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25), so **[#893](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/893)** — verify e2e against a deployed warehouse — is filed. **Dependabot batch cleared + the #858 spike ran (user-approved):** all 12 version bumps (#860–#871) merged — the stale branches predated the #878 audit fix, and #861 (mypy 2.1→2.3) needed a manual companion commit aligning the pre-commit hook rev that Dependabot can't touch (the `typecheck-deps-sync` gate would have failed it); mypy 2.3.0 verified clean repo-wide first. CodeQL alert 49 fixed ([#884](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/884) — the deadline-thread helper now records-and-RE-RAISES BaseException, keeping the no-masquerade property compliantly). **#858 spike findings** (issue comments have the full detail): real payloads captured from BOTH warehouses (redacted, parked untracked in `backend/tests/fixtures/lineage_native/` for the build PR) — UC `system.access.table_lineage`/`column_lineage` **work on Free Edition** with `event_time` as the watermark and reads-as-NULL-target rows to filter; Snowflake `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` captured with UPPER identity byte-identical to `asset_identity`; **`ACCESS_HISTORY` is silently EMPTY on Standard** (45k QUERY_HISTORY rows, 0 ACCESS_HISTORY — the preflight must corroborate, not catch); **`GET_LINEAGE` errors cleanly (0A000) on Standard** → the build's source ladder is GET_LINEAGE → ACCESS_HISTORY → OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES, preferring the clean-error preflight. Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25; captures are banked so the build no longer depends on the window.) (**2026-07-17 — `schema_drift` SHIPPED end-to-end ([#881](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/881) engine + [#883](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/883) UI):** the first stateful monitor kind — one registry entry (#880's seam held: zero chain edits), a session-aware baseline-diff executor (the #794 comparison pattern; runners never see stateful kinds), per-datasource introspection (SQL via one bound-param `information_schema` query, flat-file via Parquet footer / bounded CSV sample, **Iceberg from table metadata — closing #859's drift leg**), `metric_value` = drifted-column count under ADR-0016 bands, dry-run preview that never writes, and the edit-gated re-baseline action (drop-the-row, recapture-next-run — never introspection on the API thread). The review caught two shippers: concurrent first runs both inserting the baseline — the loser's IntegrityError failed the WHOLE run and discarded sibling results (now `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`), and `UPPER()` information_schema matching MERGING two case-variant tables into a union schema no real table has (now exact-spelling-wins + classified 'ambiguous' refusal). Honest-limits documented: CSV dtype-inference noise (ignore_columns/re-baseline is the workaround) and whole-blob Parquet reads (range-read filed as [#882](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/882)). Two CI-only failures taught local-gate lessons now in memory: `pnpm lint` ≠ `tsc`, and version-dependent library inference must never anchor a fixture.) (**2026-07-16 — the W4 schema-drift prep chain shipped in one session:** #427 one-engine-per-runner ([#875](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/875) — review added `LazyEngine`, `pool_pre_ping` for the mixed-suite stale-connection regression, and the `owned_runner` lifecycle) · #592 slice 1, the `monitor_baselines` diff-reference store ([#876](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/876) — migration-safety review caught a missing `gen_random_uuid()` id default that only a real INSERT could surface) · #726+#429 monitor-kind strategy registry + runner-advertised capability gate ([#880](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/880)) · the #859 Iceberg metadata fast-paths ([#879](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/879) — review caught that snapshot `total-records` never nets row-level deletes: a merge-on-read table would have false-PASSED a volume floor; both metadata paths now require PROOF of zero deletes). **CI-wide outage fixed same session (#877/[#878](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/878)):** npm retired the legacy audit endpoints 2026-07-15 (410) and no released pnpm speaks the bulk endpoint (verified vs 11.13.1) — the required audit gate now queries the bulk advisory endpoint directly over the same lockfile graph, proven to FAIL on known-vulnerable versions and fail-closed on parse/endpoint drift; #877 stays open for the revert-to-pnpm-audit condition. The #858 warehouse-native lineage spike is consent-blocked on live prod reads.) (**2026-07-14 — #789 prefix-keyed per-IP buckets shipped ([#874](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/874)):** every per-IP bucket (unauth, per-provider webhook, both `ipall` ceilings) now keys on an address prefix — IPv4 /24, IPv6 /64, configurable — closing the NAT/proxy-pool dilution observed in the #725 post-deploy smoke (200 requests over 11 sibling /32s, none near the cap). The agentic review caught a shipping bug: IPv4-mapped IPv6 (`::ffff:a.b.c.d`, the dual-stack socket-peer form) folded to `::/64` — the ENTIRE IPv4 internet in one bucket, so one abuser would have 429'd every IPv4 client; now unwrapped via `ipv4_mapped` and folded as IPv4. Also fixed off review: a false ADR claim about CGNAT being unaffected (pool-based CGNAT now shares one bucket — masks are tunable, /32 disables), and test pins so a partial regression (ceilings reverting to raw /32) can't ship green.) (**2026-07-14 — #785 per-provider webhook rate-limit buckets shipped ([#872](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/872)):** the webhook class is now dual-key — a per provider+IP bucket (adf/airflow/dbt folded into the key, unknown segments share one bare-IP bucket so a scanner can't mint buckets) **plus** a `rl:webhook:ipall:{ip}` aggregate ceiling (`RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_IP_PER_MINUTE`, default 240) the agentic review added — without it, per-provider folding would have quietly multiplied the per-IP webhook budget by 4. The review also pinned the fail-open log's `key_kind` to the documented `{tok, ip}` domain (the post-#852 observability-drift lesson) and replaced a hand-copied provider frozenset with the public `db.models.ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS` — the claimed layering obstacle was false. ADR 0035 + deploy/README synced, incl. the new Redis SCAN pattern `rl:webhook:*:ip:*`.) (**2026-07-13 — prod lineage restored + the #829/#830 pair merged.** The lineage outage was ours: the dbt connection's ADLS SAS **expired 6 Jul**, so every 10-min poll failed `ClientAuthenticationError` and DataQ never read `manifest.json` — the dbt builds stayed green and kept publishing artifacts nobody consumed. Marquez was a red herring; the live path is the #759 dbt-manifest one. Re-minted the SAS (1y), re-ran the build, and the chain fired end to end (poll → `refresh_dbt_lineage` → `lineage_edges` → graph; the trigger-binding also ran the bound suite 4/4 green). **Two systemic defects fall out, still open on [#828](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/828):** a failing poll has **no user-visible signal** and the UI's empty-lineage state is indistinguishable from "this asset has no upstreams"; and `_POLL_LOOKBACK` is 15 min, so once auth was fixed the poll succeeded and still recorded **nothing** — every build produced during the outage had aged out and is stranded in the artifact store, recoverable only by re-running the producer. [#823](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/823) narrowed to the still-unproven Marquez pull path. Shipped alongside: [#829](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/829) — a suite was **unshareable from a phone** (the Add button painted at x=407 on a 390px viewport) — and [#830](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/830) — the assets UI printed the raw OL namespace (an Iceberg DSN) instead of a human label. **Both agentic reviews earned their keep:** on #829 the review caught that the new e2e spec was **worthless** (`toBeInViewport()` defaults to `ratio: 0`, and 71% of the broken button was on screen — it passed on the bug); on #830 it caught a **missed fifth render site** (the dashboard) and that the label still **leaked the DB username** when a catalog URI had no path, which made the commit message's own claim false.) (**user follow-up batch #801–#805 all closed 2026-07-12** — the five UI/ops follow-ups filed off the ADR-0015 session: mobile overlay nav ([#817](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/817)), hierarchical asset browse ([#818](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/818)), asset-health split into connection-vs-data-quality axes ([#819](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/819)), the left-to-right lineage graph ([#820](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/820)), and the Marquez local verification + runbook correction ([#821](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/821)). Four latent bugs surfaced and were fixed en route: a run that evaluated nothing read as a green "Passing"; a `cancelled` run rolled up green on **both** health axes; an asset→asset navigation showed the *previous* asset's data (nothing could reach it before the graph's clickable nodes); and the demo seed had **no lineage at all**, so a fresh install could not show the feature. **#813 closed via [#814](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/814)** — weekly `/security-scan` OWASP spot-check found the MCP `create_check`/`update_check` tools had no Pydantic layer, so `name`/`expectation_type` could exceed the REST 256/128 bounds and hit a raw Postgres `StringDataRightTruncation` instead of a clean 422; pushed `check_service.validate_lengths` (now public) into both, extended to `suite_io_service.import_suite`'s direct `Check(...)` construction too (agentic review caught that same-class gap pre-merge). Same session: 13 open CodeQL code-scanning alerts triaged — 10 dismissed as false-positive/by-design (module-level cache globals, a documented lazy-import cycle, an intentional test canary), 3 genuine (`side-effect-in-assert` ×2, `unnecessary-lambda`) fixed in [#815](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/815). **#799 comparison:columns + tolerance + optimization shipped (this PR)** — FDC's column grain as `comparison:columns` (per-column matched/mismatched/additional-per-side VALUE buckets, dropna parity, same sample shape → redaction/report reuse), numeric `config.tolerance` {absolute, relative} on both grains (the #808-review float32 finding — one-sided NULLs never tolerance-equal), and the shared keys+positions alignment pass (×1.52 on the 1M-row benchmark, bucket parity asserted; integer keys now render "1" not "1.0" in samples); catalog fixture regenerated, per-column breakdown + tolerance fields in the UI. · **ADR [0015](adr/0015-two-connection-comparison-check-model.md) written + merged ([#790](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/790), 2026-07-12)** — the two-connection comparison check model: the suite stays single-connection and supplies the **target under test**; a `comparison` check adds one **source** ref. Closes the design gate reserved since ADR 0014; build set [#791](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/791)–[#795](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/795) filed on W3. **#795 comparison UI + report download shipped (this PR) — the ADR 0015 build is COMPLETE (#791–#795, five PRs in one session 2026-07-12)**: side-by-side check editor (source pane = connection picker + table-or-SQL per type + keys; target pane locked to the suite's connection — the §1 invariant made visible; common options below), RunDetail bucket counts + redacted sample tables + on-demand CSV/XLSX report download (derived server-side from the redacted buckets via new `comparison_report.py` + `openpyxl` (MIT, pinned), never stored), comparison-safe CheckEdit (name+thresholds only), catalog entry + regenerated GX-contract fixture, feature-matrix row; Playwright comparison spec green against the live local stack (which also re-validated #797 — the stale api container 429'd the suite until recreated). **#794 comparison run path shipped ([#809](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/809))** — `services/comparison_run.py` builds a per-run executor bound to the suite's resolved target (source side = the check's ref + `config.source` through `resolve_target`/batch materialization or a query spec; target side = the run's materialized table or `config.target_query`), reads both via the #792 reader, diffs with the #793 engine, and maps buckets → `CheckOutcome` (mismatch-% → `metric_value` under ADR 0016 banding; counts → `observed_value`; capped samples → `sample_failures`). `_run_outcomes` routes comparison checks to the injected executor (None → per-check `error`, callers stay safe); worker builds it only when the suite has comparison checks. Failure semantics #122: reader/engine refusals + unexpected exceptions → operational `error` results (classified messages, never raw text). The sample redactor gains a comparison branch — suffix-stripped policy matching so `pii_columns: [email]` masks `email_src`+`email_tgt`, unknown columns keep default-mask. **#793 comparison engine shipped ([#808](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/808))** — `datasources/comparison.py`, the FDC record-grain diff port (ADR 0014 §2: engine yes, app no): key-joined outer-merge buckets (matched / mismatched / additional-in-source / additional-in-target) with per-column mismatch counts, typed `DuplicateKeyError` (FDC silently drop_duplicates-ed — pairing must never be ambiguous) + `ComparisonInputError`, NA-safe nullable-`string` comparison (null==null matches; a literal "nan" ≠ NULL — fixes the FDC astype(str) quirk), pairwise numeric canonicalization (numpy NULL-int→float64 "10.0" vs Arrow "10" can't fabricate mismatches; dup-key detection on normalized keys, samples echo original values), mismatch-% badness scalar over the logical-row union, capped JSON-clean sample buckets. **#792 DatasetReader seam shipped ([#807](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/807))** — `services/dataset_reader.py`: per-datasource capped DataFrame reads behind profiler-style dict dispatch (SQL via the profiler engine plumbing, flat-file object read, `pyiceberg` scan; orchestration types 422), COUNT/scan-metadata preflight + post-read race guard + typed `DatasetTooLargeError` (fail-fast, never truncation), read-time read-only re-validation of query specs, `COMPARISON_MAX_ROWS` setting (default 100k, per-check `config.max_rows` override) documented in deploy/README. **#791 schema + authoring slice shipped ([#798](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/798))** — `checks.source_connection_id` FK + kind⇔presence CHECK (migration `b7c8d9e0f1a2`, up+down tested), author-time validation (source spec via `resolve_target`, read-only per-side SQL per ADR 0019, join keys, orchestration sources rejected), RESTRICT delete guard (409 `connection_in_use`), export/import `(name, env)` round-trip, version snapshots, and the run-path guard (a comparison check yields a per-check `error` result until the #794 runner — never fails its siblings' run). User follow-up batch filed to `v1.1 Backlog` (2026-07-12): [#799](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/799) comparison:columns + optimization pass · [#800](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/800) Astronomer verification · [#801](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/801) mobile overlay nav · [#802](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/802) hierarchical asset browse · [#803](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/803) asset health split · [#804](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/804) Marquez in harness · [#805](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/805) lineage graph view · [#806](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/806) stale-nosec chore. | **Open issues** | **39** in the **`v1.1 Backlog`** milestone (80 open repo-wide) — `gh issue list --milestone 'v1.1 Backlog' --state open`; the population is stated here because the figure has silently drifted before, so **re-count it, don't decrement it**. (**2026-07-18 (latest) — periodic QA/security sweep → prod deploy `4f8b2ae0` → a 6h silent worker outage (#905) → column-level lineage BUILT + UC live-verified (#901/#902 via [#903](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/903)).** **Sweep:** rule-4a mutation spikes on the newest code, findings filed as [#898](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/898) — `warehouse_refresh.py` **236/239 (98.7%)** once the DB-backed tests were un-skipped (mutmut's copied tree lacks `.env` → every DB-covered module silently reads "no tests"; export `TEST_DATABASE_URL` — the spike's first pass showed exactly that artifact), SF/UC providers ~65% on covered mutants (survivors: static SQL text against stub connections — accepted — plus real gaps in ladder result-shape/edge-filter assertions), frontend Stryker **81.8%** over the four assets modules (`lineageLayout.ts` 66% worst). Security scan clean across Bandit / pip-audit / pnpm-audit / **full-history** betterleaks; the one historical finding (the #849 test's original jwt.io-demo fixture, unpurgeable — force-push blocked) is fingerprint-allowlisted via `.betterleaksignore` ([#899](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/899)) so the periodic full-history scan reads zero and a real leak can't hide behind known noise; targeted #849/#852-class re-verification green (logger-level scrub incl. exported OTel attributes + rendered tracebacks, `azure.core` filter + no-propagate, span URLs path-only, classified-never-raw persistence). **Deploy** (first since 07-13; ships schema_drift #881/#883, monitor registry #880, `monitor_baselines` + warehouse-lineage migrations, warehouse-native lineage #886–#891 dark-by-default, rate-limit prefix buckets #874, 12 dep bumps): pre-checklist green (CI green on the SHA, both migrations up+down tested locally), migrate `Succeeded`, all three apps on the SHA tag, probes correct, one harness `window` (4 min): 5 mockdata jobs green + the `tr_orders_landed` event trigger ran `pl_flow_a_orders` **Succeeded on live Snowflake**, harness auto-stopped. **THEN THE WORKER WEDGED SILENTLY ([#905](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/905), P1):** a transient broker blip 50s after the roll hung the reconnect — beat never ticked ONCE for 6 hours (no retries, no exceptions, replica `Running`, telemetry frozen at 00:13Z) — the third "alive but doing nothing" incident (#852, #854 kin), detected by the telemetry frontier + console cross-check and fixed by a revision restart (beat up 06:17Z, gap recovery swept immediately, backlog dispatched 5/5). The smoke's 00:19Z ADF run was stranded outside both the 15-min poll lookback and the 1-h gap-recovery window — the #828-documented gap, recorded not re-produced. #905 carries the class fix: probeable beat liveness + loud/bounded reconnects + the workspace-wide `last_polled_at`-staleness alert. **Column lineage (#901 + the #902 ladder fix, user-directed, [#903](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/903)):** UC `system.access.column_lineage` pulled on the same watermark/window as table lineage and persisted as a `columns` JSONB **on the edge** (additive migration `a1b2c3d4e5f8`; union-merge across incremental refreshes — never forgets, never regresses to NULL; grain failure degrades with a classified note, never fails table edges), surfaced on AssetDetail with the **#845 one-rule redaction extended to columns** — an edge touching a redacted node collapses to a count-only redacted box server-side (column names are schema disclosure; pinned at the wire). The Snowflake tier ladder now **descends on authorization errors** instead of aborting (#902 — found LIVE: the GET_LINEAGE probe 002003s for a role holding only lower-tier grants like `SNOWFLAKE.GOVERNANCE_VIEWER`; skip reasons + degrade note now carry the real grant-vs-edition cause). **Live evidence:** UC verified end-to-end against the real workspace with prod-parity creds (6 table edges tier `unity_catalog_system_access` + 200 column rows captured → the committed projected fixture the tests ride); Snowflake live-blocked at the grant wall — the PAT authenticates (role-locked `DATAQ_READER`), every ACCOUNT_USAGE tier denied, admin path MFA-walled → the enable steps (Snowsight `GRANT IMPORTED PRIVILEGES` or finer `GOVERNANCE_VIEWER`, + the `WAREHOUSE_LINEAGE_ENABLED` flip) are the operator's, documented in orchestration.md. Azure note: the subscription is **PayAsYouGo since ~07-13** (no spending limit — awake resources bill real money); runbook probe nit fixed (SPA catch-all serves 200 on `$FE/openapi.json`; the docs gate is `/api/v1/openapi.json → 404`).) (**2026-07-17 — ADR [0036](adr/0036-connection-anchored-check-engines.md) merged ([#894](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/894)):** connection-anchored check engines — every datasource connection offers GX, and a connection additionally unlocks its native engine(s) per its capability set (Snowflake → **DMF**, Unity Catalog → **DQX**, a future BigQuery → **Dataplex**); engine selection is **per check** (`check.engine`, default `gx`, validated against the connection's capabilities; `kind` stays orthogonal to `engine`), superseding the earlier suite-level `engine` toggle. Design-only (index + CLAUDE.md §5 link); DMF is the first native build, DQX/Dataplex trigger-gated. **#858 warehouse-native lineage BUILT end-to-end, all four slices merged; [#858](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/858) auto-closed:** the spike (below) hardened into a shipped feature behind a new **`WarehouseLineageProvider`** seam distinct from the catalog `LineageProvider` — the warehouse returns identifiers in the engine's own case, byte-identical to `asset_identity`, so there is **no fold/enumerate** step and the #823 identity-drift class cannot arise here. **[#886](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/886)** Snowflake provider (`is_incremental=False` → snapshot-diff + **prune**, since `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` is a current-state view) with the spike's source ladder GET_LINEAGE → ACCESS_HISTORY → OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES: GET_LINEAGE gated by a `0A000` **preflight** (clean-error descent, not catch; per-seed traversal deferred → **[#892](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/892)**), ACCESS_HISTORY **empty-corroborated** against QUERY_HISTORY (silently empty on Standard — a catch would mislabel it), OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES the live-verified floor; review fix — `skipped_tiers` carries the real exception message, not a hardcoded "unsupported on this edition" that was wrong for Enterprise. **[#887](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/887)** Unity Catalog provider (`is_incremental=True` → incremental `event_time` watermark, **never prune**, since `system.access.table_lineage` is an append-only log) with a **6h safety re-scan window** so ingestion-lag rows landing after the watermark aren't lost (idempotent upsert makes the re-read harmless); the watermark never regresses below `since`. **[#890](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/890)** the daily beat task (`refresh_warehouse_lineage`, **dark by default** — `warehouse_lineage_enabled=False`) + a backward-compatible migration (`960d18679639`, 5 nullable additive columns) persisting `lineage_watermark`/`last_refresh_at`/`last_tier`/`degraded_reason`/`last_error` per connection; the recorded reason is **classified, never raw** (same discipline as #828's poll-health). **[#891](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/891)** surfaces tier/degraded/failing on the asset graph — an **INFO**-level advisory (distinct from the failing-source **WARNING**) reading the stored `degraded_reason` / `last_error`, gated behind the same asset-stake rule as `failing_lineage_sources`. Providers are tested against **real captured payloads** (byte-for-byte identity pins per #823, query-column projections committed / full captures gitignored). Two process notes banked to memory: a `mypy (tests)` gate miss (CI runs mypy over `backend/tests` too — #418 — a `str | None` used with `in` without an `is not None` narrow cost one round-trip on #891); and the live-warehouse reads stayed spike-parked (Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25), so **[#893](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/893)** — verify e2e against a deployed warehouse — is filed. **Dependabot batch cleared + the #858 spike ran (user-approved):** all 12 version bumps (#860–#871) merged — the stale branches predated the #878 audit fix, and #861 (mypy 2.1→2.3) needed a manual companion commit aligning the pre-commit hook rev that Dependabot can't touch (the `typecheck-deps-sync` gate would have failed it); mypy 2.3.0 verified clean repo-wide first. CodeQL alert 49 fixed ([#884](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/884) — the deadline-thread helper now records-and-RE-RAISES BaseException, keeping the no-masquerade property compliantly). **#858 spike findings** (issue comments have the full detail): real payloads captured from BOTH warehouses (redacted, parked untracked in `backend/tests/fixtures/lineage_native/` for the build PR) — UC `system.access.table_lineage`/`column_lineage` **work on Free Edition** with `event_time` as the watermark and reads-as-NULL-target rows to filter; Snowflake `OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES` captured with UPPER identity byte-identical to `asset_identity`; **`ACCESS_HISTORY` is silently EMPTY on Standard** (45k QUERY_HISTORY rows, 0 ACCESS_HISTORY — the preflight must corroborate, not catch); **`GET_LINEAGE` errors cleanly (0A000) on Standard** → the build's source ladder is GET_LINEAGE → ACCESS_HISTORY → OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES, preferring the clean-error preflight. Snowflake trial lapses ~2026-07-25; captures are banked so the build no longer depends on the window.) (**2026-07-17 — `schema_drift` SHIPPED end-to-end ([#881](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/881) engine + [#883](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/883) UI):** the first stateful monitor kind — one registry entry (#880's seam held: zero chain edits), a session-aware baseline-diff executor (the #794 comparison pattern; runners never see stateful kinds), per-datasource introspection (SQL via one bound-param `information_schema` query, flat-file via Parquet footer / bounded CSV sample, **Iceberg from table metadata — closing #859's drift leg**), `metric_value` = drifted-column count under ADR-0016 bands, dry-run preview that never writes, and the edit-gated re-baseline action (drop-the-row, recapture-next-run — never introspection on the API thread). The review caught two shippers: concurrent first runs both inserting the baseline — the loser's IntegrityError failed the WHOLE run and discarded sibling results (now `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`), and `UPPER()` information_schema matching MERGING two case-variant tables into a union schema no real table has (now exact-spelling-wins + classified 'ambiguous' refusal). Honest-limits documented: CSV dtype-inference noise (ignore_columns/re-baseline is the workaround) and whole-blob Parquet reads (range-read filed as [#882](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/882)). Two CI-only failures taught local-gate lessons now in memory: `pnpm lint` ≠ `tsc`, and version-dependent library inference must never anchor a fixture.) (**2026-07-16 — the W4 schema-drift prep chain shipped in one session:** #427 one-engine-per-runner ([#875](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/875) — review added `LazyEngine`, `pool_pre_ping` for the mixed-suite stale-connection regression, and the `owned_runner` lifecycle) · #592 slice 1, the `monitor_baselines` diff-reference store ([#876](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/876) — migration-safety review caught a missing `gen_random_uuid()` id default that only a real INSERT could surface) · #726+#429 monitor-kind strategy registry + runner-advertised capability gate ([#880](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/880)) · the #859 Iceberg metadata fast-paths ([#879](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/879) — review caught that snapshot `total-records` never nets row-level deletes: a merge-on-read table would have false-PASSED a volume floor; both metadata paths now require PROOF of zero deletes). **CI-wide outage fixed same session (#877/[#878](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/878)):** npm retired the legacy audit endpoints 2026-07-15 (410) and no released pnpm speaks the bulk endpoint (verified vs 11.13.1) — the required audit gate now queries the bulk advisory endpoint directly over the same lockfile graph, proven to FAIL on known-vulnerable versions and fail-closed on parse/endpoint drift; #877 stays open for the revert-to-pnpm-audit condition. The #858 warehouse-native lineage spike is consent-blocked on live prod reads.) (**2026-07-14 — #789 prefix-keyed per-IP buckets shipped ([#874](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/874)):** every per-IP bucket (unauth, per-provider webhook, both `ipall` ceilings) now keys on an address prefix — IPv4 /24, IPv6 /64, configurable — closing the NAT/proxy-pool dilution observed in the #725 post-deploy smoke (200 requests over 11 sibling /32s, none near the cap). The agentic review caught a shipping bug: IPv4-mapped IPv6 (`::ffff:a.b.c.d`, the dual-stack socket-peer form) folded to `::/64` — the ENTIRE IPv4 internet in one bucket, so one abuser would have 429'd every IPv4 client; now unwrapped via `ipv4_mapped` and folded as IPv4. Also fixed off review: a false ADR claim about CGNAT being unaffected (pool-based CGNAT now shares one bucket — masks are tunable, /32 disables), and test pins so a partial regression (ceilings reverting to raw /32) can't ship green.) (**2026-07-14 — #785 per-provider webhook rate-limit buckets shipped ([#872](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/872)):** the webhook class is now dual-key — a per provider+IP bucket (adf/airflow/dbt folded into the key, unknown segments share one bare-IP bucket so a scanner can't mint buckets) **plus** a `rl:webhook:ipall:{ip}` aggregate ceiling (`RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_IP_PER_MINUTE`, default 240) the agentic review added — without it, per-provider folding would have quietly multiplied the per-IP webhook budget by 4. The review also pinned the fail-open log's `key_kind` to the documented `{tok, ip}` domain (the post-#852 observability-drift lesson) and replaced a hand-copied provider frozenset with the public `db.models.ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS` — the claimed layering obstacle was false. ADR 0035 + deploy/README synced, incl. the new Redis SCAN pattern `rl:webhook:*:ip:*`.) (**2026-07-13 — prod lineage restored + the #829/#830 pair merged.** The lineage outage was ours: the dbt connection's ADLS SAS **expired 6 Jul**, so every 10-min poll failed `ClientAuthenticationError` and DataQ never read `manifest.json` — the dbt builds stayed green and kept publishing artifacts nobody consumed. Marquez was a red herring; the live path is the #759 dbt-manifest one. Re-minted the SAS (1y), re-ran the build, and the chain fired end to end (poll → `refresh_dbt_lineage` → `lineage_edges` → graph; the trigger-binding also ran the bound suite 4/4 green). **Two systemic defects fall out, still open on [#828](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/828):** a failing poll has **no user-visible signal** and the UI's empty-lineage state is indistinguishable from "this asset has no upstreams"; and `_POLL_LOOKBACK` is 15 min, so once auth was fixed the poll succeeded and still recorded **nothing** — every build produced during the outage had aged out and is stranded in the artifact store, recoverable only by re-running the producer. [#823](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/823) narrowed to the still-unproven Marquez pull path. Shipped alongside: [#829](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/829) — a suite was **unshareable from a phone** (the Add button painted at x=407 on a 390px viewport) — and [#830](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/830) — the assets UI printed the raw OL namespace (an Iceberg DSN) instead of a human label. **Both agentic reviews earned their keep:** on #829 the review caught that the new e2e spec was **worthless** (`toBeInViewport()` defaults to `ratio: 0`, and 71% of the broken button was on screen — it passed on the bug); on #830 it caught a **missed fifth render site** (the dashboard) and that the label still **leaked the DB username** when a catalog URI had no path, which made the commit message's own claim false.) (**user follow-up batch #801–#805 all closed 2026-07-12** — the five UI/ops follow-ups filed off the ADR-0015 session: mobile overlay nav ([#817](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/817)), hierarchical asset browse ([#818](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/818)), asset-health split into connection-vs-data-quality axes ([#819](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/819)), the left-to-right lineage graph ([#820](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/820)), and the Marquez local verification + runbook correction ([#821](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/821)). Four latent bugs surfaced and were fixed en route: a run that evaluated nothing read as a green "Passing"; a `cancelled` run rolled up green on **both** health axes; an asset→asset navigation showed the *previous* asset's data (nothing could reach it before the graph's clickable nodes); and the demo seed had **no lineage at all**, so a fresh install could not show the feature. **#813 closed via [#814](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/814)** — weekly `/security-scan` OWASP spot-check found the MCP `create_check`/`update_check` tools had no Pydantic layer, so `name`/`expectation_type` could exceed the REST 256/128 bounds and hit a raw Postgres `StringDataRightTruncation` instead of a clean 422; pushed `check_service.validate_lengths` (now public) into both, extended to `suite_io_service.import_suite`'s direct `Check(...)` construction too (agentic review caught that same-class gap pre-merge).
